caption.video0:
The video opens with a woman, Dr. Sabine Hazan, speaking. She has long, dark, wavy hair and is wearing glasses and a white lab coat over dark clothing. She is seated in what appears to be an office or study, with bookshelves visible behind her. A red banner at the top of the screen displays the text: ""We followed them for 90 days...Their bifidobacteria dropped to like zero—from like a million to like zero."" A blue banner at the bottom asks: "Are COVID-19 vaccines killing the bifidobacteria?" The TikTok logo and username "@electrixgreen" are also visible in the bottom right corner.

Dr. Hazan begins by stating that since the agenda is the vaccine, they decided to look at what the vaccines are doing to the microbiome. (0:00) She explains that she started enrolling doctors who were getting vaccinated, asking for their stool samples before and after vaccination. (0:07) A quick series of visual cuts shows a person in blue gloves and a mask preparing a syringe (0:07-0:11) and two people in masks and dark uniforms at a counter, possibly handling paperwork or medical supplies (0:11-0:12), before returning to Dr. Hazan. (0:12)

She recounts that patients would come to her, having heard she was testing the microbiome, wanting a baseline before vaccination. (0:21) Dr. Hazan mentions that she undertook this study herself and paid for it after grants and other funding proved to be a "waste of time, waste of money." (0:33)

After a month, she started noticing that the bifidobacteria, "this important microbe," was dropping in the first four patients she followed, pre and post-vaccination. (0:47) She then started questioning what was going on. (0:58) She describes it as "precision medicine," "forensics of the gut," where they compare a patient's microbiome before and after. (1:05) When only a certain group of microbes were "getting killed," she knew "you gotta pay attention." (1:13)

She continues that after 10, 20, 30, even 34 patients, they were seeing this "killing of the bifidobacteria." (1:21) She acknowledges that "nobody would have taken" this data for publication, so she decided to submit it as a poster to the American College of Gastro. (1:30) It was accepted and won the "best research award" as a poster. (1:41) Her colleagues called her, asking how this was happening, as vaccines are supposed to improve immunity, and bifidobacteria is a huge part of immunity. (1:47)

Dr. Hazan hypothesizes that it's creating a bacteriophage or bifidophage. (2:01) She specifically mentions four patients they followed for 90 days who were in "amazing shape," and their bifidobacteria dropped to "like zero—from like a million to like zero." (2:07) This "persistence in the damage" was observed not only for 90 days but for six to nine months later. (2:27) This finding made her "panic." (2:33)

She then explains that while looking at the microbiome of newborns to mothers who were breastfeeding, they noticed there was "no bifidobacteria in those newborns." (2:36) She states that newborns are supposed to have a "ton of bifidobacteria," about 90% of their microbiome. (2:49) This led her to ask if the "spike protein [is] going to the breast milk, into the baby's gut and killing whatever the baby's trying to build." (3:02)

Dr. Hazan connects this to her work on autism, noting that a common finding in autistic children is the "loss of bifidobacteria." (3:11) She wondered if this loss in newborns due to vaccinated mothers could be how it happens, leading to children stopping talking "two years down the road." (3:27) She states this discovery was "serendipitous" because she was looking at how to treat the virus, "knowing something nobody knew." (3:35)

She reiterates the importance of the microbiome in neurological problems, cancer, Crohn's disease, and Lyme disease. (4:20) At 0:48, the video briefly shows a scientific poster titled "PROGENABIOME Importance of Bifidobacteria in Crohn's Disease" and at 0:49, another poster titled "PROGENABIOME Loss of Bifidobacteria in Lyme disease: Cause or Effect." Both posters credit Sabine Hazan, Sonya Davey, Amelia Goudswaard, Brad Barrows, and Thomas J. Borody.

The interviewer, Jan Jekielek, is then seen on screen, his lower third graphic identifying him as "JAN JEKIELEK, Senior Editor, The Epoch Times." (4:44) He asks if her hypothesis is that the spike protein, whether from the virus or the vaccine, is killing the bifidobacteria and causing a problem. (4:44) Dr. Hazan confirms, "Correct." (4:58) She adds that they are doing research to try and prove this. (5:03)

Dr. Hazan then gives a personal example of research: during the pandemic, she deliberately killed her own bifidobacteria to see what would increase it. (5:23) She was the "guinea pig" throughout the pandemic. (5:31) She tried drinking a lot of kefir, but her bifidobacteria wasn't increasing, even though the kefir was labeled as containing bifidobacteria. (5:45) She went to Whole Foods and Ralphs, picked up 23 products that claimed to have bifidobacteria (including a "$27 tonic water"), and after testing them, found that "only three of them" actually had bifidobacteria. (6:10)

She concludes by emphasizing that "research is all about, you know, let me why isn't this happening? Why isn't it? Let me figure out why this is not happening." (6:52) She notes that her kefir didn't have bifidobacteria, so it wouldn't increase hers. (7:04) She then focused on fermented foods and is happy to report her bifidobacteria is back to normal. (7:08) She ends by stating that research is about finding one thing and then opening up new science, new research to discover more, calling her findings "fascinating." (7:18)

The video concludes with a black screen displaying "ONLY ON EPOCHTV.COM" (7:27) and then the TikTok logo and username again. (7:30)

At the bottom of the screen, a red banner identifies him as "Hasan BİTMEZ - Saadet Partisi Kocaeli Milletvekili" (Hasan Bitmez - Saadet Party Kocaeli Deputy). Below that, another line of text reads: "TBMM, KAMU DENETÇİLİĞİ KURUMU, ANAYASA MAHKEMESİ, SAYIŞTAY, ADALET BAKANLIĞI, YARGITAY, DANIŞTAY, DIŞİŞLERİ BAKANLIĞI İLE BAĞLI KURULUŞLAR" (Turkish Grand National Assembly, Public Oversight Institution, Constitutional Court, Court of Accounts, Ministry of Justice, Supreme Court of Appeals, Council of State, Ministry of Foreign Affairs and affiliated organizations). A small Turkish flag logo is visible on the left side of this banner. In the center of the screen, a logo for "GÜNDEM 7X24" with social media handles is overlaid.

From 0:00 to 0:02, the man speaks passionately, stating, "Allah'ın gazabından kurtulamayacaksınız" (You will not escape the wrath of Allah). He continues to speak with a stern expression, looking down at his paper and then up at the audience.
At 0:03, he continues, "Hepinize..." (To all of you...).
At 0:04, he completes his thought, saying "...saygıyla selamlıyorum" (I salute you with respect). As he says this, he lowers his head and appears to lose his balance slightly.

At 0:06, the camera cuts to a wide shot of the parliamentary chamber. The speaker is visible at a podium on the right side of the frame. The chamber is largely empty in the foreground, with many red chairs, but members are seated in the background. Two large Turkish flags flank a central panel at the back of the chamber, which has text reading "EGEMENLİK KAYITSIZ ŞARTSIZ MİLLETİNDİR" (Sovereignty Unconditionally Belongs to the Nation). Above this, several ornate light fixtures are visible. Two large screens on either side of the chamber display what appears to be a live feed of the proceedings or a similar image. A logo for "TBMM CANLI" (Turkish Grand National Assembly Live) and "anka" is visible in the top right corner.

At 0:07, as the speaker is finishing his speech and saying "saygıyla selamlıyorum", he stumbles and collapses to the floor, falling out of view behind the podium. A collective gasp or murmur is heard from the chamber. People seated nearby immediately rush towards him. Several individuals, some in suits, others in more casual attire, quickly move to his aid, with some bending over him on the floor.

By 0:09, more people are seen moving towards the area where he collapsed. Some members in the foreground are standing and looking concerned.
By 0:12, a group has gathered around the fallen speaker, with some actively trying to assist him. A female voice can be heard saying "birleşime" (to the session), possibly indicating a call for order or a break. Another voice says "20 dakika" (20 minutes), which could be a suggestion for a recess. The scene remains chaotic as more people gather around the fallen man, with several individuals visibly distressed. The video ends at 0:14 with the commotion still ongoing in the chamber.

caption.video0:
The video features a man, identifiable as "nontoxicdad" by the watermark, in different store aisles, discussing the ingredient Trisodium Phosphate (TSP).

The video begins at **0:00** with the man standing in a grocery store cereal aisle, holding a family-size box of Cinnamon Toast Crunch cereal. A skull and crossbones warning symbol is displayed over the cereal box. The overlaid text reads, "A toxic paint cleaner ingredient." The man states, "A toxic paint cleaner ingredient."

At **0:01**, the camera zooms in on the ingredient list of the Cinnamon Toast Crunch box. A finger points to the words "Trisodium Phosphate" highlighted in yellow. The overlaid text says, "found in Cinnamon." The man's audio continues, "found in Cinnamon Toast Crunch."

At **0:03**, the man is back holding the cereal box in the grocery aisle. The text "Come with me" appears, followed by a Home Depot logo and the word "Depot" as he says, "come with me to Home Depot."

At **0:05**, the man gestures with his hands, still holding the cereal box, and the text "and check this out." appears. His audio continues, "and check this out."

At **0:06**, the scene changes to a Home Depot store, specifically an aisle stocked with paint thinners and cleaning supplies. The man, now wearing a brown hoodie, points to the shelves above him. He says, "Here we are at Home Depot, at the paint thinner and paint cleaner section." The overlaid text mirrors his speech.

At **0:09**, the man points to a green box on the top shelf labeled "TSP-PF Phosphate-free." He states, "And you can see there is TSP, but it's TSP substitute." The overlaid text reflects his words.

At **0:12**, the camera shows a close-up of the "TSP-PF Phosphate-free" box, with his finger pointing to "Phosphate-free." The text reads, "It's tsp phosphate-free." He then says, "I talked to them."

At **0:15**, the man is back in the wider shot of the Home Depot aisle, explaining, "They stopped carrying regular TSP." Over his hand, an image of a clear bag labeled "TSP Trisodium Phosphate" appears, alongside the text "Trisodium."

At **0:19**, as the man gestures, a skull and crossbones warning symbol appears again. He explains, "because it's toxic to people that are using it." The text overlay reads, "phosphate because it's toxic to people that are using it."

At **0:22**, the man holds a small yellow resealable pouch labeled "Sunnyside TSP Trisodium Phosphate." He says, "So what I did is I actually ordered it, it's online at Home Depot." A screenshot of a Home Depot online product listing for "16 lbs. TSP Heavy Duty Cleaner in 4 lbs. Resealable Pouches" by Sunnyside is displayed. The text overlay reads, "it's online at Home Depot."

At **0:25**, he continues to hold the pouch, asking, "why did they pull it. It says right here."

At **0:27**, a close-up of the yellow pouch's warning label is shown. It reads: "CAUTION! HARMFUL IF SWALLOWED OR INHALED. EYE AND SKIN IRRITANT. MAY CAUSE EYE AND SKIN BURNS. KEEP OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN." The text overlay states, "if swallowed or inhaled." The man's audio matches, "Harmful if swallowed or inhaled."

At **0:29**, back in the Home Depot aisle, the man emphasizes, "This is the same TSP that they're"

At **0:31**, a close-up of cereal pieces being poured is shown, and the man's audio continues, "putting in your cereal." The text "putting in your cereal" is overlaid.

At **0:33**, the man holds the yellow pouch again, saying, "It's harmful if it's swallowed. Why?" The text reflects his question.

At **0:35**, he explains, "Because it's a trisodium phosphate salt." A Google search result box appears, defining TSP as "a white, crystalline, inorganic compound with the chemical formula Na3PO4. It's highly soluble in water, producing an alkaline solution. TSP has many uses," The text overlay states, "Because it's a trisodium phosphate salt."

At **0:39**, the man gestures toward his stomach and then with his hands, explaining, "And when you eat it, it breaks up in your digestive system to sodium ions." A graphic of the Sodium (Na) element from the periodic table appears.

At **0:42**, he extends his hands, and a visual effect shows a brown, clumpy substance (representing phosphate) forming. He concludes, "and phosphate. And phosphate is toxic to your body, leading" The text overlay reads, "and phosphate. And phosphate is toxic leading."

At **0:45**, an image of a man holding his head, indicating a headache, appears with a skull and crossbones symbol. The man's audio states, "to neurological and behavioral effects." The text overlay says, "to neurological effects."

At **0:47**, a 3D animation of a human kidney is shown, and the man says, "Kidney damage," with corresponding text.

At **0:48**, a close-up of a human hand with visible blood vessels is displayed. The man's audio states, "and cardiovascular issues." The text overlay reads, "and cardiovascular."

At **0:49**, the man is back in the Home Depot aisle, holding the yellow pouch. He continues, "It can even interfere with calcium absorption," with the text overlay "It can even interfere with calcium absorption."

At **0:52**, an image of Bugs Bunny flexing appears, followed by a diagram comparing "Healthy Bone" and "Osteoporotic Bone." The man states, "leading to weaker bones and osteoporosis." The text overlay displays, "leading to weaker bones and osteoporosis."

At **0:55**, the man holds the pouch, stating, "Long term."

At **0:56**, the video cuts back to the close-up of the Cinnamon Toast Crunch ingredient list, with a finger pointing to "Trisodium Phosphate." The man emphatically says, "So, for the love of God, if you see TSP on any food label," The text overlay reflects his speech.

Finally, at **0:59**, the man is back in the Home Depot aisle, holding the yellow pouch. He concludes, "especially your kids' cereal, put it back on the shelf if you want to live a non-toxic lifestyle." The text overlay matches his words.

The video features an anime-style female character with white hair, blue eyes, and orange crab-like hair accessories. She wears a blue top with yellow/orange accents around her shoulders. Throughout the video, a static text overlay at the top reads, "There's no way this is real."

The character begins speaking at **0:00**, with her mouth animated to match her words. She states, "So there's this Gamer Supps flavor called Lean," and the word "Supps" appears at the bottom of the screen, followed by "There's this Gamer Supps flavor called Lean."
At **0:04**, as she mentions "Lean," an image of a purple Gamer Supps tub, labeled "LEAN" and featuring a cartoonish depiction of Joe Biden, is briefly superimposed on the screen. She continues, "and it has Joe Biden on it."
From **0:06**, the background behind the anime character changes to a blurred image of two men in dark uniforms, one clearly identifiable by "SECRET SERVICE" on his vest. The character states, "And they got sent like a cease and desist from the Secret Service." The words "Like," "Desist," and "Secret" appear sequentially at the bottom of the screen, synchronized with her speech.

At **0:10**, the original "LEAN" Gamer Supps tub with Joe Biden reappears on screen. The character explains, "And so what they did was they brought it back." The words "And" and "Did" appear in sync.
At **0:11**, a second Gamer Supps tub appears next to the first one. This new tub is labeled "FENT" and features a character resembling Joe Biden but with sunglasses and a different hairstyle (labeled "Bo Jiden") next to an anime girl in water. The character exclaims, "It's legally distinct! Bo Jiden," with the words "brought," "Back," "Distinct!" and "Bo Jiden" appearing in sequence at the bottom.

The scene returns to the anime character alone at **0:16**, who then asks, "So my question is, if Joe Biden wasn't the president anymore, could they put him back on regular like this?" The words "Question," "If," "Wasn't," "Anymore," "Him," and "Regular" appear as she speaks. The "LEAN" tub with Joe Biden is briefly shown again at **0:21** as she says "like this?"
At **0:22**, the character, looking slightly downcast and shaking her head, says "No." She then asks, "Why not? What about if he died?"
At **0:25**, she posits a hypothetical: "Hypothetically speaking if Joe Biden suddenly passed away without—" As she speaks this, a semi-transparent text overlay appears over her face, stating: "She is trying to make a joke, not a threat :)"
She continues at **0:29**, "...you know, he just suddenly died mysteriously, could they put him back on?" The "FENT" tub with "Bo Jiden" is displayed again at **0:30**.

At **0:31**, she then asks, "Doesn't he have like cancer or something? Am I making that up? Am I making that up right now?" As she asks this, another semi-transparent text overlay appears: "she is in fact making that up mr government."
A simulated chat message bubble appears above her at **0:34**, reading "Chat: He's just old." The character responds, "He's just old. Oh, okay!"
Finally, at **0:36**, she questions, "Don't they like shoot him up with meds to keep him awake during his debates and speeches and stuff?" Another semi-transparent text overlay appears: "mr government she doesn't know what she is on about."
The video concludes at **0:39** with the character, now looking slightly concerned, stating, "Please help him!"

caption.video0:
This video shows a split-screen layout. At the top, a yellow banner contains text stating: "Instagram has allowed a health misinformation video on Ayurvedic treatment of eye disorder (myopia) to continue with more than 9M views and counting, while quietly removing my critical video that informs the public of correct treatments showing scientific evidence for the effective treatment because Ayurveda quacks reported it. How can doctors battle misinformation if the platform is biased towards supporting quacks and frauds?"

Below the banner, two Instagram video clips are displayed side-by-side. In both clips, a person (appears to be a young man) is lying down with a donut-shaped ring of dough placed around their right eye. This dough ring forms a well, which is then filled with a dark liquid, likely an oil. A person's hand is visible pouring the liquid into the dough well in some frames.

The left Instagram video, titled "Myopia Treatment in Ayurveda," features text overlays that read: "Get Myopia treated," "Get dry eyes cured," and "Get Computer syndrome treated." It also has "AI AYURNNIDANAM" visible. This video has "31.x" (partially visible) likes.

The right Instagram video, captioned "theliverdr," contains text overlays: "Stitch incoming" and "Netra Basti, Ayurvedic eye therapy, absolute BS!". This video is 2:40 in length.

At the bottom of the screen, a man with glasses and a light-colored collared shirt is speaking directly to the camera. He gestures with his right hand throughout his narration.

**Audio Description (Speaker's Narration):**

The speaker begins by pointing to the left Instagram video, stating, "This is a video uploaded by an Ayurveda practitioner who calls herself as Dr. Urvi." (0:00) He clarifies that "Ayurveda practitioners are not actually doctors, they're actually Vaidyas." (0:09)

He continues to explain the content of the left video: "So Vaidya Urvi uploaded this video which claims that this particular treatment shown here, where they place a donut around your eyes and fill it with oil, claims to have cured myopia, which is nearsightedness, dry eyes, and also a delusional condition known as computer syndrome, which I've never heard of or never read in my medical textbooks." (0:11-0:33)

Pointing to his own video on the right, he says, "This video is the one which I uploaded, criticizing this video, where I actually shown scientific evidence for how to treat myopia or nearsightedness, and criticizing the pseudo-scientific claims that this particular video makes." (0:33-0:47)

He then highlights the issue with Instagram's platform moderation: "Instagram has deleted my video, which is science-based, and allowed this nonsensical video to continue to be on the platform." (0:47-0:55) He notes the misinformation video "has now more than 9 million views, and people think that doing this nonsense can actually cure them of refractory error, which is myopia or nearsightedness." (0:56-1:07)

The speaker expresses frustration: "When the platform themselves are promoting medical misinformation, fraud and quackery, what can doctors who try to battle medical misinformation do?" (1:07-1:17) He reiterates, "This particular video is continuing to be on Instagram, whereas this video, which I made, criticizing the pseudo-scientific claims of this video, is now deleted." (1:17-1:28)

He elaborates on the Ayurvedic treatment, "Netra Basti": "This particular treatment, which is given by Ayurvedic practitioners in various wellness centers, known as Netra Basti, claims that by making a donut out of all-purpose flour or Maida, and putting it around the eyes and filling that with various types of medicated oils can actually cure various eye disorders." (1:28-1:47) He dismisses the treatment as unscientific: "This treatment has absolutely no scientific, anatomical or physiological explanation of how it actually works. This is complete BS." (1:47-1:56)

He provides a brief medical explanation for myopia: "Nearsightedness or myopia is a disease of the cornea or because of a different in shape of the eyeball, in which the light does not focus on the retina." (1:56-2:06) He emphasizes the correct treatments: "The only way this can be corrected or treated is by using glasses or using contact lenses or refractive surgery, not by placing a donut on top of the eye and filling it with oil. There is absolutely no way that this actually treats anything from the eye point of view." (2:06-2:22)

The speaker concludes by condemning Ayurvedic practitioners: "Ayurvedic practitioners are pseudo-science peddlers, and they actually peddle a lot of this medical misinformation, taking away patients from realistic healthcare." (2:22-2:32) The video then zooms in on the right Instagram video as he states, "So next time when Ayurveda practitioners make these extraordinary claims on delusional treatments, please ask for medical and scientific evidence for the same, and someone please tell me why they wasted a whole donut just like that." (2:33-2:48)

The video opens with a document titled "Australian Government Department of Health Therapeutic Goods Administration" and "BNT162B2 [mRNA] COVID-19 vaccine (COMIRNATY®)" from January 2021. The document shows a table labeled "Table 4-2. Mean concentration of radioactivity (sexes combined) in tissue and blood following a single IM dose of 50 µg mRNA/rat." This table lists various samples (tissues and organs) and their "Total Lipid Concentration (µg lipid equiv./g (or mL))" at different time points (0.25 min, 1 h, 2 h, 4 h, 8 h, 24 h, 48 h).
On the right side of the screen, a man with a beard is visible, looking intently through a microscope.

As the audio begins, a male voice states, "The TGA desperately tried to prevent this document being released under Freedom of Information." Simultaneously, the text "CRITICAL TRIAL DATA HELD BACK FROM AUSTRALIANS ...BY TGA" appears overlaying the table. The speaker continues, "Would have anyone in their right mind injected this stuff into their body if they knew the mRNA would spread to their brain, liver, and bone marrow, and female ovaries?" tweetText:
RFK Jr.’s VP Pick Calls for Chronic Disease Investigation

Today, @RobertKennedyJr announced entrepreneur Nicole Shanahan as his VP running mate.

She said, “Conditions like autism used to be one in 10,000. Now, here in the state of California, it is one in 22.”

Three things are causing the chronic disease epidemic, Shanahan declared:

1.) “One is the toxic substances in our environment, like endocrine, disrupting chemicals in our food, water, and soil.”

2.) “Second is electromagnetic pollution. You don’t hear politicians talking much about that either.”

3.) The third cause is the cumulative impact of medications and vaccinations received throughout childhood, for which comprehensive safety studies assessing long-term effects are currently lacking.

• “No single safety study can assess the cumulative impact of one prescription on top of another ... We just don’t do that study right now, and we ought to.”

The video opens with a medium close-up shot of a woman, Nicole Shanahan, standing at a podium. She is speaking into two microphones. She has long, dark brown hair, parted in the middle and styled half-up, half-down. She is wearing a vibrant purple blazer over a white top and small turquoise stud earrings. A delicate gold necklace with a small charm is visible. Her facial expression is serious and focused as she speaks. The background is a blurry blue wall with a large, indistinct red shape on the right. In the bottom left corner, a white and red "KENNEDY24" logo is visible.

At 00:04, the camera zooms out to a wider shot, revealing the full stage and a large part of the audience. Shanahan is still at the podium, now visible in her full purple suit. Behind her, a large blue backdrop displays "KENNEDY SHANAHAN" in large white and red letters, with "THE FUTURE STARTS NOW" underneath. On either side of the main backdrop are two smaller banners that read "KENNEDY FOR PRESIDENT" and "KENNEDY24.COM". American flags are positioned on both sides of the stage. The stage itself is decorated with red, white, and blue bunting. The audience, a diverse crowd of hundreds of people, fills the floor in front of the stage, facing her. Many are holding up small American flags or "KENNEDY SHANAHAN" signs.

Shanahan continues speaking, emphasizing her points with hand gestures. She is passionate and animated. The audience listens intently, with some people nodding in agreement.

At 00:15, the camera returns to a medium close-up of Shanahan. She continues her speech, and her expression shifts to one of concern as she talks about "chronic disease." She occasionally glances to her left or right, maintaining eye contact with different parts of the audience.

At 00:23, the camera cuts back to the wider shot of the stage and audience. Shanahan is at the podium, and the audience is still very engaged, many still holding their flags and signs. The crowd appears to be quite large, extending back into the venue.

At 00:35, another medium close-up of Shanahan, similar to the initial shot. She is talking about "toxic substances in our environment" and lists "pesticide residues, industrial pollutants, microplastics, PFAS, food additives, and forever chemicals." She uses expressive hand gestures to emphasize the widespread nature of these issues.

At 00:51, Shanahan's expression becomes more animated, showing anger as she describes the environmental and health issues. She gestures with both hands. The audience can be heard reacting with murmurs of agreement.

At 01:00, she transitions to her second point, "electromagnetic pollution." Her tone is serious, and she points out that politicians rarely discuss this issue.

At 01:10, a wider shot shows the audience applauding and cheering loudly, waving their American flags and signs enthusiastically as Shanahan says, "We need to investigate every possible cause of chronic disease epidemic that is devouring our nation from the inside." The energy in the room is high, with many standing and clapping.

At 01:20, the camera returns to a wider shot of Shanahan on stage, and she moves to her third point: "medications." Her voice is serious and she expresses regret in discussing this topic.

At 01:32, a medium close-up of Shanahan again. She explains that no single safety study assesses the cumulative impact of multiple prescriptions and vaccinations, indicating this is a gap in current medical research. She uses hand gestures to illustrate the "one shot on top of another shot."

At 01:44, the audience erupts in cheers and applause, waving their flags vigorously, seemingly in strong agreement with her point about lack of safety studies for cumulative medical interventions. Shanahan smiles slightly, acknowledges their support, and then continues speaking.

At 01:54, the camera returns to a medium close-up of Shanahan. She is visibly pleased by the audience's reaction, smiling and nodding. Her tone becomes more determined as she speaks about what can and will be done.

At 02:01, she highlights a statistic: "conditions like autism used to be one in 10,000, now here in the state of California it is one in 22." Her voice is grave, underscoring the severity of the increase.

At 02:13, Shanahan's expression is again serious as she lists other health conditions: "allergies, obesity, anxiety, depression." She concludes, "our children are not well, our people are not well, and our country will not be well for very much longer if we don't heed this desperate call for attention."

At 02:27, the audience responds with more enthusiastic applause and cheers, once again waving their flags and signs. The camera pans across the crowd, showing their continued engagement.

At 02:44, Shanahan, in a medium close-up, talks about speaking to government agencies and politicians, noting that they acknowledge the problem but take no action. Her expression is one of frustration.

At 02:50, her tone shifts to hope as she names Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the only candidate who takes the chronic disease epidemic seriously. The crowd erupts in cheers and applause, chanting "Kennedy! Kennedy!" and waving their flags. Shanahan claps along with them and smiles broadly, gesturing with her right hand to emphasize her alliance with Kennedy.

At 03:02, the shot is wider, showing the full stage and the energized audience, all actively participating in the applause and flag-waving.

At 03:07, Shanahan is back in a medium close-up, visibly moved by the crowd's reaction. She says it's not about "a new pill" but about "cleaning up our environment" and a "shift in our priorities." She uses open hand gestures to convey these broader solutions.

At 03:38, the audience cheers and applauds again, acknowledging her call for politicians to listen to the people's suffering from chronic disease. The wide shot shows the crowd's continued support.

At 03:52, Shanahan states that she will assemble the "best technologists and scientists in the world" to use AI and computation to examine health records databases globally to solve chronic disease. She gestures assertively with her hands, projecting confidence.

At 04:18, the audience explodes with cheers and applause, waving flags and signs, reinforcing their agreement with her ambitious plan. The camera captures the widespread enthusiasm.

At 04:23, the video ends with a final medium close-up of Shanahan, looking determined and hopeful, stating, "We will find the answers to our most pressing health concerns within weeks." She nods confidently as the video fades out.

tweetText:
Shared from @queencitydom on Instagram 🙏😔

March 18th, 2021 is the day that I made a decision that flipped my entire world upside down.

I’ve been EXTREMELY quiet about this decision and the adverse effects that it has caused me simply because I didn’t want to scare anyone out of making their own decision on receiving the vax..

With all this being said, I made the decision to get my vax before I left Las Vegas to move back to my home state of North Carolina because I wanted to play it safe and make sure those around me weren’t in harms way. I also do have underlying autoimmune issues which I have openly talked about for a while now.

I’m not ashamed of why I received the vax because I know my heart was in a good place and my intentions were pure..and yet here I am struggling with adverse side effects from this life changing decision.

Doctors have been pretty lost on what exactly is going on..running multiple tests, medical imaging and neurological exams coming back with no answers..

Here’s just a glimpse of what I’ve been dealing with-

Symptoms that come and go:
Dystonia
Difficulty walking and weakness within the legs
Dull aching & sharp shooting pains within the legs
Vertigo
Heart palpitations
Lack of sensation throughout the body
Cold sensation in the legs
Pain behind the eyes
Tremor in right hand

Symptoms that are 24/7:
Short term memory loss
Trouble forming sentences
Feeling out of body
Depth perception is off
Visual Changes
Severe brain fog

I am not sharing this post to scare anyone out of making their own decision, however I do believe that people must know exactly what the risks are. These stories shouldn’t be so hidden on social media platforms or the news and I’m done being so quiet about this..

If there is anyone out there that is experiencing something similar don’t be afraid to let your voice be heard. Please share!!! I can use all the insight and help I can get right now

A white blanket or throw is draped over the back of the couch, behind her. She appears to be in distress, her face red and crumpled from crying, with tears visible on her cheeks. Her mouth is slightly open, and she emits continuous, audible sobs throughout the video. A crumpled white tissue or paper is on her lap.

Two small dogs are on the couch next to her, on her right side. The dog closer to her, positioned on her lap/thigh area, is a small, light tan-colored dog, possibly a Pomeranian or similar breed. Behind this dog, partially obscured, is another small dog, mostly black with white markings, possibly a French Bulldog or a similar short-nosed breed.

From 0:00 to 0:04, the woman is visibly sobbing, with her hands clasped together near her lap, fidgeting slightly. The tan dog looks up at her with what appears to be a concerned expression.

Around 0:04, the tan dog begins to actively try and comfort the woman. It gently nudges her left hand with its nose, its tail giving a slight, slow wag. The woman continues to cry, but her right hand makes a slight, soft petting motion towards the dog.

By 0:07, the tan dog is more insistent, pushing its head into her left hand and then attempting to reach her face with its nose. Its tail wags more visibly. The woman's sobs are continuous and pronounced.

From 0:10 to 0:16, the tan dog's efforts to comfort her intensify. It lifts its paws onto her left arm, trying to get closer to her face. It then stretches its neck and starts licking the woman's chin and cheek repeatedly. The woman, while still sobbing, slightly leans her head back to allow the dog to reach her, and a faint, sad smile or softening of her features briefly appears as the dog licks her. Her right hand gently rests on the dog's back. The black and white dog watches the interaction from behind the tan dog. The video ends with the tan dog still licking the woman's face, her sobs still audible. The overall mood is one of profound sadness for the woman, contrasted with the unwavering comfort offered by her pets.

00:00 - "...because another dirty little secret that's come out in all this is that, that we've been ignoring the, the issue of parasites and cancer. Right, if you look at the, if you look at, um, a cancer, um metastasis, like or or an adenocarcinoma of the bowel for example, or or breast cancer, under the light microscope. caption.video0:
The video features a woman with dark hair, pulled back, standing behind a purple podium with a microphone. She is wearing a peach-colored blazer over a pink top. Behind her are three flags: the American flag on the left, and two Florida state flags on the right and center. The podium displays the text "FLORIDA CANCER INNOVATION FUND" in white lettering, with a purple ribbon logo next to the word "FLORIDA."

The woman speaks with animated hand gestures, emphasizing her points.

Here's a detailed breakdown of the video's content:

*   **0:00-0:06:** The woman says, "...just it's so, it's so important. So that's one of the components of this first round of grants that are going out." She gestures with open hands to her sides, then brings them together at the podium.
*   **0:06-0:17:** She continues, "And then the second portion of this, uh, and and honestly, I've had so many people come up to me and talk about this, and that is the generic drug repurposing component of this grant." She extends her hands, palms up, as she speaks.
*   **0:17-0:21:** She specifies, "And so that's specifically for things like Ivermectin." Her hands remain open, gesturing slightly.
*   **0:21-0:34:** She acknowledges the drug's past media attention: "So I know that that's been in the news quite a bit. It made a lot of news during COVID. Mel Gibson was on Joe Rogan's podcast and he was talking about people that he was friends with and they overcame stage four cancer." She makes a sweeping gesture with her right hand to the side, then brings both hands to the podium.
*   **0:34-0:42:** Sharing her perspective, she states, "And so here's like my perspective and I've talked to the Surgeon General about this. Like that's generic form. I think it costs like 77 cents per pill." She again uses open-handed gestures, then briefly clenches her fists.
*   **0:42-1:13:** She raises a crucial question about incentive: "Is there any incentive on the part of some to want to research this when you're not guaranteed to have a big ROI, right? If you have a novel technology or a pharmaceutical, and you have that monopoly and that competitive edge, well then you're probably going to make a lot more money. If you're researching like something like Ivermectin that's generic, are you going to be able to, uh, A find a study that you can get the money to be able to fund it, but are you guaranteed revenue for your company going down in the future?" She repeatedly uses hand gestures to illustrate concepts like "big ROI" (extending her hands wide), "monopoly" (holding her right hand as if clutching something), and "generic" (open hands).
*   **1:13-1:37:** She emphasizes the state's unique position: "I I don't know the answer to that, but I do know that we should look at it. We should look at the benefits of it. We shouldn't just speculate and guess, we should as the state of Florida with over a billion dollars in funding since Ron DeSantis took office for cancer, we should be looking into things that are in generic form and do they show outcomes and do they show hope for people who are suffering?" She points her right thumb behind her as she mentions Ron DeSantis, then gestures with both hands again.
*   **1:37-1:55:** She continues to explain Florida's motivation: "And we're uniquely positioned to do that because we're not in it for an ROI from a corporate standpoint. We're in it for an ROI on behalf of you, the taxpayer and you who has a family member who's suffering from cancer and God forbid that you suffer from cancer." Her gestures become more pointed and empathetic.
*   **1:55-2:39:** She concludes with a personal reflection and an expression of pride: "And I'm just honestly, I don't like talking about cancer. Ever. I told Dr. Panetta on more than one occasion, I'd rather sit in my closet, never say the cancer word again, but I am so immensely proud of what this state has done to really step up to try to provide hope to a lot of people, to invest in meaningful things. It's one thing to appropriate money, but it's another thing to drive outcomes, to hold yourself accountable and to try to incentivize prevention and better treatments. So very proud of this guy for a lot of reasons, because he put up with me while I was battling cancer, but um, very proud of of what he's done to really stand by me through the fight and to give hope to a lot of people across the state." She makes a "stop" gesture with both hands, then clasps them together, and finally points behind her again, likely referring to Governor DeSantis, as she mentions his support during her cancer battle. She then picks up some papers from the podium and steps back slightly.

*   **0:00-0:30:** The shot is a medium close-up of the interviewee, a white man with short, neatly styled brown hair, wearing a dark blue suit, a white shirt, and a red tie. He is seated at a white desk. The background is a blurred image of the Houses of Parliament under a bright blue sky, with a green tree line visible. He is actively speaking, making various hand gestures to emphasize his points. A blue circular graphic with "sky" in white text is visible in the blurred background on the left, and "sky news" is visible on the right.
    *   The lower third banner reads "BREAKING NEWS Shadow Health Sec: Must modernise & change NHS". Below this, a scrolling ticker displays "Deputy Prime Minister Oliver Dowden says the Government's Rwanda Bill 'gets the balance right' and does not rule out future amendments for peace". The interviewee is identified as "Shadow Health Secretary Wes Streeting MP".
*   **0:30-0:37:** The camera pulls back to a wider shot, showing both the interviewee and the interviewer. The interviewer, a bald Black man with a beard, also wearing a dark blue suit, white shirt, and a patterned purple tie, is seated opposite the interviewee at the same white desk. He holds a pair of glasses in his right hand and has a piece of paper in front of him. The Houses of Parliament background is now clearer and more prominent, reflecting slightly on the glossy white surface of the desk. Wes Streeting continues to speak, gesturing with both hands. The interviewer listens attentively, occasionally nodding.
*   **0:37-0:49:** The shot cuts back to a medium close-up of Wes Streeting, similar to the beginning. He continues to elaborate on his points, using hand gestures.
*   **0:49-0:56:** The camera widens again to show both men. Wes Streeting continues to speak animatedly, using both hands in a chopping motion to illustrate a point, then bringing them together. The interviewer is leaning forward slightly, still holding his glasses, and listening.
*   **0:56-1:09:** The shot returns to a medium close-up of Wes Streeting. He speaks with a determined expression, using a precise finger gesture, then folding his hands.
*   **1:09-1:24:** The camera widens to show both men. Wes Streeting has finished his point and is now listening, resting his hands on the table. The interviewer begins to speak, leaning forward slightly and using his hands while holding his glasses.
    *   The lower third banner has changed to "Shadow Health Sec: Complacency on NHS & its future". The scrolling ticker remains the same.
*   **1:24-1:27:** The shot is a close-up of the interviewer. He is speaking, looking down briefly at his notes, then back at Wes Streeting.
*   **1:27-2:29:** The shot cuts back to a medium close-up of Wes Streeting. He listens for a moment with a serious expression, then begins to speak, using hand gestures to emphasize his points, particularly raising his index finger. The background and lower third remain consistent.

**Audio Description:**

*   **0:00-0:30:** Wes Streeting states, "fully committed to the NHS as a public service, free at the point of use. And whether it's Singapore, Australia where I was recently, um, continental Europe. I don't think the problem with the NHS in our country is that it's free at the point of use and publicly funded. It's not the model of funding that's a problem, it's where the money goes. Because when you compare the NHS to other OECD countries, we're pretty much top of the charts in terms of hospital spending, but when it comes to primary care, community services, mental health, diagnostics, we're either near the bottom, at the bottom, or seriously lagging behind."
*   **0:30-0:36:** The interviewer interjects, "So, we've got enough money, but we're spending it in the wrong place."
*   **0:36-0:56:** Wes Streeting agrees, "In the wrong place. So, for example, there are now 2,000 fewer GPs. Those smaller number of GPs are providing a million more appointments than they were during before the pandemic. But people still struggle to get a GP appointment. So instead of going to see a GP which costs the NHS 40 quid, they often end up in A&E which costs 400 pounds."
*   **0:56-1:10:** Wes Streeting continues, "So that's why I say to the NHS and make the case to the public about Labour's reform agenda, it's not just about more money, it's about where the money goes to get better outcomes for patients and better value for taxpayers money."
*   **1:10-1:27:** The interviewer then asks, "Well, one way that they get better value in Singapore is um, low-paid labour. A third of the Singapore workforce consists of low-paid Chinese, Filipino and Malaysian immigrant workers. The equivalent here would be to have 25 million immigrant workers. Now your party wants a number of immigrants down and pay up. How are you going to square that circle? If you want to copy any part of that model, you're going to have to do something like that."
*   **1:27-2:29:** Wes Streeting replies, "What we have an over-reliance on overseas workers working in our health and care system, and that's challenging for two reasons. One is we've got a global shortage of health and care workers, so we can't assume that that pool of talent will always be there to draw from. And secondly, the UK is recruiting from red list countries, countries identified by the World Health Organisation as having a serious shortfall of their own. So I think that's immoral. We've got to invest in training our own home-grown talent. That's why we're committed to double the number of medical school places so we're not turning away thousands of straight A students. But we've also got to improve the pay of care workers so we can recruit and retain those brilliant care professionals who are crucial for the future of meeting the challenges of growing aging society, rising chronic disease and and rising cost pressures."

summary: The post making claims about malaria in Florida is false & misleading.  The genetically modified mosquitoes released in Florida are completely different from those that transmit malaria. The Florida project uses Aedes aegypti mosquitoes, which cannot carry or transmit malaria  https://www.factcheck.org/2023/07/scicheck-u-s-acquired-malaria-cases-spark-false-claims-of-links-to-gates-funded-research/  https://factcheck.afp.com/doc.afp.com.33MA7HB

caption.video0:
The video is titled "Bill Gates Wants You To Experience Malaria" and features Bill Gates speaking on a stage, interspersed with text overlays that comment on his actions.

**Scene 1 (0:00-0:15):**
Bill Gates, wearing a light blue and white striped button-down shirt and glasses, stands on a stage with a dark blue background. He is speaking directly to the audience, gesturing with his hands. He says, "...But malaria, that even the million deaths a year caused by malaria, greatly understate its impact. Over 200 million people at any one time are suffering from it."
A text overlay at the bottom reads: "Bill Gates Released Genetically Modified Mosquitoes in Florida, Now There's A Malaria Outbreak."

**Scene 2 (0:15-0:21):**
Bill Gates continues speaking and gesturing, saying, "...It means that you can't get the economies in these areas going because there's just it holds things back so much. Now, malaria is, of course, transmitted by mosquitoes."
The text overlay remains at the bottom of the screen.

**Scene 3 (0:21-0:31):**
The camera angle widens slightly, showing Bill Gates walking towards a small table on the stage. He reaches down and picks up a glass jar, then opens it. As he does this, he says, "I brought some here so you could experience this. We'll let, let those roam around the uh auditorium a little bit."
He upturns the jar, seemingly releasing its contents into the air above the table. The audience can be heard reacting with gasps and murmurs. The camera then zooms in on the table, showing a digital clock displaying "12:04" and counting down, though the time is irrelevant to the action.
The text overlay is still visible.

**Scene 4 (0:31-0:38):**
The camera cuts back to a close-up of Bill Gates. He is smiling and clapping his hands together playfully. He says, "There. There's no reason only poor people should have, have the experience." The audience laughs in response to his comment.
The text overlay continues to be displayed.

**Scene 5 (0:38-0:41):**
The camera shows a shot of the audience, many of whom are smiling and laughing, and some are clapping. The digital clock "12:00" is briefly visible in the foreground.
The text overlay is still present.

**Scene 6 (0:41-0:48):**
The camera pans out to a wide shot of the stage, showing Bill Gates standing in front of a large screen that displays an image of a mosquito on a finger, with "TED" written below it. He continues speaking, saying, "Those those mosquitoes are not, not infected, but uh." The audience is visible in the foreground.
The text overlay remains throughout the video.

summary: The video post is from 2023 and is misleading.  While some local Republican groups in Florida have called for mRNA COVID-19 vaccines to be banned and labeled as “bioweapons,” these actions are not legally binding. They represent political opinions, not state policy or law:  https://fullfact.org/health/florida-covid-vaccines-banned/  https://health.wusf.usf.edu/health-news-florida/2023-07-20/politifact-fl-state-has-not-taken-steps-to-ban-covid-19-vaccines  https://cbs12.com/news/local/brevard-county-gop-covid-19-vaccine-bioweapon-gov-ron-desantis-florida-attorney-general-ashley-moody-treasure-coast-july-13-2023

caption.video0:
The video is a news report titled "BOOM: Florida To Officially Classify mRNA COVID Shots As Illegal 'Bio-Weapons'".

The video begins at **0:00** with two news anchors, a man and a woman, sitting at a news desk. The male anchor, Jim Grimes, is on the left, and the female anchor, Liz Quirantes, is on the right. Both are dressed professionally. Behind them is a blue backdrop with the "12 I-TEAM NEWS INVESTIGATION" logo.

At **0:00**, the male anchor states, "COVID vaccine turns out to be back in the news."
At **0:02**, the female anchor adds, "Republican Party leaders on the Space Coast are minutes away from officially calling on the governor and other state leaders to ban MRNA-based COVID vaccines immediately." On-screen graphics identify the anchors as "JIM GRIMES" and "LIZ QUIRANTES."
At **0:10**, the female anchor continues, "I-TEAM Chief Investigator Mike Magnoli has more on what exactly is going on. Mike."

At **0:16**, the video transitions to a close-up graphic of the "CBS 12 NEWS I-TEAM" logo, which includes a microphone icon.

At **0:18**, the scene changes to reporter Mike Magnoli, dressed in a suit and tie, standing in front of a blue background with a subtly visible "I-TEAM" logo. He is holding a microphone. On-screen text identifies him as "MIKE MAGNOLI" and "CBS 12 NEWS I-TEAM."
At **0:19**, Mike Magnoli says, "Good evening everybody. While in their own words, the leaders of Brevard County's GOP say that they believe the vaccines are a biological weapon."
At **0:25**, the on-screen text changes to "COUNTY GOP: 'COVID VAX A BIOWEAPON?'" while Mike Magnoli continues, "As you say, this is the executive committee of the party in Brevard. They haven't hit send on this letter yet, but that vote coming up at 6:30 and I'm told it's fairly likely going to pass. If it does, they're asking state leadership to make it illegal to give or to take MRNA vaccines in Florida."

At **0:44**, the video shows an animated graphic. A stylized microphone floats in the center, and then four documents appear horizontally arranged, each with blurred text and a red warning triangle at the top. The microphone then moves to hover over the third document.
A voiceover at **0:45** explains, "In this four-page letter, complete with footnotes, Brevard County Republicans cite sources which led them to a stunning conclusion."
At **0:54**, the voiceover continues, "Here it is, in their own words, government..." The video ends at **0:56** with the four documents displayed, before the voiceover can finish the sentence. The "COUNTY GOP: 'COVID VAX A BIOWEAPON?'" graphic remains on screen.

From 0:00 to 0:06, Hasan Bitmez is speaking with a strong, passionate voice. He says in Turkish, "Allah'ın gazabından kurtulamayacaksınız." (You will not escape God's wrath.) Immediately after he says "selamlıyorum" (I greet), at approximately 0:07, the camera cuts to a wide shot of the entire parliamentary assembly hall. The hall is grand, with many red seats arranged in a semicircle. Two large Turkish flags flank a dark wooden backdrop with the inscription "EGEMENLİK KAYITSIZ ŞARTSIZ MİLLETİNDİR" (SOVEREIGNTY BELONGS UNCONDITIONALLY TO THE NATION) on the wall.

As the wide shot appears, Hasan Bitmez, who is still standing at the podium in the center, suddenly sways backward. His head goes back, and he collapses to the floor behind the podium. There is a brief sound of the microphone being knocked, followed by a sudden increase in chatter and commotion from the other members of the assembly. Several individuals from the surrounding seats quickly rush towards the podium to assist him. Some people in the red seats stand up, looking concerned. One individual is seen actively bending down to help the fallen speaker, while others converge. The sound of a voice saying "Birleşime..." (To the session...) can be heard amidst the commotion at around 0:13. The scene remains chaotic as more people gather around the fallen speaker.

The video opens on a bright, sunny day with a man in an orange t-shirt and black shorts, standing on green grass, attempting to walk with the assistance of a quad cane. A second man, wearing a dark t-shirt and light-colored pants, stands closely behind him, providing physical support by holding a gait belt wrapped around the first man's waist. To the left, a black wheelchair is partially visible on the grass. In the background, there's a paved area with cars parked and a building with brick and dark siding. Trees line the background under a clear blue sky.

From **0:00 to 0:02**, the man in orange says, "They told us..."

From **0:02 to 0:08**, he continues, "that travel should be accountable to..." The man takes small, shuffling steps, shifting his weight. The helper behind him maintains a steady grip on the gait belt, providing stability.

At **0:08**, the man in orange says, "one of." He slowly lifts his right foot slightly, then places it back down, struggling to find balance.

From **0:10 to 0:15**, he repeats, "one of..." while continuing to try and shift his weight, but his feet remain largely in the same spot on the grass. The helper stays close, supporting him.

From **0:15 to 0:18**, the man says, "Like everything else." The helper leans in, appearing to offer verbal encouragement or instruction.

At **0:18**, the man says, "G-G-G-Jesus."

At **0:19**, the helper responds with a soft "Yeah."

From **0:20 to 0:22**, the man in orange says, "Yeah, this..." while making another small, wobbly movement.

From **0:22 to 0:24**, the man continues, "Makes it hard."

At **0:25**, the man says, "Man..."

From **0:27 to 0:29**, the man in orange says, "There just figure out one more way."

From **0:29 to 0:31**, the helper says, "One way to walk."

From **0:31 to 0:34**, the man takes a slightly larger step with his right foot, pulling his left foot forward to meet it. He uses the cane for support.

From **0:34 to 0:36**, the helper says, "Yep, good."

From **0:36 to 0:41**, the man in orange responds, "You know, what's locked right there, so I just put..." He struggles a bit with his balance, leaning on the cane, as the helper continues to support his back.

From **0:41 to 0:42**, the man says, "Nah, it is."

At **0:42**, the helper says, "Yep."

From **0:42 to 0:45**, the man slowly lifts his right foot and then his left, attempting to move forward.

From **0:45 to 0:49**, he takes a more confident step with his right foot and then his left, pushing the cane forward as well. He is moving away from the initial starting point towards the paved path.

From **0:49 to 0:50**, the man in orange says, "What about that?"

From **0:50 to 0:51**, the helper responds, "Yeah..."

From **0:51 to 0:54**, the helper continues, "You want to go on this?"

At **0:54**, the man says, "You don't mind?"

From **0:54 to 0:56**, the helper replies, "No, not at all."

From **0:56 to 0:57**, the helper adds, "I mean that's how we came up with..."

From **0:57 to 0:58**, the helper says, "to kind of challenge him on other stuff."

From **0:58 to 0:59**, the helper says, "All right, here we go." The man takes another small step.

From **0:59 to 1:02**, the helper says, "And we'll get some further in." The man continues to take very small, deliberate steps, aided by the cane and the helper's firm support.

From **1:02 to 1:07**, the helper says, "Actually, that's not... that's probably not the right." The man continues to slowly move his feet and the cane, showing great effort in each movement. The camera follows him as he makes his way closer to the building.

The video ends at **1:22** with the man still slowly walking on the grass, supported by the helper and the quad cane, with the building and shrubs in the background.

The video opens with a person's hands positioned above a black baking sheet containing several raw chicken breasts. To the left of the baking sheet is a bottle of blue Dawn dish soap. The kitchen counter features a white textured tile backsplash and a sink with a black faucet.

At 0:01, the person begins rubbing a white, creamy substance into the chicken breasts on the tray. Shortly after, at 0:03, they pick up the Dawn dish soap bottle and squirt a stream of blue soap directly onto one of the chicken breasts. The blue soap immediately lathers up, mixing with the white substance already on the chicken. The person continues to rub the chicken breasts, spreading the foamy blue and white mixture over them. Some pieces become completely covered in suds, while others still show hints of raw pink chicken.

At 0:05, the person lifts a suds-covered chicken breast, rubs it between their hands, and then places it back on the tray. They repeat this action with another chicken breast around 0:08, ensuring it's thoroughly lathered.

From 0:10 to 0:12, a sudsy chicken breast is held under a running faucet in the sink for a brief rinse before being returned to the tray. The person then resumes rubbing the remaining chicken pieces on the tray, distributing the soap and suds. This scrubbing and lathering process is repeated multiple times throughout the video.

At 0:22, another sudsy chicken breast is rinsed under the faucet. Following this, at 0:24, the person uses a paper towel to wipe their hands, dropping the soiled towel onto the soapy chicken on the tray.

Around 0:26, more blue Dawn dish soap is squirted onto the tray, which now contains a mix of chicken, suds, and wet paper towels. The person continues to rub the chicken vigorously, creating more foam. At 0:37, another chicken piece is rinsed under the faucet.

The cleaning process intensifies around 0:40 as the person starts picking up chicken pieces, some now partially wrapped in soap-soaked paper towels, and rinses them under the faucet. They place these rinsed pieces back onto the tray, which has even more paper towels mixed in.

At 0:45, a final generous amount of blue dish soap is squirted over the contents of the tray, and the person continues to rub and lather the chicken pieces in the foamy liquid. The video ends with the person rinsing a chicken breast under the faucet and placing it back onto the very soapy tray at 0:54, continuing to rub the chicken pieces in the suds.

Throughout the entire video, an upbeat electronic dance music track plays. The lyrics are repetitive and include phrases like "L.A. DJs just shake it down," "Baby girl," "We want Pop the Donkey, Donkey. We want that vibe," and "That's the way we like to go." The music is continuous and synchronized with the repetitive actions of scrubbing and lathering the chicken.

caption.video0:
The video showcases a man experiencing and then recovering from symptoms of dyskinesia, likely due to Parkinson's disease, after taking a single drop of an unknown substance.

The video begins at **0:00** with a medium close-up of a middle-aged man with graying hair, wearing a light blue polo shirt and cream-colored pants. He is sitting on a light-colored sofa in a brightly lit room. He is intently focused on his right hand, using his left hand to manipulate a small, dark object (possibly a tiny bottle or dispenser). He then brings his right hand to his mouth, demonstrating how to place the substance "under your tongue and rub it in your cheek," as he audibly explains. His facial expression shows concentration and a slight grimace as he rubs the substance, suggesting it might have a strong taste or sensation. He advises, "Don't do too much, you're gonna be asleep all afternoon," implying the substance has a calming or sedative effect.

At **0:15**, the camera pulls back to a wider shot, revealing the man sitting back on the sofa, looking pensive, with his left hand resting on his chin. Another person's hand (wearing a ring) is briefly visible in the bottom left, pointing. A text overlay "1:37 PM" appears on the screen. An off-screen male voice instructs, "You know what you should do? Don't try to communicate, just relax. See what happens." The man on the sofa appears to be following this advice, remaining still and quiet.

The scene cuts at **0:22**. The man is now lying stretched out on the sofa with his hands clasped behind his head. The time overlay still reads "1:41 PM", indicating that only a few minutes have passed. He slowly sits up, his movements appearing much smoother and more controlled compared to his initial agitated state.

At **0:28**, the man is sitting upright on the sofa, looking calm and composed. The off-screen male voice comments, "I think you're calmed down." The man on screen replies, "So quickly."

At **0:33**, a close-up shot of the man's hands is shown, resting on his lap. They are perfectly still, with no signs of tremor or involuntary movement. His fingers are slightly spread. The off-screen male voice asks, "Isn't amazing?" A female voice begins a voiceover, stating, "He used just a single drop."

The close-up of his hands continues at **0:37**. His hands are now slightly raised, palms down, with fingers slightly curled, as if posed. They remain remarkably steady. The female voiceover continues, "and his hands afterwards were rock steady. And the dyskinesia left." The man on screen then speaks, clearly and calmly, saying, "My voice is coming back."

Finally, at **0:44**, the video transitions to a split-screen comparison.
*   The left side, labeled "BEFORE," shows footage from earlier in the video, where the man is experiencing significant dyskinesia. He is seen swaying his upper body, making jerky, uncontrolled movements with his arms and hands, and appears distressed.
*   The right side, labeled "AFTER," shows the man sitting calmly and composed, looking directly at the camera with a relaxed expression. His body is still, and his hands are resting gently, demonstrating a complete cessation of the involuntary movements.
During this split-screen, the man's voice is heard again, saying, "It works most of the time. In fact, it's..." He then makes a long, clear, and sustained "Ahhh" sound, further illustrating his improved vocal control, as he moves his hands in a more fluid, controlled manner.

She has shoulder-length brown hair and is wearing a light brown blazer over a black turtleneck, accessorized with a gold chain necklace. A microphone is positioned on the desk in front of her. Behind her, a dark wooden wall is visible, along with a large New York State flag on the right, featuring the state seal with "EXCELSIOR" prominently displayed. Governor Hochul is speaking animatedly, using hand gestures to emphasize her points. Subtitles of her speech appear at the bottom of the screen.
*   **0:59-1:00:** The scene transitions to a solid background composed of three vertical color blocks: yellow on the far left, a bright blue in the center, and a dark navy blue on the far right.
*   **1:00-1:02:** The background changes to solid white. A thin blue outline of the state of New York appears, then quickly fills with the same bright blue color seen previously. Finally, the text "WE ARE NY" appears in white, with "NY" highlighted in yellow. Below this, "NY.GOV" is displayed in smaller white text.

Wade, the established law for 49 years that gave women rights, that my mother's generation had to fight for, that my generation took for granted, that are no longer there for my daughter's generation, after 49 years of having the established law of the nation overturned by this Supreme Court." - The full text of this statement appears on screen as she delivers it.

caption.video0:
The video opens with a distressed woman, identified as Brittany Watts, in a red turtleneck, covering her face with her hands. Another woman, wearing a blue and white plaid blazer and a yellow blouse, stands beside her, placing a comforting hand on her shoulder. A news chyron appears at the bottom of the screen, stating "WATTS' CASE BOUND OVER TO GRAND JURY" and "WARREN". The reporter's voice-over begins, "Brittany Watts overcome with emotion after learning her case is moving forward."

The scene transitions to a courtroom, showing a man in a light purple shirt and dark tie sitting at a desk, followed by a wider shot of the courtroom. The reporter continues, "Watts is charged with felony abuse of a corpse, accused of trying to plunge a toilet after having a miscarriage delivery at 22 weeks while using the restroom."

A police detective, identified as Det. Nick Carney from the Warren Police Dept. by a chyron, is shown speaking in the courtroom. He states, "She said she felt the baby come out and there was a a big splash."

The video then cuts back to Brittany Watts, seated at a table, looking somber. The reporter explains, "Investigators say they found a baby stuck in a toilet at Watts' home on September 22nd."

Next, a forensic pathologist, Dr. George Sterbenz, is seen speaking in the courtroom, identified by a chyron. The reporter states, "Forensic pathologist Dr. George Sterbenz testified an autopsy found no injury to the fetus, and that the unborn baby had died before passing through the birth canal." The reporter adds, "He says Watts' medical records showed she visited the hospital twice before the delivery."

Dr. George Sterbenz then speaks, explaining, "This fetus was going to be non-viable. And it was going to be non-viable because she had premature rupture of membranes. Her water had broken early. And the fetus was too young to be delivered."

The assistant prosecutor, Lewis Guarnieri, from the Warren Asst. Prosecutor's office, is then shown speaking at a podium. The reporter states, "Both the assistant prosecutor and Watts' attorney argued why this case should or should not move forward." Guarnieri explains the prosecution's view: "The issue isn't if how the child died, when the child died. It's the fact that a that the baby was put into a toilet, large enough to clog up the toilet, left in that toilet, and she went on her day."

Watts' defense attorney, Traci Timko, is then seen speaking at the podium. She argues, "This 33-year-old girl with no criminal record is demonized for something that goes on every day."

The reporter then states, "Judge Terry Ivanchak found probable cause to bind the case over." Judge Terry Ivanchak, of the Warren Municipal Court, is shown speaking from his bench. Text overlays his speech: "THERE ARE BETTER SCHOLARS THAN I AM TO DETERMINE THE EXACT LEGAL STATUS OF THIS... FETUS, CORPSE, BODY, BIRTHING TISSUE WHATEVER IT IS... MATTER OF FACT I'M ASSUMING THAT'S WHAT MOST OF THESE ISSUE 1'S ALL ABOUT AT WHAT POINT SOMETHING BECOMES VIABLE."

The video concludes with Brittany Watts crying and being comforted by two other individuals. The reporter signs off, "In Warren, Nadine Grimley, WKBN 27 First News." This is followed by a news channel graphic and then a news anchor, Lindsey Watson, who welcomes viewers and encourages them to subscribe to the WKBN 27 First News YouTube channel and download their app for breaking news alerts.

The video is a short social media clip (likely TikTok or Instagram Reel) demonstrating the electromagnetic fields (EMF) emitted by a charging phone and a person holding it.

Here's a detailed breakdown:

**Visuals:**
*   **0:00-0:02:** The video starts with a man holding an EMF meter (a small device with a green light and a speaker) near a white wall charger plugged into an outlet. Two young girls are sitting on a dark couch/bench behind him, watching. The room has wooden floors and a window in the background. Text overlays: "ITS HURTING US..." with two emojis (a surprised face with a brain on fire and an angry face), and "WALL" in blue text. A large white text box covers the lower part of the screen: "Still Going To Be Using Your Phone While Charging? 😨".
*   **0:02-0:07:** The man moves the EMF meter closer to the wall charger. The meter's green light begins to flash and it emits a loud beeping sound, indicating it's detecting power. The girls watch intently.
*   **0:07-0:14:** The man moves the meter away from the wall charger and holds it near a black smartphone that is not plugged in. The meter's light is off, and there is no sound. He moves his hand around the phone. Text overlay: "PHONE" then "IS" then "NOT READING ANY POWER ON IT."
*   **0:14-0:24:** The man plugs a charging cable into the phone. As soon as the phone starts charging (indicated by the screen lighting up briefly), he brings the EMF meter near the phone. The meter immediately starts flashing its green light and beeping loudly again. Text overlay: "HAS POWER RUNNING ALL THROUGH IT."
*   **0:24-0:32:** The man holds the EMF meter near the hand of the girl on the left, who is holding her own phone (which is presumably also charging, or simply "on"). The meter starts flashing and beeping when near her hand. Text overlay: "YOUR HAND."
*   **0:32-0:40:** The man asks the girl on the right to show her hand. She extends her hand towards him. He holds the EMF meter near her hand; it does not light up or beep. Text overlay: "OVER."
*   **0:40-0:49:** The man places a small, flat black object (possibly an EMF shield sticker) on the back of the girl's hand. He then holds the EMF meter near her hand, which is now holding the charging phone. The meter immediately starts flashing and beeping loudly, indicating it is detecting power. She appears to be trying to place the object on the phone, not just her hand. Text overlay: "YOUR HAND."
*   **0:49-0:54:** The meter continues to beep and flash as the girl (on the right) holds her charging phone near her other hand. She touches her chest, seemingly reacting to the perceived "power." The girl on the left watches, still holding her own phone. A new text overlay appears: "Check my profile to protect yourself using a EMF Shield on your cell phone 🛡️." The video ends with the meter still beeping.

**Audio:**
*   **0:00-0:02:** A man's voice says, "the wall charger plugged in there."
*   **0:02-0:06:** The EMF meter emits a loud, rapid beeping sound as the man holds it near the plugged-in wall charger. He says, "Reading power."
*   **0:07-0:13:** He moves the meter to the phone. The beeping stops. He says, "the phone here is not reading any power on it."
*   **0:14-0:24:** He plugs the phone in. The meter immediately starts beeping loudly again as he brings it near the charging phone. He says, "Oops... got power running all through it."
*   **0:24-0:31:** He brings the meter near the girl's hand while she is holding a phone. The meter beeps. He says, "Let's see if we can do it with your hand... see your hand... reading power."
*   **0:31-0:40:** He moves the meter to the second girl's hand, who is not holding a phone. The beeping stops. He asks, "No, hold on, hold on here. Let me see your hand. Try it over... okay, no, hold your hand."
*   **0:40-0:48:** He places something on her hand and then has her hold a phone. The meter starts beeping loudly again when near her hand. He says, "There, let go. Do it on your own."
*   **0:48-0:54:** The meter continues to beep. The girls react with smiles and a touch to the chest, as the man says, "Hey, let go. Watch her face." (It's unclear if "Watch her face" is directed at the camera or the other child). The beeping continues until the end of the video.

The video opens with two men seated in comfortable armchairs in a studio setting. The man on the left, Bill Maher, has gray hair and glasses, wearing a blue and white plaid shirt over a black t-shirt and dark pants. He is seated in a multicoloured armchair. The man on the right, John Cusack, has dark hair and is wearing a black collared shirt and black pants, seated in a purple armchair with a patterned cushion. Between them is a small, round table with various bottles, glasses, and a lamp. The background consists of a brick wall on the right and a framed picture on the left.

The conversation revolves around medical opinions and vaccines:

*   **00:00 - 00:05:** Bill Maher asks, "But then why are doctors wrong about so much?" John Cusack immediately responds, "Well, they're right about a lot too."
*   **00:05 - 00:09:** Maher continues, "And very often people who aren't doctors have been right about things."
*   **00:09 - 00:12:** Cusack asks for an example.

Like even if you had the disease, you still had to get a vaccine. That's powerful stupid, they don't do that in Europe." He gestures with his hand as he speaks.

*   **00:38 - 00:42:** Cusack emphatically states, "That was debunked though. I mean, that was... That's not debunked."
*   **00:42 - 00:49:** Cusack, gesturing with both hands, says, "Don't you know people who've had COVID four or five times? I do. That's not natural immunity. There's no immunity there. You've had COVID five times."

*   **00:54 - 01:02:** Maher, with an open hand gesture, says, "Uh, well, that's certainly nothing unscientific about the fact that the vaccine, which does weaken you in order to strengthen you..."
*   **01:02 - 01:06:** Cusack quickly agrees, "Says do all vaccines. Exactly. Yeah."
*   **01:06 - 01:19:** Maher continues, gesturing widely, "But while you're in a weakened state, yes. That's why so many people like me got it, did not have it while the thing was raging and I was taking zero precautions 'cause I was never that afraid of it." He briefly holds a pen or small object in his right hand.
*   **01:19 - 01:23:** Cusack points out, "But that's anecdotal. Like I've had every booster there is."
*   **01:23 - 01:27:** Maher nods as Cusack finishes his point, "And I have never had COVID. I get a flu shot once a year. I haven't had the flu in 20 years."
