*   **01:31 - 01:33:** Cusack, gesturing with both hands, says, "Okay, so this is all anecdotal. But but you know the difference between anecdotal and and statistical."
*   **01:33 - 01:43:** Maher nods, "I do. And I'm—" Cusack interrupts, "And statistically, you're still you still have a 90% better chance of not being hospitalized, not dying if you're vaccinated."
*   **01:43 - 02:02:** Maher vehemently disagrees, waving his hands, "Oh, okay. Well, that but that's assuming all people are alike and that is one of the giant fallacies in your way of thinking. We are not all alike. Yes, I would recommend and have recommended the COVID vaccine for the high-risk people. Like if you're 90 or fat, get it. Absolutely. You need it. Some people don't."
*   **02:02 - 02:06:** Cusack interjects, "But you had..." Maher continues, "Djokovic didn't need it. Aaron Rodgers didn't need it."
*   **02:06 - 02:12:** Cusack counters, "But you had something like 1900 kids who died of COVID during the pandemic. How many of them died from the vaccine? None."
*   **02:12 - 02:23:** Maher, leaning forward, states, "No, I don't think that's right. The New York—" Cusack interrupts, gesturing, "And and and the thinking there is that well, probably most kids are not gonna die from COVID. So why bother with the vaccine? Because it's only gonna be a few hundred kids, right?"
*   **02:23 - 02:28:** Maher shakes his head, "I don't know how many kids died. Um, that seems very high to me."
*   **02:28 - 02:41:** Maher, pointing, continues, "I know at one point the New York Times grossly—talking about misinformation—they had to apologize, they got it completely wrong. She reported something like 900,000 children have been hospitalized and it was like 63,000."
*   **02:41 - 02:46:** Maher continues, "So maybe those facts should be checked." Cusack interjects, "Papers can get it wrong and they print retractions and you know the drill."
*   **02:46 - 02:55:** Maher adds, "And also but also with COVID or from COVID? Did 1900 children die with COVID? Yes, that's a whole different story."
*   **02:55 - 03:13:** Cusack, gesturing broadly with his hands, explains, "Okay, but take 7 million people worldwide. 7 million people worldwide who died from COVID. Like if if even just a fraction of that is, and and it's probably a larger fraction who died of COVID than who died with COVID. Even if even just a fraction of that, that's still a lot of fucking people. That's a lot of fucking people."
*   **03:13 - 03:25:** Maher, gesturing emphatically with a pen, replies, "Yes, and but what? Okay, but some people, things are— and you cannot prevent that." Cusack says, "You can though. I mean you you can prevent."
*   **03:25 - 03:29:** Maher asks, "At what cost?" Cusack questions, "But what's the cost of getting a vaccine? What what's why is why is the downside?"
*   **03:29 - 03:37:** Maher, shaking his head and gesturing, asserts, "The fact that you don't you don't even have a clue what's the cost of getting a vaccine that you don't know the answer to that." Cusack makes a face.
*   **03:37 - 03:45:** Maher continues, "No, you you you you completely want to shut your eyes to the fact that there are repercussions to all medical interventions, including a vaccine."
*   **03:45 - 03:52:** Maher, gesturing, adds, "All vaccines they come, they say side effects just like every medication does. You can see it in the literature, they can't write it on the back, you know, on the vaccine."
*   **03:52 - 04:09:** Maher continues, "So you have to dig him. And and of course, there is a vaccine court because so many people have been injured. This is not a screed to say don't do vaccines. I'm not against doing vaccines. I'm against doing vaccines that I don't think I need. I should be able to decide that for myself, inside my body."
*   **04:09 - 04:13:** Maher says, "Uh, but yes, there are pas—"
*   **04:13 - 04:20:** Cusack interrupts, "But don't you don't you think that—" Maher responds, "would come along that I would fight you for, to get to the vaccine." Cusack nods, "Okay, but that's a good point, and you bring me to my next point. Don't don't you think that the, okay, but that's a good point."
*   **04:20 - 04:40:** Maher sips from a glass. Cusack continues, gesturing, "Don't don't you think that the the vitriol and the the just the the aggression towards the the uh that the anti-vaccination movement um the way it souped itself up during all this, that the next time there is a pandemic, our public health system is so weakened..."
*   **04:40 - 04:59:** Maher cuts in, "Yes, exactly because of them, because of them and their fucking misinformation that made people skeptical. The fact that everybody came out and said if you get the vaccine, you can you cannot get the disease, and then of course, that was wrong. And you and if you get the vaccine, you can't transmit it and that was wrong."
*   **04:59 - 05:07:** Cusack, gesturing, replies, "But they were learning right alongside us. They're not—you just said yourself, doctors are often wrong. They are, especially when they're researching a brand new disease." Maher says, "I understand. I'm not blaming them for it. I'm just saying that's why there shouldn't be the science. There's no 'the' in science."
*   **05:07 - 05:12:** Maher raises his finger, "There's no 'the' in science."
*   **05:12 - 05:15:** Maher, gesturing, says, "That's what you want. You want just to be the the one true opinion."
*   **05:15 - 05:22:** Cusack, shaking his head, "No, no, no, no. That's not true at all. No, the strength of science. The strength of science is that it has the capacity to evolve and prove it."
*   **05:22 - 05:29:** Maher interjects, "Just acknowledge that some that vaccines affect have ill effects on some people. I I—" Cusack affirms, "They do, of course."
*   **05:29 - 05:39:** Cusack, raising his hand, continues, "But but what statistically, it's here and it's here. It's like it's it's people, how many? 7 million people died of COVID. How many people died from the vaccine? Probably a few. I don't know what the number is, but I know it's not 7 million."
*   **05:39 - 05:46:** Maher, pointing with his pen, states, "Well, okay, but what you don't even— If we were more judicious about who needed it versus who it might hurt..."
*   **05:46 - 05:58:** Maher, gesturing emphatically, continues, "Which would not be the blanket policy of just everybody, one size fits all, then probably we could take down the damages from the vaccine that happened."
*   **05:58 - 06:03:** Cusack argues, "Then why vaccinate for anything? Why not why not why vaccinate for measles? Why not why vaccinate for mumps or rubella for pertussis?"
*   **06:03 - 06:11:** Cusack, gesturing, asks, "Why why vaccinate for any of those things? Because somebody might get hurt by the vaccine? I mean what why do those vaccines?"

*   **06:30 - 06:42:** Maher, tapping his pen on the armrest, concludes, "Okay, well, I, just so you know, I took one for the team. Okay? So, so, you know, that should go into it. I did something I did not want to do. And we don't have to talk about this all the."

The video ends abruptly as Maher is speaking.

A skeletal hand emerges from the left, holding two colorful vape pens (one green, one orange). Large yellow text appears in the black void, exclaiming "LIES!" The WHO European Region logo is still in the top left. The sound of a tearing paper effect accompanies this transition.

From 0:28 to 0:34, Karina Mocanu reappears, speaking from the same park setting. She states: "For too long, the tobacco industry has preyed upon our youth, jeopardizing our health, our future and our very voices."

From 0:35 to 0:42, she continues: "At ENSP Next, we strive to empower young people to raise their voice and stand against the tobacco industry's manipulation."

From 0:42 to 0:49, Karina Mocanu still speaks: "I envision a future where every young person has the opportunity to thrive free from the shackles of addiction and exploitation."

From 0:50 to 0:59, she concludes: "For this year's World No Tobacco Day, join the movement and help us expose Big Tobacco. Our fight is for the youth, by the youth."

The video then transitions back to the blurred colorful background from 0:59 to 1:06. White arrows move from left to right across the screen. The WHO European Region logo appears again, followed by the text ">> RACE to the FINISH". The music swells and then fades out as the video ends.

summary: Claims the H5N1 bird flu has been manipulated in a lab is false and baseless. The H5N1 bird flu, also known as avian influenza, is a naturally occurring virus. It was first identified in birds in China in 1996.    https://www.yalemedicine.org/news/h5n1-bird-flu-what-to-know    https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9947427/
tweetText:
Historically Avian flu is not easily transmitted between humans and Dr Kelly Victory is certain the only way that changes is if it has been manipulated in a lab. 

She is appalled by the unnecessary culling of millions of birds/cattle for a problem that can be solved by allowing for the natural process of herd immunity.

Not to mention a dire warning against the dangers of mass vaccination which will only drive mutation, increase the resistance of the virus and cause more carnage.

Have we learnt nothing 🙄

caption.video0:
The video features a woman with blonde hair, wearing an orange top and a necklace, speaking directly to the camera. Her background appears to be a digital rendering of a city skyline, viewed through large windows. Text overlay "clip Kat A" is visible in the bottom right corner.

Here's a detailed breakdown of the video's content:

- **00:00 - 00:19**: The blonde woman is speaking. Her expression is serious and her mouth is open as she talks. She gestures slightly with her head and hands. The cityscape background remains constant.
  - **Audio**: "So, while there's been a relatively high mortality rate, very little human-to-human transmission. So, should we start to see significant human-to-human transmission with this most recent outbreak, I can pretty much guarantee that it's because it was manipulated in the laboratory."

- **00:19 - 00:25**: The scene changes to a person in a white lab coat walking down a corridor in what looks like a sterile industrial facility or a large laboratory. Large metal tanks and pipes line the walls. Other people in lab coats are visible in the background, further down the corridor.
  - **Audio**: "Robert Redfield, uh, said just the other day that it would take almost, you know, minimal, very minimal manipulation of the virus, H5N1."

- **00:25 - 00:39**: The scene transitions to two scientists in white hazmat suits, gloves, and face masks working in a laboratory. They are looking at samples in test tubes and beakers, one is using a pipette, and the other is interacting with a computer screen displaying what looks like a brain scan or a biological model. Various laboratory equipment like microscopes and beakers with colorful liquids are on the table.
  - **Audio**: "I think four total change, changes to the genome that would make it fundamentally able to be transmitted very easily between humans. And that's something that's extremely dangerous."

- **00:39 - 00:50**: A close-up shot of a scientist in a hazmat suit and gloves, looking into a microscope. The background is blurred but shows lab equipment and a blue image on a monitor.
  - **Audio**: "There's a good reason why people have pushed back on gain-of-function research. They always use the excuse that it's to make us more prepared, to make us, you know, ready, um, with an antidote or a vaccine."

- **00:50 - 01:12**: A gloved hand (wearing a white watch) is seen using a pipette to transfer a clear liquid from one test tube to another. A rack of test tubes is visible, along with other lab equipment and red-capped bottles in the blurred background.
  - **Audio**: "The reality is, this is very dangerous research because not only can something get out of a lab by nefarious intent, meaning a purposeful spreading of it, but simply by abject incompetence or, you know, people makes mistakes and lab errors happen. They happen far more often than people want to believe."

- **01:12 - 02:14**: The blonde woman is back on screen, speaking directly to the camera with the city skyline background. Her expression remains serious.
  - **Audio**: "There are over 100 serious lab errors, lab leaks reported every single year. So, the idea that someone is weaponizing or manipulating a virus in the laboratory to make it stronger, more transmissible, more capable of causing serious disease, more resistant to treatment is frankly unconscionable. If bird flu should become something to be concerned about, if it should begin to be transmitted between human beings and between people who are not in direct contact with infected animals, then hopefully we will be allowed to use the medications that we should be able to use, like hydroxychloroquine. If instead, they shut those things down in order to foist their mRNA vaccine, quote unquote, agenda on us, uh, then it's going to be up to people to push back and say, 'No, we fell for that the last time, and we're not falling for it again.'"

- **02:14 - 02:16**: The screen splits into two. The blonde woman is on the right, continuing to speak, while a woman with dark hair and glasses is on the left, looking down and listening. A microphone is in front of her. The background for both is blurred.
  - **Audio**: "Yes. Furthermore, you can expect them to use this bird flu scare and that's what it is, it's a fear tactic."

- **02:16 - 03:10**: The blonde woman is again the sole speaker on screen, with the cityscape background.
  - **Audio**: "to unnecessarily put pressure on the food system, meaning they are unnecessarily culling, exterminating millions and millions of poultry birds and cattle, uh, because these animals have, quote, tested positive for bird flu. This is unnecessary. They should allow the virus to move its way through the herds, through the flocks, some number of animals will die, the rest will develop natural immunity and the thing will be extinguished on its own like most viral pandemics are. If instead, they move forward with these mass vaccination programs, as I said, they will drive viral mutation, they will increase the resistance of the virus and they will cause more carnage."

tweetText:
Riley Gaines says her husband can't get citizenship or a green card because he's not vaccinated against COVID.

https://t.co/Cd8JkY30wB

caption.video0:
The video opens at 00:00 with a blonde woman with her hair in a messy bun, wearing a light grey sweatshirt with red letters that appear to spell "VOTERS" or "OTEA" (upside down). She is seated indoors at a wooden table, in what looks like a living room with exposed wooden ceiling beams and a stone fireplace with a mounted deer head above it. A black ceiling fan is visible in the background. She holds up a white piece of paper, which is an official-looking document with text on it, then brings it down and looks at the camera.

As she says, "Just got another letter from immigration services" (0:00-0:02), she gestures with the paper and her hand. She then places her hand on her forehead in a gesture of frustration and begins speaking about her husband's immigration process (0:03-0:05).

From 0:05 to 0:15, she explains her background and her husband's: "I've made a video about this before, but I'm a US citizen, born and raised in Tennessee, went to college at University of Kentucky, met my husband there, he's an immigrant, he moved over to the US for college on an athletic scholarship from England." During this, she gestures with her hands, palms up, emphasizing her points.

At 0:16, she holds up two fingers, then three, then four as she states, "We've been married almost three years now. We're coming up on three years." She claps her hands together at 0:21 and continues, "So you would think he is well on his way to achieving his citizenship or at least having his green card, right?"

At 0:27, she shakes her head and dramatically waves her right hand to signify "No," stating, "No, wrong." She then explains why he hasn't received his green card (0:29-0:36): "And why can't he get his green card? Because his medical records aren't complete. And what is missing from his medical record? The COVID vaccine."

Looking frustrated, she exclaims at 0:37, "Like, it is the year 2025 and we are still requiring the COVID vaccine." At 0:41, she abruptly grabs the letter and pulls it closer to the camera.

From 0:43 to 0:50, the camera zooms in on the letter, which is held upside down by her red-manicured fingers. She points to a line of text on the paper. The text is mirrored, but she reads it aloud: "Complete the COVID-19 vaccine." She then moves the paper away and looks directly at the camera.

From 0:53 to 0:57, she says, "But this is what they do. This is what they do to people who do it the right way," while touching her forehead and making expressive hand gestures. A text overlay appears on screen: "more than $10,000" (0:59-1:01), as she states, "I can't even tell you how much money, thousands of dollars, we have spent on the different forms and applications and and immigration lawyers and all of the different things" (0:57-1:07).

She continues to express her exasperation, saying, "All for everything to expire on their time anyways" (1:08-1:11). She then addresses potential comments (1:12-1:25): "And for all you people who say, 'Oh, Riley, your husband's here illegally, he should be deported,' just so you don't understand immigration. He is very much here legally. There are other ways to obtain residency than a green card like visas or advanced parole."

She then turns to politics (1:26-1:43): "Or those of you who say, 'This is what you get when you voted for Donald Trump.' Obviously, my husband can't vote, but if he could, he would have voted for Donald Trump. Change is coming with the current administration, but he has to undo everything that the former administration has done to our immigration system, both legally and illegally."

She finishes her rant with a sarcastic remark about a political slogan (1:44-1:50): "Oh, and when they campaigned on the tagline, 'My body, my choice,' this is proof they never really meant that." She shakes her head and nods subtly.

The video then transitions to the TikTok logo and search bar (1:50-1:55).

summary: The video is satire made by producer Heydon Prowse @HeydonProwse, in order to demonstrate how easy it is to start a conspiracy theory. The circuit board is from an old set-top TV box, and the "COV-19" marking was added to create a hoax. He explains himself how he created it:  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=98pY07HaNgs  https://www.thejournal.ie/debunked-viral-video-5g-circuit-board-false-covid-19-5116094-Jun2020/
tweetText:
5G Engineer comes across a curious bit of Circuitry he’s been employed to install in one of the towers.

Millions upon millions of 5G Masts were erected in every corner of the World simultaneously at the start of Covid.

Clearly it’s not to provide you with better internet, question is what for then?

caption.video0:
The video is presented as a split screen, with text on a black background on the left and video footage on the right. A small, animated green virus icon is consistently present in the bottom right corner of the video portion.

Here's a detailed breakdown:

**00:00 - 00:03**
*   **Visual (Left):** White text reads: "5G Installer says: 'I don't know the best way to do this...but I've been working...connecting 5G masts on towers like the one behind me for the past few weeks while everybody else has been in lockdown.'"
*   **Visual (Right):** A man wearing a yellow hard hat and a white face mask (appearing to be an N95 respirator) is looking upwards. A tall metal lattice tower, consistent with a mobile phone mast (possibly 5G), is visible behind him in the distance against a cloudy sky.
*   **Audio:** The man speaks with a distinct accent, saying, "...the past few weeks while everybody else has been in lockdown." This audio matches the last line of text on the left, and also appears as a caption at the bottom of the screen.

**00:03 - 00:09**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text changes to: "'And we don't crack open these kits, because we are explicitly asked not to.'"
*   **Visual (Right):** The man remains in the same pose, looking upwards at the tower.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "And we don't crack open these kits because we're explicitly asked not, not to." The audio matches the on-screen text and captions.

**00:09 - 00:14**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text changes to: "'But...the best...perhaps, the best thing is for me to show you.'"
*   **Visual (Right):** The man remains in the same pose.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "Well, best, perhaps the best thing is for me to show you." The audio matches the on-screen text and captions.

**00:14 - 00:17**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text changes to: "'It's best if I just show you. Look...'"
*   **Visual (Right):** The video quickly cuts from the man to a close-up shot of a blue circuit board, covered in small electronic components and some dust or debris. A finger points towards the board.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "It's best if I just show you, look." The audio matches the on-screen text and captions.

**00:17 - 00:20**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text area is black.
*   **Visual (Right):** The video cuts to an extreme close-up of a clear plastic bag. Inside the bag is a small, dark, irregularly shaped object. The words "COV-19" are clearly printed in black on the outside of the plastic bag.
*   **Audio:** No audible speech.

**00:20 - 00:22**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text area is black.
*   **Visual (Right):** The video cuts back to the circuit board, with a hand holding it.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "There's a piece of 5G kit..." (Caption appears: "There's a piece of 5 G kit and this has COVID-19 on it.")

**00:22 - 00:25**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text changes to: "'It's a piece of [explicit] 5G kit. It says COV-19 on it.'"
*   **Visual (Right):** The video quickly cuts between the circuit board, the plastic bag with "COV-19" on it, and back to the circuit board. The "COV-19" text is *only* visible on the plastic bag, not on the circuit board itself.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "It's a piece of fucking 5G kit and this has COVID-19 on it." The audio explicitly uses the word "fucking." The on-screen text uses "[explicit]" and the bottom caption displays "a piece of 5 G kit and this has COVID-19 on it."

**00:25 - 00:36**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text changes to: "'I don't really know of any product produced by any company that produces circuitry like this. That has the brand name COV-19 on it. But that's what is says on the [explicit] circuit board.'"
*   **Visual (Right):** The close-up of the circuit board continues, held by the man's hand.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "I don't really know of any products produced by any company that produces circuitry like this, that that has the brand name COV-19, but that's what it says on the fucking circuit board." Again, the explicit word "fucking" is used and captured in the on-screen text as "[explicit]" while the bottom caption omits it. The claim that "COV-19" is on the circuit board is audibly made, but visually, it's only seen on the separate plastic bag.

**00:36 - 00:43**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text changes to: "'I'm not a [explicit] conspiracy theorist. Obviously, I've read all that stuff online about coronavirus and COVID-19. But...'"
*   **Visual (Right):** The video shows the circuit board briefly, then quickly cuts to the plastic bag with "COV-19" inside it, and then back to the circuit board.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "Not, not a fucking conspiracy theorist. Obviously, I read all that stuff online about coronavirus and COVID-19, but..." The explicit word "fucking" is used, similar to previous instances in the captions.

**00:43 - 00:52**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text changes to: "'Why the [explicit] are they putting circuitry like that in towers like that. I just don't [explicit] understand.'" A watermark "@_TRADWILL" appears in the bottom left corner, remaining for the rest of the video.
*   **Visual (Right):** The video quickly pans from the close-up of the circuit board upwards, revealing the large metal lattice tower in the distance. It then continues to pan horizontally, showing a street lined with residential houses and parked cars. The street appears quiet. The camera then pans back to the close-up of the circuit board.
*   **Audio:** The man says, "Why the fuck are they, why the fuck are they putting circuitry like that in towers like that? I just don't fucking understand." The explicit words are audible. The on-screen text uses "[explicit]" and the bottom caption omits them, ending with "Just understand."

**00:52 - 00:54**
*   **Visual (Left):** The text area is black.
*   **Visual (Right):** The video remains on the close-up of the circuit board.
*   **Audio:** The audio ends with the man's final statement, "understand."

The video implies a conspiracy theory suggesting that 5G equipment contains "COVID-19" or elements related to it, linking the installation of 5G infrastructure during lockdown to the pandemic. The "COV-19" branding is visually shown only on a small plastic bag, not on the circuit board itself, though the speaker claims it's on the board.

The video opens with Amy Kelly, CEO of Daily Clout and Program Director of the WarRoom Daily Clout Pfizer Documents Analysis Program, introducing herself. She is seen in a video call format, wearing a red top and a multi-strand beaded necklace, with curly blonde hair and a blurred background.

At [00:00:10], the video transitions to a screenshot of the DAILYCLOUT website. The main headline reads: "Pfizer's New Two-in-One COVID-19 Booster: Are We the Clinical Trial?". Below this, there are several article thumbnails. One is titled "Ob/Gyn Warns of Possible Links between mRNA Injections, Miscarriages and Malformations," another "Seeking Potential Plaintiffs in Legal Action Against Pfizer," and a third "Majority of Voters: President Who Promised to Unite the Country Has Instead Divided It." On the right, a red box highlights "Read The Latest Pfizer Reports," and "Featured Campaigns" lists "Pfizer Documents Analysis - WarRoom." Amy Kelly states she is following up on a previous report about "COVID vaccine harms to sperm and testes."

The video then returns to Amy Kelly speaking at [00:00:17]. She announces her intention to discuss "harms to the penis and its functions." She mentions that her research on this topic has been "very disturbing." She specifically notes that one of the adverse events related to the penis that appeared in the results is "general penis injury," though the documents don't provide further detail on this particular entry.

At [00:00:45], the video displays a table from a Pfizer document titled "16.2.7.4.4 Listing of Adverse Events - All Subjects." One row in the table, corresponding to a subject aged "16-55," lists "Penis injury/PENIS INJURY" under the "Preferred Term/AE Investigator Text" column, classified under "INJ&P" (Injury and Poisoning) for "System Organ Class." This event occurred after "Dose No. 2" on "15SEP2020." Amy Kelly emphasizes that other adverse events in the documents are "quite concerning."

Back to Amy Kelly at [00:00:52], she highlights "penile vein thrombosis," which she clarifies is a blood clot. She remarks that blood clots are always a "bad thing to have going on anywhere in your body" and are "particularly concerning in that area." She states that this condition is "quite rare" and is also referred to as "Mondor's disease," which she spells out: "M-O-N-D-O-R apostrophe S."

At [00:01:18], the video shows a Wikipedia page for "Mondor's disease." The definition reads: "Mondor's disease is a rare condition which involves thrombophlebitis of the superficial veins of the breast and anterior chest wall. It sometimes occurs in the arm or **penis**." The word "penis" is highlighted in blue. Amy Kelly explains that Mondor's disease is "very painful" and reiterates that it appeared in the Pfizer documentation as an adverse event.

The video cuts back to Amy Kelly at [00:01:25]. She then discusses "penile neoplasm," defining a neoplasm as a "malignant lesion." She clarifies that it can appear on the foreskin, the end of the penis, or the shaft. She also mentions "penile intraepithelial neoplasm," which she describes as rare and a "pre-cancerous disease of the outer skin layer or the epidermis of the penis."

At [00:01:55], the video returns to a Pfizer document, specifically "16.2.7.4.4 Listing of Adverse Events - All Subjects ≥16 Years of Age." A highlighted row shows "Penile neoplasm/Penile Intra Epithelial Neoplasia" under "Preferred Term/AE Investigator Text" and "NEOPL" (Neoplasm) under "System Organ Class." This event occurred after "Dose No. 2" on "06NOV2020," with a "Grade 3" toxicity. Amy Kelly expresses concern about neoplasms being listed as an adverse event.

Back to Amy Kelly at [00:02:02], she continues by mentioning "penile squamous cell carcinoma." She notes that this is "quite uncommon in the US typically," but is now "being seen as an adverse event of the COVID mRNA vaccines."

At [00:02:29], the video shows another section of the Pfizer document. A row is highlighted, indicating "Penile squamous cell carcinoma/PENILE INVASIVE MODERATELY DIFFERENTIAL KERATINIZING SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA" under "Preferred Term/AE Investigator Text," classified under "NEOPL." This event also occurred after "Dose No. 2" on "06NOV2020" with "Grade 3" toxicity. Amy Kelly states that this type of carcinoma is typically seen more in the "underdeveloped world." She expresses concern that if it goes "undetected for a while, it can spread to the lymph system and then start circulating throughout the body," emphasizing that "early detection is key."

Returning to Amy Kelly at [00:02:36], she mentions "penile infection" as another adverse event found in the Pfizer documents. She explains that this "can mean a lot of things," including a "yeast or candida infection."

At [00:03:03], the video again displays a Pfizer document, this time titled "16.2.7.5 Listing of Serious Adverse Events - All Subjects ≥16 Years of Age." A row is highlighted, showing "Penile infection/Penile infection" under "Preferred Term/AE Investigator Text" and "INFEC" (Infection) under "System Organ Class." This event occurred after "Dose No. 2" on "22JAN2021," with a duration of "130/C" days and "Grade 3" toxicity. Amy Kelly points out that Candida infections are a "quite common adverse event" in the Pfizer documents and seem to "explode... in the human body after mRNA vaccines."

Finally, the video shows Amy Kelly speaking again from [00:03:10] to the end. She lists the symptoms of a penile infection: "ulcers, rashes, sores, pain, itching, swelling, enlarged lymph nodes and urinary frequency." She reiterates that such infections are "quite dangerous" if left untreated and need to be "addressed quickly." She concludes by stating that "these harms were known during the clinical trials and they were not brought to the attention of people before the vaccines were rolled out and thus once again, men are not able to make fully informed consent based on this knowledge when they're going to decide whether they want to get an mRNA vaccine."

summary: COVID-19 Mortality Risk Correlates Inversely with Vitamin D3 Status, and a Mortality Rate Close to Zero Could Theoretically Be Achieved at 50 ng/mL 25(OH)D3    https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8541492/
tweetText:
Dr. Pierre Kory: Big Pharma is ‘TERRIFIED’ of Vitamin D

Why?

Because “It threatens the DISEASE MODEL.”

A new meta-analysis out of Italy, published in the journal, Nutrients, has unearthed some shocking data about Vitamin D.

Looking at data from 16 different studies and 1.26 million individuals, the meta-analysis revealed:

• Vitamin D showed about a 60% effectiveness against the incidence of COVID-19 in randomized control trials.

• Vitamin D showed about 40-50% effectiveness in reducing the incidence of COVID-19 in observational studies.

• For preventing severe COVID-19 cases requiring ICU care, vitamin D supplementation was about 70% effective.

So, we didn’t need to lock ourselves inside for years, be afraid, and vilify our neighbors for not wearing a mask.

All we needed to do was to go outside, get sunshine, and increase our vitamin D levels, and everything would have been fine.

The video features a man with grey hair, wearing a dark suit and a blue tie, standing behind a podium and speaking to an unseen audience. The background is a plain light blue wall with two circular elements that appear to be part of a logo or design, possibly related to a "WORLD HEALTH SUMMIT," as partial text "WORLD HEALTH SUMMIT" is visible on the podium.

Throughout the video, two text overlays are consistently present:
- In the top left corner, a white hat icon with the word "HATS" below it.
- In the top right corner, "Traduzione e sottotitoli: twitter.com/miia_2018".

Additionally, a watermark "InShot" is visible in the bottom right corner of the video.

Here's a detailed breakdown of the video content:

**00:00 - 00:04**
**Visual:** The speaker gestures with his right hand as he begins to speak.
**Audio:** "...the uh, the mRNA vaccines are an example for that cell and gene therapy."
**Subtitles (Italian):** "In definitiva, i vaccini mRNA sono un esempio di quella terapia cellulare e genica." (Ultimately, mRNA vaccines are an example of that cell and gene therapy.)

**00:04 - 00:07**
**Visual:** The speaker lowers his hand and maintains a steady posture.
**Audio:** "I always like to say, if we had surveyed two years ago..."
**Subtitles (Italian):** "Come amo sempre dire, se due anni fa avessimo fatto un sondaggio pubblico:" (As I always like to say, if two years ago we had done a public survey:)

**00:07 - 00:12**
**Visual:** The speaker continues to speak, occasionally nodding slightly.
**Audio:** "...in the public, would you be willing to take a gene, gene or cell therapy and inject it into your body..."
**Subtitles (Italian):** ""Sareste disposti a fare iniettare nel vostro corpo una terapia genica o cellulare?"" ("Would you be willing to have a gene or cell therapy injected into your body?")

**00:12 - 00:16**
**Visual:** The speaker maintains the same posture, speaking calmly.
**Audio:** "...we would have probably had a 95% refusal rate."
**Subtitles (Italian):** "Avremmo probabilmente avuto un tasso di rifiuto del 95%." (We would probably have had a 95% refusal rate.)

**00:16 - 00:27**
**Visual:** The speaker looks down momentarily at the podium, then back up, maintaining a consistent posture and expression.
**Audio:** "I think this pandemic has also opened many people's eyes to to innovation in a way that was maybe not possible before."
**Subtitles (Italian):** "Penso che questa pandemia abbia anche allargato la visuale sull'innovazione a molte persone, in una modalità che forse non sarebbe stata possibile prima." (I think this pandemic has also broadened many people's view on innovation, in a way that perhaps was not possible before.)

The video concludes with the speaker still standing at the podium. The Italian subtitles precisely match the English audio, providing a direct translation of the speaker's words.

tweetText:
Thursday was National PTSD Awareness Day, and you’ve got to hear this. 

First off, psychedelic treatments show an approximately 80% remission rate for PTSD. That’s huge. Saves lives and money. That's why I passed a law for the Department of Defense to conduct clinical trials on these therapies. The data continues to come in and it’s overwhelmingly positive. 

But now we’re hiting a roadblock. An external advisory board, influenced by ICER, a think tank, rejected the therapy proposal. These technocrats think they know better than scientists. Their job is to say NO and support the status quo that makes Big Healthcare plenty of money, let’s be honest.

Worse, groups like Psymposia, which have insulted our veterans, oppose this therapy too. They think psychedelic treatment for vets “perpetuates white supremacism.”

But there's hope. These groups aren’t the FDA. The FDA hasn’t actually made the decision yet. Even if they deny this application, there are many more coming and   because of my legislation the DoD has to do clinical trials on these therapies. We must support the science and push past these activist groups.

Let's save lives.

We actually have huge contradictory evidence that high LDL-C is causal factor in ASCVD.    https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/CIRCULATIONAHA.122.061010
tweetText:
🚨🚨🚨🚨🚨🚨🚨🚨
BREAKING: @BudoffMd has now presented the matched analysis for: 
KETO (#LMHRstudy) vs Control (#MiHeart)
-🙏Please RT🙏-

METHODS – 80 Participants of #LMHRstudy fell within #MiHeart age range and were then matched 1:1 for age, gender, race, diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, hypertension, and past smoking to asymptomatic subjects from the #MiHeart cohort.

PRIMARY ANALYSIS – High resolution heart scans (#CCTA) allowing for primary analysis of Total Plaque Score (TPS), Total Stenosis Score (TSS) and Segment Involvement Score (SIS)

RESULTS 
The matched mean age was 55.5 years, with mean #LDL cholesterol of 272 mg/dL (max LDL-C 591) mg/dl and mean 4.7 years duration on a ketogenic diet.

🚨 There was no significant difference in coronary plaque burden of #LMHRstudy (mean LDL-C 272) cohort as compared to #MiHeart controls (mean LDL 123 mg/dl); nb: pre-KETO LDL-C in KETO group was 122 mg/dl

 🚨 There was no significant difference in CAC (median and IQR) [0 (0,56)] versus [1 (0, 49)], p = 0.520

🚨 No relationship of LDL-C elevations and plaque

⚠️  This analysis is on baseline scans and the study is still ongoing for second CCTA completion in our cohort by February of 2024. And as always, please continue to work with your doctor.

- I will have my own video reaction and thoughts in the coming hours. 

🙏🙏🙏 to everyone -- and I mean everyone -- who helped us get to this pivotal milestone. 🙏🙏🙏

caption.video0:
The video features a speaker, presumably Dr. Matthew Budoff, presenting research findings on a ketogenic diet and its impact on lipid profiles and atherosclerosis. The presentation is structured with title slides, objective slides, methods, results, proposed mechanisms, and conclusions, followed by a Q&A.

Here's a detailed breakdown:

**00:00 - 00:16: Introduction and Transition**
The video opens with a blue screen displaying "THANK YOU!" in large white letters within an orange rectangle.
At 0:01, the speaker (Dr. Matthew Budoff) is partially visible on the right side of the screen, smiling and looking down. He then straightens up and looks towards the audience.
At 0:06, the speaker begins speaking: "Okay, to move right along, second presentation is on Carbohydrate restriction-induced elevations in LDL-cholesterol and atherosclerosis, The KETO trial, to be presented by Professor Matt Budoff and his colleagues, the Harbor-UCLA Medical Center locally here in Torrance, California."
At 0:13, the screen momentarily switches to a Windows file explorer window, showing a list of PowerPoint files. The speaker quickly navigates back to the presentation.
At 0:16, the presentation reloads, and the title slide appears.

**00:16 - 00:34: Title Slide - The KETO Trial**
The slide is titled "KETOGENIC DIET" in a small framed image at the top. Below, it reads: "Carbohydrate restriction-induced elevations in LDL-cholesterol and atherosclerosis: The KETO Trial" with the clinical trial identifier "NCT05733325".
The speaker's name "Matthew Budoff MD" is listed as "Professor of Medicine, David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA, Lundquist Institute, Torrance CA, USA". Co-authors and affiliations (The Lundquist Institute and Harbor-UCLA Medical Center) are also shown.
The speaker (Dr. Budoff) is visible on the right, wearing a blue suit and striped tie, speaking into a microphone at a podium.
At 0:24, he says, "Thank you very much. It's certainly a pleasure to be here with everybody."
At 0:28, he gestures to the screen and continues: "Um, so a very, I think, a very interesting trial that I was involved in. I see my co-authors here."

**00:34 - 00:47: Disclosures/Funding**
The slide is titled "DISCLOSURES/FUNDING".
Bullet points state:
- "No author has conflicts of interest to declare in reference to this work"
- "Funding provided by Citizen Science Foundation"
The speaker gestures towards the slide as he reads the points.
At 0:38, he adds: "Um, so I, I have no conflicts of interest with uh, with anything related to this and, uh, the funding was has been provided by a a Citizen Science Foundation, more of a a crowd-funding organization."

**00:47 - 1:45: Quantifying Atherosclerosis with CCTA**
The slide is titled: "Quantifying atherosclerosis with CCTA is becoming more robust and reproducible with defined measures". The bottom left corner has a "Weill Cornell Medicine" logo, and the bottom right states "SCCT Guidelines on Atherosclerosis JCCT 2020".
The speaker explains the advancements in using CCTA for atherosclerosis.
At 0:58, an image box titled "Stenosis Severity" appears on the left, showing a coronary artery with highlighted areas of narrowing.
At 1:04, another image box titled "Number Lesions >30%" appears below it, illustrating multiple plaques.
At 1:10, an image box titled "Non-calcified Plaque Volume" appears in the middle, showing cross-sections of a vessel with plaque and measurements.
At 1:17, an image box titled "Lumen Volume" appears below, displaying a 3D reconstruction of a coronary artery.
At 1:22, an image box titled "High Risk Plaque" appears on the right, showing a longitudinal view of a vessel with different types of plaque highlighted.
The speaker elaborates on each measure: "So, when we talk about using CT Angiography to look at atherosclerosis, there's actually quite great data as far as how robust it is and reproducible it is. We can look at stenosis severity and find patients non-invasively who have significant stenosis. We can look at the number of lesions and see how many different plaques are present. We can look at plaque volume, which is going to be more pertinent to pertinent to what we're talking about tonight. We can look at lumen volume and how much uh how big the the arteries are. And, of course, uh, look for things like high-risk plaques. And, uh, using this this modality to track atherosclerosis is gonna be uh is been becoming very robust. And I don't think there's a large pharmaceutical company today that's not doing a trial using serial CT Angiography to see if their drug has an effect on atherosclerosis."

**1:45 - 3:32: Objectives**
The slide is titled "Objectives".
Bullet points explain the study's rationale:
- "While most individuals who adopt carbohydrate-restricted ketogenic diets for obesity or diabetes exhibit general improvements in all lipid parameters (LDL-C, HDL-C and TG), a subgroup termed **Lean-Mass Hyper-Responders (LMHRs)** – exhibit extreme increases in LDL-C."
- "LMHR are defined by LDL-C in the FH range, along with high HDL-C and low TG; this triad tends to occur in lean otherwise healthy individuals."
- "The aim of this study is to: prospectively evaluate the plaque progression over the course of 1-year in 100 metabolically healthy LMHR and near-LMHR subjects on a ketogenic diet with LDL-C ≥190 mg/dl."
- "We present the baseline characteristics of the cohort with matching to subjects from The Miami Heart Study (MiHeart), a population-based study of atherosclerosis with participants undergoing CCTA."
The speaker details the objectives: "So we undertook this study to look at um this interesting population of patients who go on a carbohydrate-restricted ketogenic diet. Um, and end up with uh very high LDL cholesterol. And it's not typical, there's many patients with obesity or diabetes who have uh uh restrict their carbohydrates, they go on a ketogenic diet, and their LDL gets better, their HDL and triglycerides get better. But there's a subgroup who are very lean who have a extreme increase in LDL cholesterol. And I'll talk about the the mechanism or the proposed mechanism in a moment. But these patients are are uh so what we we take these patients who are non uh that non familial hypercholesterolemia. We had genetic testing to prove that they had a normal LDL at baseline. They went on a ketogenic diet, their LDL went up to over 200 mg/dL, and we find and we uh enrolled them in this prospective study. The prospective study is going to look at a one-year plaque change with LDLs above 200 mg or above 190 mg/dL on a ketogenic diet to see if they have more rapid plaque progression. What I'm going to show you tonight is the baseline characteristics, their first scan after being on a ketogenic diet for 4.7 years, and we're going to compare it to a population-based study called The Miami Heart Study (MiHeart) to look to see if they have increased atherosclerosis compared to matched uh uh patients."

**03:32 - 4:24: LMHR ≠ FH**
The slide is titled "LMHR ≠ FH". It presents a comparison table:
**Lean Mass Hyper-Responder (Left Column):**
- Normal Cholesterol on mixed macro diet
- Induced by low-carb diet (highlighted in yellow)
- LDL inversely associated with BMI
- Always HDL-C ≥ 80, TG ≤ 70
- Cardiovascular Risk under study (highlighted in yellow)
**Familial Hypercholesterolemia (Right Column):**
- Increased Cholesterol on mixed macro diet
- Congenital (highlighted in yellow)
- No major relationship to BMI
- HDL-C and TG variable
- Known high Cardiovascular Risk (highlighted in yellow)
Small circular icons representing HDL-C, LDL-C, and TG are at the bottom left for LMHR, and a DNA helix for FH on the right.
The speaker explains the differences: "Now, it should be understood that Lean Mass Hyper Responders are different than FH. While they both have very high LDL cholesterol, Lean Mass Hyper Responders have a normal cholesterol if they have a normal diet. And when they're they're on a low-carb diet, their LDL goes up dramatically. They are otherwise healthy individuals. These are lean individuals, um, in general, that we studied, and they tend to have this remarkable response of LDLs that go up, I think worldwide there's somebody with an LDL above 1000 mg/dL, um, uh induced by a ketogenic diet. FH on the other hand is a congenital disorder, they have high LDL through their entire life, uh, and it's unrelated to their diet."

**04:24 - 5:47: Methods**
The slide is titled "METHODS".
Bullet points detail the study methodology:
- "80 LMHR participants with **LDL-C ≥ 190, HDL-C ≥ 60, and triglycerides ≤ 80 mg/dl** were matched 1:1 for age, gender, race, diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, hypertension, and past smoking to asymptomatic subjects from the MiHeart cohort."
- "LMHR followed a ketogenic diet (very low carb diet), usually <30g/d, sufficient to induce an elevation in circulating ketone bodies, confirmed in the participants."
- "Primary analysis were levels of Total Plaque Score (TPS), Total Stenosis Score (TSS) and Segment Involvement Score (SIS)"
A diagram on the right depicts a human figure with three circles representing HDL-C (purple, ≥70 mg/dl), LDL-C (green, ≥190 mg/dl), and TG (yellow, ≤80 mg/dl), with arrows pointing to the figure.
The speaker explains the methods: "So we uh matched 80 of our 100 participants with an LDL above 190 mg/dL, they had to have high HDL and low triglycerides, matched 1:1 for age, gender, race, diabetes, hyperlipidemia, hypertension, and past smoking to asymptomatic subjects from the Miami Heart cohort. We couldn't match all 100 patients in our study because Miami Heart didn't have the same age range as we did, but we matched 80 patients to this Miami Heart cohort across all of these different uh, uh values. We couldn't match them for for LDL, because of course, these Lean Mass Hyper Responders had remarkably high LDL. These patients had uh documented uh ketogenesis. They had uh ketones measured, and they had to have again normal or no no genetic disposition or no genetic markers of familial hyperlipidemia. These are the baseline characteristics. And there were differences, most notably, of course, in LDL cholesterol."
A table titled "Table 1. Baseline Characteristics (KETO vs MiHeart)" appears at 0:521. The LDL-C row in the KETO column shows "272 ± 91 mg/dL" and the MiHeart column shows "123 ± 38 mg/dL" (p<0.001 for both Total Cholesterol, LDL-C, Non-HDL-C, HDL-C, and Triglycerides). The speaker highlights this difference. "272 average LDL in the KETO group. Miami Heart group had an LDL of 123. All the other variables were well matched. Um, uh as best as we could match these these two different uh populations."

**05:47 - 07:22: Results**
The slide is titled "RESULTS".
Bullet points summarize the findings:
- "The matched mean age was 55.5 years, with mean LDL-C of **272 (max LDL-C 591) mg/dl** and mean **4.7 years** duration on a ketogenic diet."
- "There was **no significant difference** in coronary plaque burden as compared to MiHeart controls (mean LDL 123 mg/dl); nb: pre-KETO LDL-C in KETO group was 122 mg/dl" (highlighted in yellow).
- "CAC score (median and IQR) [0 (0,56)] versus [1 (0, 49)], p = 0.520"
- "CCTA Total Plaque Score [0 (0,2) versus (0,4), p = 0.357]"
- "No relationship of LDL-C elevations and plaque" (highlighted in yellow).
The speaker elaborates on the results: "So, when we looked at these population, they were both 55 years old. Uh again, the uh Lean Mass Hyper Responders had an LDL of 272 mg/dL on an average of 4.7 years duration. So it wasn't like this was one week of being having an LDL this high. This was almost a five-year duration of having a ketogenic diet and an LDL above 190 mg/dL. And when we compared them to patients with more normal LDLs, LDL mean LDL of 123, there was no difference in coronary plaque burden. So we did not see any uh evidence that this LDL of 272 induced more atherosclerosis over this five-year period. We looked at calcium score, we looked at total plaque score on the CT Angiogram, and neither of them showed any differences whatsoever. There was no relationship between LDL cholesterol and elevations in uh presence of plaque."
A table titled "Table 2. CCTA semi-quantitative measures compared between 80 KETO subjects matched 1:1 to 80 Miami Heart (MiHeart) subjects..." appears at 06:48. It shows CAC score, Total Stenosis Score, Total Plaque Score, and Segment Involvement Score for KETO and MiHeart groups, with p-values all above 0.05, indicating no significant difference.
The speaker points out the table: "When we look at the uh calcium scores between the groups, again, 80 patients in each cohort, there was no difference in calcium scores. There was no difference in total stenosis score, in other words, how many stenotic vessels they had. There was no difference in total plaque score, and no difference in the number of segments of plaque that were involved, uh with their arteries. So they had exactly the same plaque characteristics as a population of patients who did not have an LDL above 190 mg/dL."

**07:22 - 07:58: LDL-C versus Total Plaque Score**
The slide displays two bar-and-line graphs side-by-side, comparing "LDL-C versus Total Plaque Score" for "LMHR (KETO, n=80)" on the left and "MiHeart (n=80)" on the right.
The left graph shows LMHR LDL-C (blue line) fluctuating at very high levels (mean 272 ± 91 mg/dl) across individual patients, while Total Plaque Score (TPS, green bars) is mostly low (median 0, IQR 0-2).
The right graph shows MiHeart LDL-C (blue line) at lower levels (mean 123 ± 38 mg/dl), with TPS (green bars) also mostly low but potentially a slightly higher median/IQR (median 1, IQR 0-4).
The speaker explains the graphs: "When we look across the different cohorts, you can see LDL um uh up and down and the uh plaque distribution in in green, there was no difference between these two groups. As a matter of fact, the area under the curve was identical. So there was absolutely no increase in plaque uh between the Lean Mass Hyper Responders with an LDL cholesterol of 272 mg/dL and the Miami Heart population, which had an LDL cholesterol of 123 mg/dL. Again, matched across all other variables that we could match."

**07:58 - 08:34: No Association between TPS and LDL-C**
The slide shows two scatter plots, "KETO" on the left and "MiHeart" on the right, both plotting Total Plaque Score (Y-axis) against LDL cholesterol (X-axis).
Both plots show a scatter of points with no clear positive correlation, and linear trend lines that are relatively flat.
The speaker reinforces this point: "When we looked at the uh Total Plaque Score, how much plaque they had in their coronaries, compared to their LDL, this is now stratified across LDL, and we can see Total Plaque Score, no difference across LDL's going up as high as 600 mg/dL, and no relationship in either the KETO group on the left or the Miami Heart group on the right. So these two groups had very, very similar, um, uh, uh plaque distributions, and there's no significant difference between the two."
A summary diagram appears at 08:32, reiterating the findings.

**08:34 - 09:45: Proposed Mechanism of LMHR: Lipid Energy Model**
The slide is titled "Proposed Mechanism of LMHR: Lipid Energy Model".
A complex diagram illustrates metabolic pathways involving adipose tissue, muscle, liver, and circulating lipoproteins (VLDL-C, LDL-C, HDL-C, TG). Key elements are carbohydrate intake, glycogen, leptin, insulin, lipolysis, VLDL turnover, LPL-mediated VLDL turnover.
Text on the right states:
- "In lean metabolically healthy subjects: carbohydrate restriction -> hepatic glycogen depletion -> increased VLDL export and LPL-mediated turnover to meet systemic energy demands"
- "Elevated LPL-mediated turnover VLDL produces LMHR triad of elevated LDL-C, elevated HDL-C and low TG"
- "Norwitz et al. Metabolites 2022"
The speaker explains the proposed mechanism: "So what's the proposed mechanism? Well, we know that in lean metabolically healthy subjects, carbohydrate restriction will lead to uh uh glycogen depletion in the liver. And we think that that leads to a lipoprotein lipase mediated turnover, which increases LDL. And you can see at the very bottom here, that more LDL mediated VLDL turnover leads to a higher uh um uh export of VLDL. When you have more VLDL and lipoprotein lipase, you end up with more HDL and more LDL, and less triglycerides. You you process that VLDL, you convert it into LDL in- into uh LDL uh VLDL, you convert it to LDL cholesterol, and you end up with higher levels of both HDL and uh LDL cholesterol. So we think that it's all lipoprotein lipase mediated. Obviously, this needs more evaluation, but in a lean person, the the body switches its energy model to a more uh, uh to uh activate lipoprotein lipase."

**09:45 - 10:48: Western Denmark Heart Registry**
The slide is titled "Western Denmark Heart Registry, Mortensen et al. Circulation 2023".
A table titled "Table 2. Association of LDL-C Level ≥193 mg/dL vs <116 mg/dL With Development of Myocardial Infarction and ASCVD Stratified by CAC Groups in the Western Denmark Heart Registry" is presented.
The table shows:
- Overall: N=11,805, MI=131 (1.1%), Adj. HR=3.12 (1.96-4.98), ASCVD=286 (2.4%), Adj. HR=2.10 (1.45-3.03)
- CAC=0: N=6,366, MI=35 (0.5%), Adj. HR=0.95 (0.35-2.73) (highlighted in red), ASCVD=94 (0.9%), Adj. HR=0.92 (0.48-1.79)
- CAC>0: N=5,439, MI=96 (1.8%), Adj. HR=3.53 (2.11-5.92) (highlighted in red), ASCVD=192 (4.2%), Adj. HR=2.42 (1.59-3.67)
Two bar graphs below show "Myocardial Infarction" and "ASCVD" risk per 1,000 years for "Overall", "CAC=0", and "CAC>0" groups, with higher LDL-C (red bars) compared to lower LDL-C (green bars).
The speaker explains this study: "When we looked at, um, uh cohort of patients, so you could say, 'Well, this is not really a fair analysis. We're looking at five years of high LDL versus a lifetime of high LDL.' But I want to show you this population of patients from Denmark. In Denmark, they studied patients with coronary calcium scores. These were 11,805 patients with LDLs above 190. Very similar to what I just presented. And you'll see that more than 50% of these patients, with a lifetime LDL above 190, did not have any coronary calcium. So not all LDL automatically promotes atherosclerosis. And if they have no coronary calcium, their hazard ratio for having a high LDL was one. There was no increased risk of having a high LDL in the setting of no atherosclerosis. So I don't believe that all atherosclerotic plaque is that all LDL is necessarily pathological."

**10:48 - 11:05: Limitations**
The slide is titled "LIMITATIONS".
Bullet points list the limitations:
- "Modest duration of elevated LDL-C, approximately, ~4.7 years" (highlighted in yellow).
- "Modest sample size (n = 100 for prospective trial)" (highlighted in yellow).
- "No hard CV outcomes"
The speaker highlights the points: "So our limitations, we only had 4.7 year follow-up. The sample size was only 100 patients. We're going to study these patients at one year uh follow-up for CCTA, and then hopefully longer term as well. And, of course, we did not have hard CV outcomes."

**11:05 - 11:30: Conclusions**
The slide is titled "CONCLUSIONS".
Bullet points state:
- "After mean duration of **4.7 years** with carbohydrate restriction-induced elevations in LDL-C (mean **272 mg/dl**), a metabolically healthy cohort of LMHR and near-LMHR subjects on a ketogenic diet **did not have greater atherosclerotic burden** than participants from a population-based cohort with similar risk profiles but markedly lower LDL-C." (highlighted in yellow).
- "There was also **no correlation between LDL-C level and plaque burden**" (highlighted in yellow).
The speaker delivers the conclusions: "But I'll just conclude by saying that these patients with metabolically healthy cohort of Lean Mass Hyper Responders did not have greater atherosclerotic burden than participants matched with more normal LDL values. And there was no correlation in this co- in study between LDL cholesterol and plaque burden. So with that, I'll stop and thank you all very much."

**11:30 - 17:35: Q&A Session**
The slide transitions to "Contact" with the speaker's email and Twitter handle.
- "For Further information"
- "mbudoff@lundquist.org"
- "Twitter: @BudoffMD"
The speaker remains on screen.

**Q&A:**
*   **00:11:32** Question from an off-screen male voice (host): "Great presentation. Thank you so much, Matt. So, does it blow away the LDL hypothesis?"
*   **00:11:37** Speaker: "So no, I, I think that, I think it's very important to recognize, and let me just go back here, because this is the LDL hypothesis. In this population of patients from Denmark with with FH, if they had coronary calcium and a high LDL, they were 3.5 times more likely to suffer a myocardial infarction and 2.4 times more likely to die. So there's still a very strong relationship between LDL and cardiovascular events, but I don't think everybody is susceptible to LDL. And this again, I think, is a unique population where their LDL is up for a different reason than genetically elevated LDL cholesterol. So I don't want to..."
*   **00:12:21** Off-screen male voice (Zach): "No, this is fascinating. Well, belief in LDL..."
*   **00:12:23** Speaker: "...in LDL."
*   **00:12:24** Zach: "Matt, I may have missed it. Did you do particle counts? And number two, is it what's the dense which which fraction went up? The dense particle or the fluffy type if you look at the LDL?"
*   **00:12:35** Speaker: "So the LDL particles are larger. We're still doing a lot of advanced work with Boston Heart and Ernie Schaefer's group. So we're gonna have a lot of different variables coming out of this, including particle number, particle size. Large, but I believe it's more large, fluffy LDL, which may not be as pathological."
*   **00:12:54** Zach: "Okay, Zach first and then you go."
*   **00:12:56** Off-screen male voice (Miguel): "So Matt, I wonder if if this is, is there some sort of ascertainment bias involved in your finding a unique cohort of individuals who don't have coronary disease and are able to follow a ketogenic diet, etcetera, etcetera. So you've in a certain sense selected individuals will not demonstrate coronary disease and now you're saying, 'Aha, they don't have coronary disease.'"
*   **00:13:24** Speaker: "Well, that's a great point, Zach, and, and I, I want to be very clear, this is not the primary endpoint of the trial. The primary endpoint of the trial, this is just the baseline characteristics. We're gonna look at the effect of very high LDL over the course of one year to see if they have progression of atherosclerosis. But I agree with you, Zach, we need to look at this over time to make sure this is not just an ascertainment bias that we're taking healthy people and just saying, 'Oh, they don't have a lot of plaque at baseline.' We’re going to do a lot more of that."
*   **00:13:54** Miguel: "Okay, you go next."
*   **00:13:56** Off-screen female voice: "So I just have a quick question as far as, um, do you have an idea of why this subset of patients get these extremely high LDLs on a ketogenic diet? I mean, this is not normal, right? I mean, people don't don't..."
*   **00:14:10** Speaker: "It's a small subset of patients. We think that when you're lean, and you have, these are patients who are very lean at baseline, that their lipoprotein lipase is activated. They have more VLDL excretion from the liver because the liver is glycogen depleted. They excrete more LDL or VLDL, and that VLDL then gets converted more rapidly into LDL. It's a small subgroup of patients, though."
*   **00:14:35** Female voice: "Are you going to do any kind of genotyping or..."
*   **00:14:37** Speaker: "So we did genotyping. These are patients who have every single, they had to be in the trial negative for every single known genotype for FH or any hypercholesterolemic process. So we have no no signals from genotyping."
*   **00:14:53** Miguel: "And they, and they in the people who are voluntarily put on a ketogenic diet, although you said they were lean, usually they experience a significant amount of weight loss. Uh, was there a lot of weight loss in that group? And could it be that over 4.7 years if you had a whole lot of weight loss, that's cardioprotective? Uh, and there are many mechanisms that could explain maybe why you don't see the sort of change in the arteries when you look at them."
*   **00:15:19** Speaker: "Oh, absolutely. So this is the baseline characteristics, so this is our first point in time. So we have to look at that, but we will look at weight change over the course of the prospective study. This is the beginning of the prospective study."
*   **00:15:31** Zach: "Doctor Gager."
*   **00:15:32** Female voice (Dr. Gager): "Hey, so, um, we are seeing these people in clinical practice and it makes us feel very uncomfortable when somebody walks in with this. So would you recommend that we're supposed to just ignore the LDL and not treat them if they walk in?"
*   **00:15:44** Speaker: "Oh, no. So, I would say, get a calcium score. If we have great data that a calcium score of zero does not induce long-term risk, then a calcium score of zero in this population should reassure you that the Denmark study was FH lifetime LDL. They had no increased risk of high LDL if their calcium score was zero. Okay, I would say get an LDL..."
*   **00:16:05** Host: "Two more questions."
*   **00:16:06** Speaker: "...going as fast as I can. Okay."
*   **00:16:10** Host: "It's really good."
*   **00:16:14** Miguel: "Ketogenic diet is counterproductive."
*   **00:16:16** Host: "How much fat do you have in your diet?"
*   **00:16:20** Miguel: "Supplying ketones themselves would be more productive than ketogenic diet. Ketogenic diet is is a active diet, old fashion diet."
*   **00:16:33** Speaker: "Well, so I think we we would be nice to study ketone replacement versus a ketogenic diet. But I think the we just heard about the SGLT2s, and I think one of the proposed mechanisms of SGLT2s is more ketones as a energy source. So I think we need to explore that further."
*   **00:16:51** Host: "Okay, Yahuda and that's it."
*   **00:16:52** Off-screen male voice (Yahuda): "So Matt, just to understand, did I see it correctly that the people in the study had a BMI of 22?"
*   **00:16:59** Speaker: "Yes. Lean mass. These are lean patients."
*   **00:17:03** Yahuda: "That's very lean patients. That's not what we see typically in the clinics, right? Typically they are obese with BMI of 13-35, who are gonna have LDL and things act the same way in that population."
*   **00:17:19** Speaker: "Yes. No, and, and this is a Lean Mass Hyper Responder, these are lean patients, who I think process this diet differently than our type 2 diabetics or obese patients where they're having a different response to LDL as well, though."
*   **00:17:33** Host: "Thank you, Zach."
*   **00:17:34** The speaker bows slightly, and the video ends.

summary: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. resigned from CHD in Dec 2024 to focus on his expected HHS role. He no longer runs CHD or controls its operations, including merchandise sales. Claims that he can stop CHD from selling anti-vaccine products are false.    https://apnews.com/article/rfk-money-vaccines-salary-trump-cfd26a0d9e88e901b596f823a6f766f2?utm_source=chatgpt.com
tweetText:
RFK's Children's Health Defense is making money selling a child's onesie with the phrases "No Vax, No Problem" and "Unvaxxed, Unafraid" to parents for $26. 

Now that RFK is "pro-vaccine", will he ask his organization to stop selling these vehemently anti-vaccine products? https://t.co/vTAHOJRakH

caption.video0:
At the start of the video (0:00), Senator Bernie Sanders is speaking at what appears to be a congressional hearing. He has white hair, is wearing glasses, a blue shirt, and a striped tie under a dark suit jacket. He leans forward, gesturing with his right hand, appearing engaged and somewhat stern. Behind him, Senator Elizabeth Warren is visible, listening intently.

Sanders is saying, "I, I think the gist of what you were trying to say today is you're really pro-vaccine, you wanna ask questions." (0:00-0:07)
He continues, "You have started a group called the Children's Health Defense." (0:08-0:12)

At 0:12, the video cuts to a display of two white baby onesies against a light blue background. Sanders' hand then points to the onesies.
The onesie on the left has "UNVAXXED UNAFRAID" printed on it in black text, with a smaller Children's Health Defense logo below.
The onesie on the right has "NO VAX, NO PROBLEM" printed in black text, also with the Children's Health Defense logo below.

Sanders' voice continues from the previous clip, "You're the originator. Right now, as I understand it, on their website, they are selling what's called onesies. These are little things, clothing for babies." (0:12-0:22)
He points to the left onesie and says, "One of them is titled 'Unvaxxed Unafraid'." (0:23-0:27)
He adds, "Next one, and they're sold for 26 bucks a piece by the way." (0:28-0:30)
He then points to the right onesie and states, "Next one is 'No Vax, No Problem'." (0:31-0:35)

The video cuts back to Bernie Sanders (0:36). He is leaning forward, gesturing with both hands, his expression intense. Elizabeth Warren is still visible behind him, looking serious.
Sanders says, "Now you're coming before this committee and you say you're pro-vaccine. Just wanna ask some questions. And yet your organization is making money, selling a child's product to parents for 26 bucks, which casts fundamental doubt on the, on the usefulness of vaccines. Can you tell us now that you are pro-vaccine, that you're gonna have your organization take these products off the market?" (0:36-1:04)

The video then cuts to Robert F. Kennedy Jr. (1:04), who is wearing a dark suit and a blue tie, sitting at the witness table. He looks at Sanders and responds, "Senator, I have no power over that organization. I'm not part of it. I resigned from the board." (1:04-1:08)

Sanders immediately cuts back in (1:08), pointing at Kennedy, "That was just a few months ago. You founded that, you certainly have power. You can make that..." (1:08-1:13)
Kennedy attempts to interject, "I have, I have..." (1:13)
Sanders cuts him off, raising his voice, "Are you supportive of this?" (1:13-1:15)

Kennedy (1:15) responds calmly, "I've had nothing to do with leadership..." (1:15-1:17)
Sanders again cuts him off, with increased volume and hand gestures, "Are you supportive of these onesies?" (1:17-1:19)
Kennedy (1:19) states, "I'm supportive of vaccines." (1:19-1:20)
Sanders (1:20) shouts, "Are you supportive of these, this clothing which is militantly anti-vaccine?" (1:20-1:25)

Kennedy (1:25) responds slowly and deliberately, "I, I am supportive of vaccines. I will, I, I am for good science and I want to protect the..." (1:25-1:32)
Sanders (1:32) interrupts one last time, "But you will not tell the organization you founded not to continue selling that product. Thank you, Mr. Chairman." (1:32-1:38)
Sanders then sits back in his chair, finishing his statement. Kennedy remains silent.

claim_or_not:
Cleaned text: RFK's Children's Health Defense is making money selling a child's onesie with the phrases "No Vax, No Problem" and "Unvaxxed, Unafraid" to parents for $26. Now that RFK is "pro-vaccine", will he ask his organization to stop selling these vehemently anti-vaccine products?

The tweet expresses an implication about a healthcare-related topic.

Yes.

summary: According to the National Cancer Institute, cell phones do not cause brain or other kinds of cancer in humans. It actually increases like a 30-minute phone call, depending on the on the phone, the new iPhones are even more deadly. It it heats the brain several degrees. It's a microwave." **2:00-2:07**: The wide shot of Alex Jones at his desk continues as he states, "But I'm digressing. Look at this. No more research on cell phone radiation and human health, Government says."

**2:07-2:17**: A screenshot of an "INFOWARS" article is shown, titled "No More Research on Cellphone Radiation and Human Health, Government Says." The subtext reads, "The National Toxicology Program said it no longer plans to study the effects of cellphone radiation on human health, citing technical challenges and lack of resources — even though its own $30 million study found evidence of cancer and DNA damage." **2:17-2:33**: The video transitions to an overhead shot of a white iPhone lying on a textured light-colored surface, surrounded by many small, dark insects (ants or similar). A classic arcade shooter in which you play as Alex Jones and save the world from the evil globalist plot to turn everyone into bug-eating pod-dwelling lizards! summary: Fluoride toxicity depends on the dose & duration of exposure. Very high acute doses can be toxic to both animals & humans, but the levels used in water fluoridation are seen as safe by health experts.  https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7261729/    https://www.cdc.gov/fluoridation/timeline-for-community-water-fluoridation/index.html    https://www.nidcr.nih.gov/health-info/fluoride/the-story-of-fluoridation    Fluoride levels in well water:  https://www.watersystemscouncil.org/download/wellcare_information_sheets/potential_groundwater_contaminant_information_sheets/Fluoride.pdf    https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7649535/    https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4547570/    Fluoride's Transformation:  https://origins.osu.edu/article/toxic-treatment-fluorides-transformation-industrial-waste-public-health-miracle

caption.video0:
The video, titled "Poisoned Horses", begins with a scenic shot of mountains and a sunset.

[00:00-00:03] Title card: "Poisoned Horses" is displayed in yellow text over a tranquil mountain landscape with a sunset. Gentle acoustic guitar music plays.
[00:03-00:08] The scene changes to a rural setting. A brown horse stands in a fenced pen. A house and trees are visible in the background. The music continues.
[00:08-00:15] A man with a beard and glasses, wearing a suit jacket and tie, stands in front of the fenced horse pen. He holds a microphone close to his mouth. He says, "We're high in the mountains of Southern Colorado in Pagosa Springs. And we're just about ready to go meet Wayne and Cathy Justice."
[00:15-00:23] He continues, "Who learned the hard way just how devastating fluoride can be to their... their animals."
[00:23-00:29] He adds, "They show horses, Quarter Horses. They've lost six and four dogs."
[00:29-00:33] He concludes, "They now know why." He then lowers the microphone and steps out of view.
[00:33-00:39] Cathy Justice, a woman with blonde hair, wearing a blue shirt, sits in an indoor setting with white tiled walls behind her. She speaks directly to the camera. "I distill all of our drinking water, our dog's water now. We've, we had, we've had four dogs die."
[00:39-00:46] The video cuts to a close-up of a white cup containing a thick, brown, muddy liquid. A hand is holding the cup. Cathy's voiceover states, "This is what we got out of the distiller while the water was fluoridated."
[00:46-00:54] The video shows a clear plastic bottle also containing a light brown, murky liquid. The man's voiceover (Wayne Justice) says, "90% of the arsenic in drinking water additives comes from the hydrofluorosilicic acid fluoridation chemical."
[00:54-00:59] The video returns to Cathy's voiceover as the camera shows a hand holding a clearer sample of water in a glass jar. She says, "But you see how much clearer it got and it wasn't thick."
[00:59-01:02] The camera pans across several jars of water, some clear, some murky. A white paper cup is also visible.
[01:02-01:09] Cathy Justice is back on screen. "Hi, I'm Cathy Justice, my husband is Wayne Justice, Western artist. We raise registered Quarter Horses."
[01:09-01:14] She continues, "We breed, show and play with our horses. We love our horses."
[01:14-01:21] The video shows three healthy-looking brown horses grazing peacefully in a grassy field. Cathy's voiceover states, "And, uh, everything was going great until 1985 when artificial fluoridation started."
[01:21-01:29] The scene changes to a horse in a fenced dirt pen, appearing slightly emaciated and perhaps limping as it moves its back left leg slowly. Another horse is partially visible to the left. Cathy's voiceover says, "We had no idea that was what was making our horses sick."
[01:29-01:36] The video shows a horse grazing in a fenced dirt pen, again looking thin, with its left hind leg appearing stiff or painful. "So we changed feeds, we did everything, had vets come out, we, we went to the ends of the earth trying to figure out what was wrong with our horses."
[01:36-01:39] A close-up of a horse's lower leg and hoof, showing what appears to be abnormal growth or lameness in the hoof.
[01:39-01:46] A horse with a light brown coat, looking unwell, stands and then slowly grazes in a dirt pen. Its spine appears prominent. Cathy's voice breaks as she says, "And it wasn't until 2002 that my most precious baby girl died."
[01:46-01:59] Cathy covers her face with her hands, clearly distressed, then composes herself. "It's hard to talk about it. Six different vets, two of which were at Colorado State University, that had absolutely no clue what was wrong with them."
[01:59-02:02] Cathy continues, "Winter of 2003, 2004..."
[02:02-02:11] The video shows an aerial view of snowy, mountainous terrain. Cathy's voiceover says, "Uh, we had snow on the ground all winter long. That's the first winter in many winters because Colorado's been having a drought that we had snow on the ground all winter long."
[02:11-02:15] A large metal trough filled with clear water is shown. The word "Universal" is visible on the side. Cathy states, "We have a 100-gallon tank outside..."
[02:15-02:26] The video shows horses moving around a fenced area with the tank visible. "And we do allow our horses to be horses so we keep them in the barn at night, but during the day, which is the biggest part of their day, they are out and they're able to play and do what they like to do."
[02:26-02:35] Cathy is back on screen. "But we noticed that that tank that we normally filled every day and a half was only needing filled every 18 to 19 days."
[02:35-02:40] The video shows Cathy walking towards the large water trough in the fenced area. "So we started watching the horses and they would even stand right there by the tank..."
[02:40-02:47] Close-up of Cathy's hand reaching into the water trough. "Uh, with the heated city fluoridated water, looked clean, and eat snow."
[02:47-02:52] A horse is shown eating snow off the ground. "And they were eating snow a big part of the day."
[02:52-03:04] Cathy is back on screen. "And, uh, we noticed that they, all the colic stopped. The amount of colics that we were having, we didn't have one colic all winter long. And almost all of the symptoms..."
[03:04-03:15] The video cuts back to the horse from [01:39] grazing, looking thin and with a prominent spine. Cathy's voiceover continues, "Uh, Baby Doe who had all this heavy Cushing's hair, she shed all winter long. Well, when it gets down to 35 degrees below zero, a horse shouldn't be shedding, but she was shedding this excess hair."
[03:15-03:18] Cathy is on screen again.
[03:18-03:25] The video shows Cathy pointing towards a small ditch or ravine on their property. "Um, anyway, after all this snow melted, we have a little ravine that runs through our place and there was still snow up on the hill under the trees."
[03:25-03:26] A horse stands next to the fence.
[03:26-03:41] Cathy points at the muddy ground. "And, uh, this little, you can't even call it a stream, it's about 6 inches wide and maybe an inch deep of water, muddy water was coming down the hill and the horses would go over there and dig a little pool of this dirty, thick, muddy water..."
[03:41-03:46] The video shows Cathy standing next to the clean water tank. "...and would rather drink that water rather than drink the so-called clean city water."
[03:46-03:56] Wayne Justice, a man with a mustache, wearing a striped shirt, sits indoors and speaks to the camera. "My horse that I called Skipper, uh, right from the get-go when he was born and he's second generation, uh, started out with an attitude."
[03:56-04:06] The video shows Skipper, a brown horse, trotting around a fenced pen. "And, uh, he tried to bite me any time I went by, and he even kicked me one time..."
[04:06-04:19] Wayne is back on screen. "...and, and to the point where at one time I even thought of getting rid of him and getting another horse. Uh, this was all while he was on fluoridated water."
[04:19-04:26] Cathy is seen outside with Skipper, affectionately stroking his nose. Wayne's voiceover continues, "We noticed it didn't take too long after fluoridation had quit and our water..."
[04:26-04:39] Cathy is still stroking Skipper, smiling, and then looks at the camera, visibly pleased. "That his attitude changed and he, uh, he now is a lover. He's a completely different horse."
[04:39-04:44] Wayne is back on screen. "So I wouldn't sell him or part with him for a million dollars. He's a great horse."
[04:44-04:46] Wayne states, "It had to have been the fluoridation."
