The background is a lush green garden with numerous yellow daffodils in bloom on the grass and trees with light pink blossoms visible further back. The lighting is bright and sunny. A "GBN" logo is visible in the top left corner of the screen.

From 00:00 to 00:06, the woman begins speaking, looking directly at the camera. Her expression is calm but serious.
She says: "I wanted to take this opportunity to say thank you personally."

From 00:06 to 00:12, she continues speaking, occasionally gesturing subtly with her hands, still maintaining eye contact with the camera.
She says: "for all the wonderful messages of support and for your understanding whilst I've been recovering from surgery."

From 00:12 to 00:23, her expression remains steady as she continues to speak.
She says: "It has been an incredibly tough couple of months for our entire family, but I've had a fantastic medical team who have taken great care of me, for which I'm so grateful."

From 00:23 to 00:31, she maintains her gaze and speaking posture.
She says: "In January, I underwent major abdominal surgery in London, and at the time, it was thought that my condition was non-cancerous."

From 00:31 to 00:46, she continues to speak, her tone remaining measured.
She says: "The surgery was successful. However, tests after the operation found cancer had been present. My medical team therefore advised that I should undergo a course of preventative chemotherapy, and I'm now in the early stages of that treatment."

From 00:46 to 00:58, her expression seems to deepen slightly as she discusses the impact on her family.
She says: "This, of course, came as a huge shock, and William and I have been doing everything we can to process and manage this privately for the sake of our young family."

From 00:58 to 01:17, she continues speaking, emphasizing the need for time and the care taken with her children.
She says: "As you can imagine, this has taken time. It has taken me time to recover from major surgery in order to start my treatment. But most importantly, it has taken us time to explain everything to George, Charlotte and Louis in a way that's appropriate for them and to reassure them that I'm going to be okay."

From 01:17 to 01:27, she offers a reassuring smile while speaking of her recovery.
She says: "As I've said to them, I am well and getting stronger every day by focusing on the things that will help me heal in my mind, body and spirits."

From 01:27 to 01:40, her tone reflects gratitude for the support received.
She says: "Having William by my side is a great source of comfort and reassurance too. As is the love, support and kindness that has been shown by so many of you. It means so much to us both."

From 01:40 to 01:49, she speaks about the family's need for privacy to complete her treatment.
She says: "We hope that you'll understand that as a family, we now need some time, space and privacy while I complete my treatment."

From 01:49 to 02:00, she reaffirms her commitment to her work but stresses the importance of her recovery.
She says: "My work has always brought me a deep sense of joy, and I look forward to being back when I'm able. But for now, I must focus on making a full recovery."

From 02:00 to 02:14, her expression is compassionate as she extends her thoughts to others facing similar struggles.
She says: "At this time, I'm also thinking of all those whose lives have been affected by cancer. For everyone facing this disease in whatever form, please do not lose faith or hope. You are not alone."

At 02:14, the video cuts to a white screen with the blue and red "GBNI BRITAIN'S NEWS CHANNEL" logo in the center. The audio of the woman's voice fades out. The logo remains on screen until the video ends at 02:19.

The video shows a close-up of a young woman lying in what appears to be a hospital bed. She has dark hair pulled back, and her face is significantly discolored with large, irregular patches of a dark purplish-brown hue. These patches are prominent on her forehead, around her eyes (especially the left eye, which is almost entirely covered by the dark discoloration), on her cheeks, nose, and chin. Her eyes appear red-rimmed and she looks unwell. She is wearing a light-colored, possibly blue and white patterned, hospital gown or top. The background is out of focus, suggesting a room with light-colored walls or curtains.

In the upper-middle section of the screen, a white comment bubble with a dark border is visible. It reads: "Reply to Melissa🤍🤍🤍's comment" and below that, "jaundice is setting in. What the hell did they give you".

The woman is speaking, and her voice sounds weak, hoarse, and somewhat strained. Subtitles appear at the bottom of the screen, transcribing her words:

*   **0:00-0:02:** The woman begins speaking, and the subtitle reads: "I originally came in"
*   **0:02-0:05:** She continues: "because I suffer from a blood disorder called PNH"
*   **0:05-0:08:** She elaborates (and the subtitle displays what sounds like a mis-transcription of the full name of the disorder): "for autism nocturnal hemoglobin area" (likely intended as Paroxysmal Nocturnal Hemoglobinuria).
*   **0:08-0:12:** She adds: "I was diagnosed with the blood disorder back in January of this year"
*   **0:12-0:14:** She explains her recent movements: "I've since then moved to California"
*   **0:14-0:20:** She continues her story: "and really trying to work on myself without getting any treatment for the disease"
*   **0:20-0:29:** She states her reason for avoiding treatment: "as they wanted to give me a bunch of vaccines and I have never had a vaccine in my life"

The video segment ends with her speaking this last sentence, with the playhead at 2:43 of a 10:44 duration.

caption.video0:
The video features two different speakers discussing the alleged dangers of microwave ovens. A persistent text overlay reads: "Your MICROWAVE Will Damage You & YOUR HEALTH!"

**00:00-00:31: First Speaker (Younger Man in Kitchen)**
A man with dark, curly hair and a patterned white shirt stands in what appears to be a kitchen. He holds a small, white electronic device. A black microwave oven is visible behind him to his right.
*   **00:00-00:07:** He gestures with his hands, speaking passionately. He refers to the "WHO's website, the World Health Organization," and mentions "public exposure limits, maximum public safety exposure."
*   **00:07-00:11:** The video briefly cuts to a white document with text and a table, titled "Typical maximum public exposure." Under a "Source" column, "Micro-power" (presumably microwave oven) lists values of "0.5 (W/m²)" for electric field and magnetic flux density. The man's audio continues to explain that microwaves can go up to "0.5 watts, which is 500 milliwatts."
*   **00:11-00:16:** The video cuts back to the man in the kitchen. He points to the microwave and states, "So, the highest amount of radiation this fella could leak is 500 milliwatts." He has a somewhat incredulous expression.
*   **00:16-00:20:** He then declares, "An AirPods Pro..." as he points a finger upwards on each hand, then brings them to his ears as if inserting earbuds.
*   **00:20-00:22:** He continues, "is designed to go between your ears..." He then gestures emphatically towards the microwave.
*   **00:22-00:31:** He leans down towards the microwave, mimicking a posture of someone listening closely to it, while saying, "it's going similar levels to a freaking microwave on full blast. So you have to be like this. This is like, this is life with an AirPods Pro." He maintains a serious expression while in this bent-over position.

**00:31-02:17: Second Speaker (Older Man at Podium)**
An older man with long, receding grey hair and glasses, wearing a black shirt, is shown speaking at a podium with a microphone. Papers and a laptop are on the podium in front of him. The background is a plain, light brown wall.
*   **00:31-00:41:** He explains, "Here you got this klystron tube that's in it, and what it does is it puts 2.4 GHz into the food, and it vibrates the molecules at this frequency." He uses hand gestures to illustrate the vibration.
*   **00:41-00:54:** He continues, "And so once the food comes out, it's secondary emission. The heat coming off the food is 2.4 GHz. And then you take this food and you throw it down your throat into your digestive tract." He places his hand on his chest as he says "digestive tract."
*   **00:54-01:02:** He raises two fingers, then one, stating, "Number two cause of death in America: first is heart attack. What's the second one? Digestive tract cancer."
*   **01:02-01:16:** He then describes research from 1980 by a "Dr. Hertzel" in Sweden, where the original microwaves came from. He claims the research found microwaves were "terrible for rats, it was horrible for everything."
*   **01:16-01:25:** He dramatically recounts, "The original microwave company was thinking he'd come up with a great report. He says, 'Look at this, it's killing my rats!'" He leans back slightly with a surprised expression.
*   **01:25-01:32:** He then states, "And they said, 'Oh, well, thank you for the report, that's all we need. We'll take the report now.' But he ended up releasing the report and they put him in prison." He lowers his head slightly at the mention of prison.
*   **01:32-01:46:** He adds, "The report's still around." He emphasizes that "microwave vibrates molecules in a whole different way than human beings have been eating food for the billions of years we've heated over a fire. It's a whole different dynamic."
*   **01:46-02:00:** He concludes, "And when I read all the information myself back in 1980, I lifted my microwave up and threw it in the dumpster." The audience applauds.
*   **02:00-02:17:** He continues, "I had to—and now I have to go to these places like Denny's if I'm on the road. I say, 'Well, do you microwave the food?' And they'll say, 'Oh yeah.' Well, I said, 'Well, I don't want my food microwaved.' 'You don't?' Ask if you go to a restaurant whether they're microwaving your food and don't eat it. It's radiation, basically radiation." He gestures with his hands throughout this explanation.

caption.video0:
The video features an elderly man identified by a lower-third as "Stanley Plotkin MD," widely recognized as a leading authority in vaccinology. The digital timestamp on the papers in front of him indicates "JAN 11 2018." He is seated at a table, wearing glasses, a suit, and a red tie, with a bottle of water on his left. He is being questioned, mostly looking towards the unseen questioner.

The audio captures a deposition where Dr. Plotkin is asked about various vaccine ingredients and related ethical considerations.

Here's a breakdown of the exchange:

*   **00:00 - 00:09:** The questioner asks if the childhood vaccine schedule contains monkey kidney cells. Dr. Plotkin confirms, stating, "Well, the polio vaccine does."
*   **00:09 - 00:27:** The questioner asks if Dr. Plotkin is aware of any simian (monkey) viruses, specifically those from primates, that contaminated polio vaccines and infected recipients. Dr. Plotkin replies, "Yes, SV40." He then explains that SV40 stands for "Simian Virus 40" and confirms it was the 40th simian virus found.
*   **00:27 - 00:46:** Asked if he's aware of any virus from any animal other than simian or bovine in any vaccine, Dr. Plotkin confirms, "Yes, there was a pig virus present in one of the rotavirus vaccines, circovirus."
*   **00:47 - 01:08:** The questioner asks about calf serum in childhood vaccines. Dr. Plotkin explains that "calf serum is removed before the vaccine is used because you don't want to sensitize the vaccinate to cows."
*   **01:08 - 01:14:** Dr. Plotkin is handed a document and identifies it as a "vaccine excipient and media summary."
*   **01:15 - 01:24:** The questioner directs him to "Kinrix" (DTaP-IPV) on the first page of the document and asks if "calf serum" is listed. Dr. Plotkin, after examining the paper, confirms he sees it.
*   **01:24 - 01:34:** Asked if any childhood vaccines contain embryonic guinea pig cell cultures, Dr. Plotkin states that "Varicella vaccine was passaged in guinea pig cells."
*   **01:35 - 01:50:** The questioner asks if any vaccines contain cow's milk. Dr. Plotkin replies, "Oh well, casein, for example, could be." When asked if a child could become sensitized to casein, he says, "Well, I'm not sure about that," and shakes his head slightly when asked if he's "not sure anymore."
*   **01:50 - 01:55:** Dr. Plotkin confirms that influenza vaccines contain egg protein.
*   **01:55 - 02:00:** He also confirms that some vaccines contain gelatin from pigs.
*   **02:00 - 02:14:** The questioner asks about human albumin in childhood vaccines. Dr. Plotkin confirms its presence and defines human albumin as "part of human serum," which is the "part of the blood that is liquid."
*   **02:15 - 02:26:** Dr. Plotkin doesn't believe human albumin remains in the final product. He acknowledges it "could be" problematic if an individual is not healthy.
*   **02:26 - 02:40:** The questioner elaborates on potential problems, suggesting human blood components binding to aluminum could develop self-antibodies. Dr. Plotkin agrees, stating, "If they develop antibodies against a serum component, that would not be good."
*   **02:41 - 02:51:** Dr. Plotkin confirms that some childhood vaccines, specifically "Rubella, Varicella, Hepatitis A," contain MRC-5 human diploid cells.
*   **02:52 - 03:18:** He defines MRC-5 cells as "human fibroblast cell strain." He further explains, "They were created by taking fetal tissue... from a particular fetus that was aborted by maternal choice and the cells, the so-called fibroblast cells were cultivated."
*   **03:19 - 03:32:** The questioner asks if WI-38 human diploid lung fibroblasts are in any childhood vaccines. Dr. Plotkin responds that "they used to" be, but he doesn't think "anything is made in those cells anymore."
*   **03:32 - 03:46:** Directed to page 3 of his document for MMR and MMRV vaccines, Dr. Plotkin confirms that WI-38 human diploid lung fibroblasts are listed as ingredients.
*   **03:47 - 04:04:** The questioner asks if human DNA in vaccines is typically purposefully fragmented. Dr. Plotkin affirms, "Yes, I would say mostly for theoretical reasons, doesn't want to put DNA into a— attack DNA— into vaccines."
*   **04:05 - 04:07:** Dr. Plotkin confirms familiarity with "insertional mutagenesis."
*   **04:07 - 04:23:** Asked if there's any study to show that injecting millions of pieces of human DNA into babies and children is safe, Dr. Plotkin replies, "The only studies are all the safety studies that have been done on vaccines."
*   **04:23 - 04:45:** The questioner asks if the purpose of the study (involving fetal tissue) was to develop or support human cell lines for vaccine creation. Dr. Plotkin clarifies, "The idea was to study the cell strains from fetuses to determine whether or not they could be used to make vaccines."
*   **04:45 - 04:57:** The questioner states the study involved 74 fetuses, which Dr. Plotkin corrects to "76." He confirms these fetuses were "all three months or older when aborted."
*   **04:57 - 05:13:** Asked what organs he harvested from these fetuses, Dr. Plotkin states, "Well, I didn't personally harvest any," but clarifies that "a whole range of tissues were harvested by co-workers."
*   **05:13 - 05:46:** He confirms these tissues were cut into pieces and cultured. He then confirms specific organs harvested included the pituitary gland, lung, skin, kidney, spleen, and heart. When asked about the tongue, he says, "I don't recall, but probably yes."
*   **05:47 - 06:14:** The questioner brings up the plaintiff's religious objections to aborted fetal tissue in vaccines. Dr. Plotkin acknowledges awareness of these objections, mentioning a Catholic Church document that advises individuals needing vaccines to receive them regardless. He then makes a controversial statement: "...and that, that, I, I think it implies that I am the individual who will go to hell because of the use of aborted tissues which I am glad to do." A slight sarcastic smile appears on his face during this statement.
*   **06:14 - 06:16:** Dr. Plotkin has "no idea" if the mother of the aborted fetus was Catholic.
*   **06:16 - 06:22:** He explicitly states, "Yes," when asked if he takes issue with religious beliefs.
*   **06:22 - 06:29:** Asked if he accepts that some people hold inherently unprovable religious beliefs, he responds, "Oh, yes, I'm sure they do," with a dismissive smile.
*   **06:29 - 06:38:** Finally, asked about vaccines he received as a child, Dr. Plotkin laughs lightly and states, "Diphtheria... I think it was probably only diphtheria."

caption.video0:
The video is a montage of news clips related to the COVID-19 pandemic and Florida's response.

**0:00-0:02** The video opens with a shot of an electronic road sign on the side of a highway. The sign displays red text, partially visible, which appears to say "CHECKPOINT AHEAD 55 MPH". A black semi-truck with a white trailer drives past on the right side of the screen, blurring slightly due to motion. The highway is surrounded by green trees. A red news banner at the bottom reads "CORONAVIRUS PANDEMIC" and "MANDATORY CHECKPOINT AT STATE LINE >> ALABAMA-FLORIDA".

**0:02-0:04** The shot zooms in on the electronic sign. The text "CHECKPOINT AHEAD 55 MPH" is clearly visible in red lights against the black background of the sign. The news banner remains at the bottom.
- Audio: A male voice says, "It's a travel checkpoint unlike anything most Americans have ever seen."

**0:04-0:09** An aerial shot shows a multi-lane highway with a long line of traffic, primarily semi-trucks, stretching for miles on the left side of the road. The right side of the highway is clear. Green forests line both sides of the road.
- Audio: A male voice says, "backing up traffic for miles." A female voice adds, "Yesterday, we saw huge delays there. Some people waiting hours to get to that checkpoint."

**0:09-0:12** Another aerial shot shows a highway with several lanes. Orange traffic cones are set up, funneling traffic into fewer lanes, indicating a checkpoint. Several large trucks and a few cars are visible. An overpass is in the background. A watermark "firstcoastnews.com" is visible in the top left.

**0:12-0:21** The scene cuts to a female reporter, Kelly Foster, standing live in what appears to be a parking lot near a checkpoint. She is wearing a coral-colored top and a turquoise necklace, holding a microphone. There are orange traffic cones, a white pickup truck, and a tent behind her. A "LIVE" graphic is in the top right. A news banner identifies her: "KELLY FOSTER | WPMI" and "NWA" logo. It then changes to "FLORIDA SCREENING STATE VISITORS >> PENSACOLA".
- Audio: Kelly Foster speaks, "Kim, no state border checkpoints in Alabama, but as you can see here in Florida, Governor Ron DeSantis issued an executive order..."

**0:21-0:33** The scene transitions to Governor Ron DeSantis speaking at a podium, flanked by an American flag on the left and the Florida state flag on the right. He is wearing a dark suit and a tie. To his right, an ASL interpreter is actively signing. A "LIVE" graphic with "MAR 17, 2020 | 10:49 AM ET" is in the top right. A news banner at the bottom reads "Press Conference on the Coronavirus - COVID-19 | Tallahassee, Florida". It then changes to "4 UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA STUDENTS TEST POSITIVE FOR COVID-19". Another banner below that reads "wfla.com | THE FLORIDA CHANNEL | NOW FOLLOWING WFLA".
- Audio: Governor DeSantis says, "I am recommending to the Board of Governors, who are going to talk to the university presidents, um about going to remote learning for the rest of the spring semester at all Florida universities."

**0:33-0:37** Governor DeSantis continues speaking at the podium. The ASL interpreter is still active. The news banners remain.
- Audio: Governor DeSantis says, "restaurants, uh, we're going to do similar to what California did."

**0:37-0:47** Governor DeSantis is still speaking, but the camera is zoomed in more closely on him. The ASL interpreter is partially visible on the right. The news banners continue to cycle through various headlines.
- Audio: Governor DeSantis says, "Bars and nightclubs, uh, effective 5 PM today, um, you know, they're going to be suspended for 30 days. I worked with um, Miami Beach mayor and Fort Lauderdale,"

**0:47-0:49** The screen splits. On the left, Governor DeSantis is still speaking at the podium, making hand gestures. The ASL interpreter is visible on his right, also gesturing. On the right side of the screen, an aerial shot shows a crowded beach with many people, possibly spring breakers, on the sand and near the water.
- Audio: Governor DeSantis says, "supported their closures of some of their beaches."

**0:49-0:57** A female news anchor with brown hair and a floral dress is seated in front of a blue background with abstract city lights. To her right, a graphic shows a stylized red coronavirus icon and the text "CORONAVIRUS PANDEMIC".
- Audio: The news anchor says, "More restrictions put in place tonight in Miami Beach. The entertainment district between 5th and 15th streets is getting an 8 PM curfew."

**0:57-1:03** The scene cuts to Governor Ron DeSantis sitting at a large wooden desk in an office. An American flag is behind him on the left, and several framed photos are on the desk. He is wearing a dark suit and a striped tie, speaking directly to the camera and gesturing with his hands. A news banner at the bottom reads "THOSE POSITIONS, THEY WILL BE ELIGIBLE FOR CALL-BACK." and "9 CENTRAL FLORIDA CO". The weather graphic "89°" is visible.
- Audio: Governor DeSantis says, "We're going to suspend for two weeks any vacation, new vacation rentals in the state of Florida."

**1:03-1:07** Governor DeSantis continues speaking from his office, gesturing. The news banner changes to "The Governor issuing executive order 20-87".
- Audio: Governor DeSantis's voice, overlaid with narration, "The Governor issuing Executive Order 20-87..."

**1:07-1:10** A night shot shows the exterior of a two-story Best Western hotel. The building is lit up, and there are orange cones with small lights marking the parking area. The Best Western logo sign is visible.
- Audio: "...ordering vacation rental properties to suspend operations."

**1:10-1:17** The scene cuts to a female reporter, wearing a red dress, standing on a sidewalk at night, holding a microphone. Car headlights are visible in the background on a street.
- Audio: The reporter says, "I talked with one business owner that said he's tired of hearing about closures. He wants some good news."

**1:17-1:27** The scene transitions to a male reporter, Troy Campbell, standing outside at night, holding a microphone with a "NEWS 6" logo. He is wearing a reddish-purple button-up shirt. The background is dark and blurry. News banners identify him: "TROY CAMPBELL | NEWS6" and "NEW AT 11:00". The banner then changes to "PEOPLE GATHER AT THE BEACH DESPITE WARNING | DAYTONA BEACH SHORES, VOLUSIA COUNTY". A "WEATHER" graphic "83°" is at the bottom, and a "CLICK ORLANDO.COM" logo is on the right.
- Audio: Troy Campbell says, "Daytona Beach Shores police say last night their officers went around to all restaurants and bars, handing them a copy of the Governor's Executive Order. They say they're out tonight patrolling to make sure everyone's in compliance."

**1:27-1:32** The female news anchor from 0:49 is back on screen, with the "CORONAVIRUS PANDEMIC" graphic, but now it has an "UNEMPLOYMENT" banner above it and "CORONAVIRUS THE FACTS" below.
- Audio: The news anchor says, "theme parks, restaurants, retail stores and other businesses are still closed."

**1:32-1:39** The video transitions to Governor Ron DeSantis speaking at a podium against a red background with a faint star logo. He is wearing a dark suit and a tie. He looks around and smiles slightly.
- Audio: Governor DeSantis says, "For those of you who aren't from Florida, welcome to our oasis of freedom." A slight pause, then a "ding" sound effect.

caption.video0:
The video opens at 00:00, showing a man identified as Stefan Oelrich on a stage, addressing an audience. He is a middle-aged man with short, graying hair, wearing a dark suit, a white shirt, and a blue tie. The background is a plain light blue wall, with two spherical, glowing blue and green structures visible on either side of him. On the far right, part of a "WORLD HEALTH SUMMIT" logo is visible.

At 00:00, the speaker states in English: "...the mRNA vaccines are an example for that cell and gene therapy."
Simultaneously, a white Italian subtitle appears at the bottom of the screen: "In definitiva, i vaccini mRNA sono un esempio di quella terapia cellulare e genica." (In essence, mRNA vaccines are an example of that cell and gene therapy.)

The video continues with the speaker moving his hands slightly as he talks.
At 00:04, the speaker says: "I always like to say, if we had surveyed two years ago..."
The Italian subtitle updates: "Come amo sempre dire, se due anni fa avessimo fatto un sondaggio pubblico:" (As I always like to say, if two years ago we had done a public survey:)

At 00:08, the speaker continues: "...in the public, would you be willing to take a gene or cell therapy and inject it into your body..."
The Italian subtitle updates again: ""Saresti disposto a fare iniettare nel vostro corpo una terapia genica o cellulare?"" ("Would you be willing to have a gene or cell therapy injected into your body?")

At 00:15, the speaker states: "...we would have probably had a 95% refusal rate. I think this pandemic has also opened many people's eyes to..."
The Italian subtitle updates: "Avremmo probabilmente avuto un tasso di rifiuto del 95%. Penso che questa pandemia abbia anche allargato la visuale sull'innovazione a molte persone," (We would probably have had a 95% refusal rate. I think this pandemic has also broadened many people's view on innovation,)

At 00:22, the speaker concludes: "...to innovation in a way that was maybe not possible before."
The Italian subtitle updates for the final time: "in una modalità che forse non sarebbe stata possibile prima." (in a way that perhaps wasn't possible before.)

The video ends at 00:27 with the speaker still on stage. Throughout the video, a "HATS" logo is in the top left corner, and text in the top right corner says "Traduzione e sottotitoli: twitter.com/miia_2018". An "InShot" watermark is visible in the bottom right corner.

The video opens with a medium shot of Donald Trump standing at a podium, speaking into a black microphone. He is wearing a dark blue suit, a white shirt, and a bright blue tie. His blonde hair is styled in his characteristic manner. Behind him, a bookshelf filled with books of various colors (red, green, blue, brown) is visible. Above the bookshelf, there's a framed painting. To Trump's right (viewer's left), an American flag stands, and to his left (viewer's right), a flag with the presidential seal is displayed, along with a decorative plate on the wall. Parts of two other men in suits are visible on either side of Trump. A "THE BULWARK" logo is present in the top right corner.

Here's a synchronized breakdown:

*   **00:00-00:01:** Trump is looking down, speaking into the microphone. He says, "...then Hepatitis B..."
*   **00:01-00:05:** He continues to look down, then lifts his head slightly and looks forward, making a small gesturing movement with his right hand at 00:03. He states, "...is sexually transmitted."
*   **00:05-00:11:** Trump looks downwards again, then lifts his head and makes a broader gesturing motion with both hands, palms up, around 00:07-00:08, as if explaining something. He says, "There's no reason to give a baby that's almost just born..."
*   **00:11-00:14:** He continues speaking while looking forward, then slightly downwards, saying, "...Hepatitis B."
*   **00:14-00:19:** Trump lifts his gaze and makes a broad gesturing motion with both hands, palms facing upwards, as he speaks. He suggests, "So I would say, wait till the baby is 12 years old and formed..."
*   **00:19-00:23:** He lowers his gaze slightly while continuing to speak, saying, "...and take Hepatitis B. And I think if you do those things..."
*   **00:23-00:29:** Trump looks mostly downwards, occasionally glancing up, and concludes his statement without significant hand gestures. He says, "...it's gonna be a whole different, it's gonna be a revolution in a positive sense in the country."

caption.video0:
The video begins with a male news anchor speaking in a news studio. Text at the top of the screen reads: "THIS FRUIT EXTRACT KILLED CANCER IN 48 HOURS!". A female news anchor is also visible in a split screen. The male anchor states that the words "cure" and "cancer" don't often go together, but a single injection of a berry found only in Australia's far north is thought to cure many types of cancer. He mentions it's been tested on animals at "death's door" and now on humans, making tumors disappear in just 48 hours.

The scene transitions to show a person walking through a rainforest. Text on screen identifies the reporter as "DR ANDREW ROCHFORD". The reporter, narrating, says that in the depths of the Queensland rainforest grows a small seed with "huge potential".

A woman, identified as "DR VICTORIA GORDON, QBIOTICS", then speaks, calling the drug "extraordinary" and "unique" and "something quite different".

The video shows a close-up of several small, reddish-brown berries on a white surface. The narrator explains that the molecule, EBC-46, is derived from Australia's "blushwood tree" and can fight cancer. Dr. Gordon states, "A full tumor destruction within seven days."

Next, a split screen shows a dog with a large, red, open tumor on the left, and on the right, the same area after treatment, looking healed with some fur growing back. The narrator says, "Following astonishing results shrinking solid tumors in animals like Oscar, world-first human trials have now begun."

The video shows a woman walking with a man outside a modern building. She is identified as "DENISE POWELL, TRIAL PARTICIPANT". Denise explains that her cancer surgeon said he could remove one tumor, but if she got more, she might lose an arm.

The scene shifts to a doctor injecting Denise's armpit. She then recounts that "less than 20 minutes, the tumor had gone purple, then black, then within a couple of days, the tumor just kind of shrivelled up and died."

A close-up of skin shows two labels, "QB46C-H011 Tumour # 1 Patient ID: 404 Visit Day: 15" and "QB46C-H011 Tumour # 2 Patient ID: 404 Visit Day: 15", next to small, dark, healing marks. This is followed by a shot of a different area of skin, also with labels and a ruler, showing similar healing marks and redness.

The video shows gloved hands preparing a vial labelled "EBC-46". The narrator mentions that this first-stage trial uses "safe, low doses" but even early results look promising.

A man, identified as "DR BEN PANIZZA, PRINCESS ALEXANDRA HOSPITAL", states that surgery treats most tumors, about 60% worldwide, and requires an operating theatre, but with EBC-46, "you could go along anywhere".

An animation depicts a red cancer cell being injected with "EBC-46". The narration explains that EBC-46 triggers an immune response, activating white blood cells to attack the tumor and shrinking it to nothing. Another animation shows a human body with areas highlighted in red, indicating "Skin cancer", "Head & Neck cancer", and "Breast cancer", which are the targets of the treatment.

The reporter, Dr. Andrew Rochford, concludes the segment, stating that if this rainforest remedy is found to be safe and effective in humans, it could offer a whole new treatment option, especially for the elderly who cannot face more chemotherapy or surgery. He adds that the second, larger phase of the trial begins next year. The video ends with a shot of the "EBC-46" vials.

A "Disclose.tv" logo appears, followed by "SHARE THIS VIDEO" and a share icon.

The video starts with the title, "WHY WERE PEOPLE IN THE 1950s SLIMMER?". This is followed by a black-and-white clip of three women, in swimsuits, drinking from cups at a beach. The video transitions to a woman in a white lab coat, with the name "Dr. Amy Lee" visible on her coat, saying, "Let's rewind to the 1950s." A digital calendar at the bottom of the screen shows "01 01 1950."

The video then shows various clips of food items and ingredients, illustrating the narration. A bowl of fruit, yogurt, and granola appears as the narrator explains that "foods were sweetened with natural sugars like honey, molasses, and maple syrup." A graph with an upward trend is shown as the narrator states that "these sugars are lower on the glycemic index." A scale balancing items like fruit and honey on one side and processed sugars on the other is shown as the narrator explains that "making them more gentle on your blood sugar levels and therefore more forgiving on your waistline."

The video then fast-forwards to today, showing modern processed foods like muffins and packaged snacks in a supermarket, as the narrator explains that "the landscape for sugar has dramatically changed." The video shows shelves of honey, then the woman in the lab coat, as she says, "Modern processed foods are often loaded with sugars like corn syrup, rice syrup, and barley malt syrup." An image of three bottles labeled "Corn syrup," "Rice syrup," and "Barley syrup" with rising arrows indicates their high glycemic index. The narrator continues, "These sugars rank high on the glycemic index, which causes a swift rise in your blood sugar levels, potentially leading to weight gain." A person steps onto a weighing scale, showing the impact of these sugars.

The video then delves into the history of breakfast, explaining that it was a "bunch of marketing ploys." An old photo of a town and factory workers is shown as the narrator recounts the Industrial Revolution's impact on eating habits, leading to a demand for "lighter breakfast." The video shows a bowl of cereal and a person pouring milk into it, explaining that cereals were created "adding a ton of sugar and using cheap grain." The narrator explains that "breakfast literally means 'Break Fast'" and that the first food eaten after overnight fasting affects "hunger hormones and gut bacteria."

The video then shifts to a promotional segment for "Nucific," a company offering "natural fat loss helpers." The narrator identifies three "harmful foods" to avoid:
1. **Yogurt:** She explains that many yogurts contain excessive sugar, comparing it to "twice as much sugar as an entire bowl of their cereal Lucky Charms." She warns against yogurt claiming "probiotic benefits" if it's pasteurized, as this kills beneficial bacteria.
2. **Wheat bread:** Despite being marketed as healthier than white bread, many commercial whole wheat breads contain "high fructose corn syrup," which is linked to obesity. Fructose confuses the body's hunger hormones (ghrelin and leptin), leading to overeating and fat storage.
3. **Cereal bars:** These often contain multiple forms of sugar, including corn syrup and fructose, contributing to weight gain.

The narrator then introduces "4 Natural Fat Loss Helpers" from Nucific, emphasizing that these can help "better digest them and turn them into energy instead of fat." She highlights the importance of healthy gut bacteria.

The first fat loss helper is **Digestive Enzymes**. The narrator shares a humorous story about a 14-year-old McDonald's hamburger that looked fresh due to preservatives. She explains that preservatives in modern foods make it difficult for the stomach to break them down, leading to indigestion and fat storage. Digestive enzymes (amylase, bromelain, and lipase) are introduced as natural extracts that break down preservatives and aid in digestion.

The second helper is **Probiotics**. The narrator explains that Candida, a yeast, can overgrow in the stomach when fed by sugar, causing low energy, brain fog, yeast infections, and bloating. Probiotics, which are good bacteria, help rebalance the gut flora, leading to faster fat burning.

The third helper is **Caralluma Fimbriata**, a root extract that reduces appetite. It is explained that this nutrient can help manage cravings, especially for emotional eaters, by modifying the hunger drive in the brain.

The fourth helper is **EGCG (Green Tea Extract)**, a powerful antioxidant and metabolism booster. It helps boost fat oxidation, even during sleep, and inhibits the storage of excess carbohydrates as body fat.

The video then summarizes the four natural fat loss helpers, emphasizing that they work together to provide optimal weight management. The narrator explains that many Americans suffer from poor digestion due to modern food processing and that improving digestion is key to losing weight.

Finally, the video concludes with an offer for Nucific's Bio-X4 supplement, which combines all four natural fat loss helpers. The speaker asserts that Bio-X4 is not a diet pill but a comprehensive digestive support compound designed for weight loss, improved digestion, and immune health. The video highlights user testimonials, safety information (all-natural, gluten-free, allergen-free, no lactose or glucose, packaged in fresh-locked glass bottles), and a special new customer offer of $49 per bottle with free shipping. It concludes with a call to action to try Bio-X4 today.

Dr. Thorp introduces himself as a physician, board-certified in obstetrics and gynecology, and subspecialty-certified in high-risk obstetrics (maternal-fetal medicine). He mentions practicing for nearly 45 years, having graduated from medical school in 1979. He highlights his extensive involvement in clinical research and publications throughout his career, particularly over the last four years concerning COVID-19 narratives.

Jim Ferguson then prompts Dr. Thorp to discuss the article, specifically touching on the claim that MRNA from vaccines does not stay localized in the injection site, and how attempts to present this information were discredited. Dr. Thorp acknowledges his wife, Maggie Thorp, an attorney and researcher, for her involvement in their work, which includes approximately 15 articles published on "America Out Loud," a platform for "truth and freedom."

Dr. Thorp introduces the central theme of their latest article: "the antagonism of progressive lies" propagated by three organizations he terms "the Unholy Trinity of the Mother Baby Death Cult." He identifies these as the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG), the American Board of Obstetricians and Gynecology (ABOG), and the Society for Maternal-Fetal Medicine (SMFM). He accuses these organizations of lying and causing harm, stating they have "murdered and injured more global mothers, pre-borns, and newborns than anybody else in the world," and asserts he has strong evidence to back this.

He recounts the progression of the "lies":
1.  **Early 2021:** Governments and these organizations claimed MRNA vaccines stayed in the deltoid muscle (shoulder). Concerns raised by him and other doctors like Pierre Kory, Ryan Cole, and Dr. McCullough were met with mockery and fact-checking.
2.  **Early 2022 (Alden et al. study):** A study published in February 2022 demonstrated *in vitro* (in the laboratory) that vaccine MRNA permanently incorporates into human liver cells. Dr. Thorp stresses this was a concerning finding from Northern Europe.
3.  **Later 2022 (Hanna et al. study):** Another study showed that the MRNA vaccine, packaged in lipid nanoparticles, is not only distributed throughout the body but is also excreted *intact* in breast milk. This was deemed "very concerning."
4.  **2023 (Hanna et al. follow-up):** The same authors published another study verifying previous findings, and further demonstrated that vaccine MRNA crosses the placenta and enters the fetal blood system in 100% of tested patients, distributing throughout the fetus. It was also found in the decidua (lining of the uterus) and was "bioactive," meaning it actively affected the cells. Dr. Thorp calls this an "abomination of science" and "assault and battery and murder on a genocidal level," stating the vaccines should never have been rolled out in pregnancy without long-term studies.
5.  **Preliminary data (Michaela Reger, Canada):** Dr. Thorp presents preliminary evidence suggesting that vaccine MRNA is *reverse transcribed* in vivo (in living organisms) into human DNA, specifically in gametes (ova and sperm), potentially causing permanent genetic alteration. He expects this to be definitively proven by the end of the year.

Jim Ferguson expresses shock and asks Dr. Thorp for advice to young women who are vaccinated and considering pregnancy, or are already pregnant. Dr. Thorp's advice is stark:
1.  **Distrust:** Do not trust governments (UK, USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand), doctors, nurses, politicians, medical journals, or hospitals. He states they have accepted millions in bribes to push this "lethal narrative."
2.  **Seek Alternatives:** Get out of the mainstream healthcare system unless it's an emergency.
3.  **The Wellness Company:** He promotes "The Wellness Company" (twc.com) as a parallel healthcare system where patients can receive consultations from trusted physicians like himself. He vouches for their commitment to the physician-patient relationship and refusal to accept "dirty blood money."
4.  **Supplements & Testing:** For those who have been vaccinated, he recommends waiting at least six months before trying to conceive. During this time, repair the immune system by taking supplements:
    *   Vitamin D (levels to top normal, ~80; with lab testing due to variable absorption).

    *   Iodine.
    *   Other important supplements, particularly "spike support" products from The Wellness Company that contain natural ingredients like nattokinase, proven to degrade spike protein and reduce plaque in arteries. He notes their 1000 patients had zero complications and double-digit improvements in cholesterol.
5.  **Hope:** He acknowledges that 70% of vaccinated individuals may do well, at least for now, but 30% experience significant adverse effects, with a subset of those facing severe consequences or death. He emphasizes there is hope through these alternative approaches and faith in a creator, but not in those who pushed the vaccines.

Jim Ferguson reiterates his concern and references a British MP, Andrew Bridgen, who is raising similar issues in Parliament, highlighting the conflict of interest of Prime Minister Rishi Sunak investing in Moderna and then directing public funds to them while refusing to address vaccine-related excess deaths. He mentions Barry Young, the New Zealand whistleblower, as another "hero."

Jim Ferguson concludes by thanking Dr. Thorp for his "brave and courageous stand" against "the evil that's in this world." He ends by quoting the saying, "The wheels of justice grind exceedingly slow, but they grind exceedingly fine. Justice will come. And these people who have sold out humanity for money, power, or whether they were blackmailed or corrupted, they will pay a terrible price when justice is done. And it will be done." tweetText:
“IT’S A MEDICINAL HERB, IT’S NOT A DRUG.”

Super Bowl Champion @JimMcMahon explains why he wants President Trump to reclassify marijuana. https://t.co/G5rlWbRX8U

caption.video0:
The video features two men, presumably a host and a guest, in a studio setting. The host, wearing a dark blue suit and tie, is on the left, while the guest, a bald man with sunglasses wearing a red t-shirt with "TRUMP MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!" printed on it, is on the right. Behind them is a large screen displaying graphics.

Here's a detailed breakdown of the video content:

*   **0:00-0:03**: The segment begins with a wide shot of the two men. The guest, Jim McMahon, is speaking. On the screen behind them, a stylized "THE WILL CAIN SHOW" logo is visible, with a city skyline in the background. The Fox News logo and "LIVE" indicator are in the top left, and a lower third banner reads "JIM MCMAHON WANTS TO RECLASSIFY MARIJUANA". McMahon says, "...I think it's ridiculous to have Fentanyl scheduled 2 right now. Fentanyl has killed millions of people."
*   **0:03-0:06**: The graphic on the screen changes to "SOME SCHEDULE 1 DRUGS" with a list: "MARIJUANA", "HEROIN", "LSD", "ECSTASY (MDMA)", and "FENTANYL". A graphic of a syringe labeled "DEA" is at the bottom right of the list. McMahon continues, "Cannabis has killed nobody."
*   **0:06-0:08**: The camera cuts to a close-up of McMahon. He gestures with his hands as he speaks. The lower third remains the same: "JIM MCMAHON WANTS TO RECLASSIFY MARIJUANA". McMahon says, "You know, we all have a cannabinoid system in our bodies."
*   **0:08-0:12**: McMahon continues, "We don't have an alcohol or an opioid system, we have a cannabinoid system. So we're supposed to be using this plant..."
*   **0:12-0:17**: The lower third changes to "JIM MCMAHON | 2X SUPER BOWL CHAMPION QUARTERBACK" and "JIM MCMAHON WANTS TO RECLASSIFY MARIJUANA". The time indicator "3:40 CT" appears. McMahon adds, "...and we got to get it de-, you know, it's been demonized for so long..."
*   **0:17-0:21**: McMahon continues speaking. The lower third text changes to "TRUMP LOOKING AT RECLASSIFYING MARIJUANA". McMahon says, "...and people are starting to realize now, hey, this this stuff actually, you know, might work. It's a medicinal herb, it's not a drug."
*   **0:21-0:23**: The host, Will Cain, begins speaking, looking at McMahon. He says, "You were telling me throughout your career..."
*   **0:23-0:26**: The shot returns to a wide view, showing the "SOME SCHEDULE 1 DRUGS" list. Cain continues, "...marijuana was a use for you. You used it throughout your career. I think you said you started when you were 13."
*   **0:26-0:31**: McMahon responds, nodding, "Yeah, I first... I said 13, yes."
*   **0:31-0:39**: Cain continues to speak, gesturing with his hands. The lower third changes to "TRUMP LOOKING AT RECLASSIFYING MARIJUANA" and the time changes to "1:40 PT". Cain says, "Now, we can talk about that, but you said the other thing, what I'm really focused on and why this is something that you think about is, you were popping pills all throughout your career in the NFL."
*   **0:39-0:42**: McMahon responds, "Yeah, I probably ate painkillers for 35, 40 years."
*   **0:42-0:49**: The camera cuts back to a close-up of McMahon. He explains, "And it was just, uh, all it did is mask pain, it doesn't doesn't help you at all. Whereas cannabis actually, it heals you from the inside out."
*   **0:49-0:55**: The lower third changes again to "JIM MCMAHON | 2X SUPER BOWL CHAMPION QUARTERBACK" and "TRUMP LOOKING AT RECLASSIFYING MARIJUANA". The time changes to "4:41 ET". McMahon continues, "And that that got me off all the pills and I haven't taken a pill in years."
*   **0:55-1:01**: McMahon gestures with his hands and looks thoughtful. He says, "Uh, you know, it's just, it's just something that I, when I first tried it, I said, 'This is for me.' I don't know what it what it is about it, but, you know, this works for me."
*   **1:01-1:09**: The shot returns to the wide view with the "SOME SCHEDULE 1 DRUGS" list. Cain asks, "And you think one of the reasons that we've seen this demonization, again, this is the conversations you and I have been having, is that you feel in part, it's business. It's the pharmaceutical industry."
*   **1:09-1:15**: The camera cuts to a close-up of McMahon. He replies, "Oh, there's no doubt about it. You know, Big Pharma pretty much runs this country."
*   **1:15-1:21**: The lower third changes to "TRUMP LOOKING AT RECLASSIFYING MARIJUANA" with the time "2:41 MT". McMahon says, "And, uh, they demonized it years ago and they've, you know, the government's had a a patent on marijuana for over a hundred years. They know how good it is. So..."
*   **1:21-1:30**: The shot returns to the wide view. Cain says, "Now, just to have the other side of this conversation because I'd imagine there's many people watching right now that disagree with you a lot about marijuana. I'm sure there's people that disagree with everyone about everything."
*   **1:30-1:37**: Cain continues, "Right. Um, it can't just be a wonder drug, Jim. There's got to be a downside, in everything in life. There is a downside, no matter what it is."
*   **1:37-1:43**: Cain asks, "Are you concerned at all with an endorsement of this drug that we will see more of whatever that may be? Whatever is the downside?"
*   **1:43-1:55**: The camera cuts back to a close-up of McMahon. He shakes his head slightly and says, "Well, I haven't found a downside yet. And, uh, I think people that are starting to use it are starting to realize, hey, this stuff is actually I feel better when I use this rather than the pills."

The video opens with a close-up of a blonde woman in a white halter-neck top. She is applying a large, gold-toned, fan-shaped earring to her left ear, which also has several smaller gold hoop earrings and a star-shaped stud. Her blonde hair is styled with a middle part, and some strands frame her face. She has dramatic winged eyeliner and long eyelashes. A black wristband and a thin gold chain bracelet with leaf-shaped charms are visible on her left wrist, and a simple gold chain necklace is around her neck.

As she says, "Okay, I've been wearing this wig all night," she continues to adjust the earring. The words "BEEN" and "MALE SPACES" appear on screen at 0:00.

At 0:01, she is still adjusting the earring, and the word "WIG" appears, followed by "NIGHT" at 0:02.

At 0:03, she frowns slightly, and the words "IT'S" and "HURTS" appear at 0:04, coinciding with her speaking about the wig being "itchy, it hurts."

From 0:05 to 0:06, she uses both hands to pull at the sides of her hair near her temples, as if adjusting something. The word "ARE" appears at 0:05. She says, "We are going to remove this wig."

At 0:07, she is pulling at her hairline near her left temple, and the word "YEP" appears as she says, "Yep." She then makes a gesture with her fingers along her hairline as she says, "Tonight we told some people that these were actually wigs," with the words "TONIGHT" at 0:09, "SOME" at 0:10, and "THESE" at 0:11 appearing on screen.

At 0:12, she is pulling on the hair at her temple, and the word "ACTUALLY" appears. She continues, "and they actually didn't believe us, even when looking up close to them." The word "BELIEVE" appears at 0:13 and "LOOKING" at 0:14.

At 0:15, she dramatically lifts the blonde wig from her head, revealing a completely bald scalp. She smiles broadly. The word "THEM" appears on screen.

At 0:16, she runs her hand over her bald head, still smiling, and says, "they didn't believe us. And I was like, wow." The word "DIDN'T" appears.

At 0:17, she looks directly at the camera and winks, with the word "LIKE" appearing on screen.

From 0:18 to 0:20, she takes a white tissue and wipes her forehead and the top of her bald head, removing what appears to be glue residue. She says, "Now I just have to get the remainder glue off of the top of my head and take off the makeup that I have in my face." The words "GET" at 0:18, "OFF" at 0:19, and "HEAD" at 0:20 appear.

At 0:21, she continues wiping her head, and the word "MAKEUP" appears.

At 0:22, she starts wiping her right eye area with the tissue. The word "FACE" appears. She says, "Let's get rid of these eyebrows."

At 0:23, she continues wiping her right eye, and the word "RID" appears.

At 0:24, she wipes her right eyebrow with the tissue, completely removing the drawn-on eyebrow makeup. The word "EYEBROWS" appears.

At 0:25, she smiles, showing her now absent right eyebrow. She says, "One swipe, they're gone." The words "SWIPE" at 0:25 and "GONE" at 0:26 appear.

At 0:27, she moves to wipe her left eyebrow with the tissue, and the words "OTHER" and "EYEBROW" appear.

At 0:28, she quickly wipes away her left eyebrow makeup.

At 0:29, she looks at the camera, smiling, with both eyebrows gone. She says, "One swipe, she's gone." The words "ONE" at 0:29 and "SWIPE" at 0:30 appear.

At 0:30, with both eyebrows removed, she smiles and says, "But yeah, that's it. Love." The words "BUT" at 0:30 and "LOVE" at 0:31 appear. The video ends with her smiling. Throughout the video, the words "MALE SPACES" are consistently present at the bottom of the screen, underneath the other overlaying text, without a clear audible connection to the spoken content.

tweetText:
Like Sweden, New Zealand is about to become smoke free.

The video opens with a New Zealand flag proudly waving from a flagpole against a backdrop of a soft, hazy sunset or sunrise. The sky is a mix of light blue and soft pink and orange hues, with a bright sun peeking from the bottom left corner. The flag features a blue field with the Union Jack in the canton and four red stars with white outlines forming the Southern Cross constellation on the fly.

As the flag is shown, a male voice states, "New Zealand is set to become one of the first and only countries in the world to achieve official smoke-free status."

At the 0:02 mark, the video transitions to a circular blue frame against the same sky background, revealing a golden trophy with a star embossed on its body. The frame then expands to fill the screen with a solid blue background, and the trophy is centered.
At 0:04, yellow text appears over the trophy: "OFFICIAL SMOKE FREE STATUS".
At 0:07, another line of yellow text appears below the first: "WHERE LESS THAN 5% OF ADULTS SMOKE CIGARETTES".
The male voice continues, "where less than 5% of adults smoke cigarettes."

At 0:10, the background changes to solid yellow, and light blue text appears: "MINISTRY OF HEALTH".
At 0:11, blue text appears below it: "LED CAMPAIGNS".
The male voice says, "This is down to Ministry of Health led campaigns,"

At 0:12, the screen shows a partial view of a webpage. The top left corner is a black circle with a white "MINISTRY OF HEALTH" logo and text that reads "Health New Zealand Te Whatu Ora." The main part of the webpage shows a smiling young woman with dark hair wearing a brown jacket and a black turtleneck. She stands by a body of water with hills in the background. Large text on the page reads, "VAPING CAN HELP YOU QUIT SMOKING." Below it, smaller text says, "Want to switch to vaping."
The male voice adds, "like VAPE TO QUIT STRONG."

At 0:15, a woman with short grey hair and glasses, wearing a green striped shirt over a white top, is shown from the waist up. She is speaking, looking slightly to her left. She is outdoors, possibly on a rooftop or balcony, with lounge furniture and glass windows visible behind her. A blue box with the text "Nancy Loucas" appears in the bottom left corner. She states, "They legalized vaping and the smoking rate went down from 16% down to 6.8% in the last eight years."

At 0:17, the scene cuts to a panel discussion. Seven people are seated in yellow armchairs on a stage with a large blue screen behind them. A woman stands at a podium on the left, addressing the panel. The blue screen displays "SMOKE FREE SWEDEN 2023" in the top right corner and a large profile picture of a man with the title "Dr. Anders Milton" and a description of his professional experience.

At 0:27, "WWW." appears in white, followed by ".SMOKEFREESWEDEN.ORG" at 0:29, also in white.
The male voice concludes, "To learn more, visit www.smokefreesweden.org."

caption.video0:
The video opens with a young woman, identified by the TikTok handle "@rileygaines," sitting at a wooden table in what appears to be a home with exposed wooden beams and a stone fireplace featuring deer antlers on the wall. She is wearing a light grey sweatshirt with red lettering and has her blonde hair tied up in a messy bun.

At **0:00**, she holds up a white letter, shaking it slightly, and says, "Just got another letter from immigration services."
She then places the letter down at **0:02**, touching her forehead with her hand and looking frustrated as she says, "I've made a video about this before."
From **0:05** to **0:18**, she explains her background: "but I'm a US citizen, born and raised in Tennessee, went to college at University of Kentucky. Met my husband there. He's an immigrant. He moved over to the US for college on an athletic scholarship from England."
At **0:18**, she holds up two fingers, then three, then four, while saying, "We've been married almost three years now. We're coming up on three years. So you would think he is well on his way to achieving his his citizenship or at least having his green card, right?"
She shakes her head and gestures with her hand from **0:27** to **0:28**, stating, "No, wrong."
From **0:28** to **0:36**, she leans forward, resting her chin on her hands, and asks, "And why can't he get his green card? Because his medical records aren't complete. And what is missing from his medical record? The COVID vaccine."
She then raises her hands in disbelief at **0:37**, exclaiming, "Like, it is the year 2025, and we are still requiring the COVID vaccine."
At **0:43**, she holds the letter up close to the camera, pointing to a section of text with her red-painted fingernail. The text is mirrored but clearly visible, stating "COVID-19 vaccine." She comments, "You can see it right here. It's backwards, disregard. Complete the COVID-19 vaccine."
She lowers the paper at **0:50**, sighs, and looks exasperated, resting her hand on her forehead.
From **0:53** to **0:57**, she gestures with her hands, saying, "But this is what they do. This is what they do to people who do it the right way."
At **0:57**, she clasps her hands together and begins explaining the financial burden, with a text overlay appearing at **0:59** that reads "more than $10,000." She says, "I can't even tell you how much money, thousands of dollars we have spent on the different forms and applications and and immigration lawyers and all of the different things."
She continues at **1:08**, gesturing with a frustrated expression, "All for everything to expire on their time anyways."
At **1:12**, she points her finger to address critics: "And for all you people who say, 'Oh, Riley, your husband's here illegally, he should be deported,' just say you don't understand immigration."
From **1:18** to **1:25**, she clarifies, "He is very much here legally. There are other ways to obtain residency than a green card, like visas or advanced parole."
At **1:25**, she addresses another group of commenters, touching her forehead: "Or those of you who say, 'This is what you get when you voted for Donald Trump.'" She then clarifies, "Obviously, my husband can't vote, but if he could, he would have voted for Donald Trump."
From **1:33** to **1:43**, she explains her political views, stating, "Changes coming with the current administration, but he has to undo everything that the former administration has done to our immigration system, both legally and illegally."
Finally, at **1:43**, she points her finger and makes a concluding statement: "Oh, and when they campaigned on the tagline, my body, my choice, this is proof they never really meant that."
The video ends with the TikTok loading screen and logo at **1:50**.

caption.video0:
The video features a news anchor, Roman Balmakov from The Epoch Times, presenting information about a study on vaccine-associated heart injury. The visual presentation includes the anchor in a news studio setting with a city skyline background, along with graphic overlays displaying the news topic and excerpts from an article.

Here's a detailed breakdown:

**0:00 - 0:26:** The video opens with Roman Balmakov, a male news anchor wearing glasses and a suit with a patterned tie, speaking directly to the camera. He is holding a white piece of paper and a pen. Behind him is a blurred blue background featuring a city skyline at night.
On the right side of the screen, a graphic with the title "HEART DAMAGE" is displayed. Below his name and affiliation ("ROMAN BALMAKOV, Host, The Epoch Times"), the "FACTS MATTER" logo is visible.
The anchor states, "According to a brand new peer-reviewed study, which came out of the country of Switzerland, a staggering one in 35 recipients of the Moderna booster shot experienced vaccine-associated heart injury. One in 35 people who took the shot experienced damage to the heart. Now, that is obviously a very shocking number."

**0:26 - 0:38:** The scene transitions to a white webpage displaying an article with the title "Subclinical Heart Damage More Prevalent Than Thought After Moderna Vaccination: Study." The author is identified as "Zachary Stieber, Reporter," and the date as "Jul 26 2023." A small thumbnail image of a hand holding a vaccine vial is visible.
The "FACTS MATTER" logo remains in the bottom right corner, and "THE EPOCH TIMES" logo appears briefly in the top right.
A section titled "Study Methods" is highlighted in yellow as the anchor reads: "Researchers posited that the incidence of vaccine-associated heart injury was more prevalent than previously thought following messenger RNA booster vaccination because of a lack of symptoms or mild symptoms."

**0:38 - 1:17:** The video returns to Roman Balmakov in the news studio. He continues to speak, gesturing with his hands and holding the pen and paper. The "HEART DAMAGE" graphic and channel logos are still present.
He explains, "And so, essentially, their hypothesis going into the study was that after getting an mRNA booster shot, a lot more people were experiencing vaccine-related heart damage than previously thought. And the reason for this was because people's hearts were getting damaged in a way where either there were no symptoms on the surface or the symptoms were mild enough for people to ignore, meaning that the heart was actually injured, but the recipient of the booster shot was simply unaware. And so, what these researchers did was that instead of just asking people how they felt after vaccination, they actually went in and they tested their blood."

**1:17 - 1:36:** The video transitions back to the webpage displaying the "Study Methods" section of the article. Another paragraph is highlighted in yellow.
The anchor reads the highlighted text: "They defined injury as a sharp increase in high-sensitivity cardiac troponin T on the third day after vaccination without evidence of an alternative cause. The levels of cardiac troponin had to hit the upper limit of normal, 8.9 nanograms per liter in women and 15.5 nanograms per liter in men."

**1:36 - 1:51:** The video returns to Roman Balmakov in the news studio. The "HEART DAMAGE" graphic and channel logos are still present.
He elaborates, "Now, the reason that these researchers decided to use what's known as a troponin test is because it is a very good indicator of whether or not there was damage to the heart. If a person has more of this protein called troponin T in the bloodstream, it means that recently they've experienced damage to the heart."

**1:51 - 1:59:** The video transitions to an outro screen. A cartoon fox in a suit and tie, inside a circular orange and yellow emblem, is centered against a blurred background of a forest with tall green trees and mossy ground.
Text overlays appear:
*   "@VIGILANTFOX" at the top center.
*   "SHARE VIDEO" with a refresh-like arrow icon on the left.
*   "HIT THE BELL" with a bell icon on the right.
*   An arrow curves from the bottom left, pointing to a purple "Subscribe" button that appears at the bottom center.
The sound of birds chirping and ambient forest sounds are heard.

caption.video0:
The video features a man speaking to the camera, with visual backgrounds changing to illustrate his points.

**0:00-0:16:** The video opens with a medium close-up of a man with short brown hair and a beard, wearing a brown t-shirt, positioned in the bottom-left corner of the screen. Behind him, neatly stacked vertically against a light blue background, are six tubes of "Colgate Cavity Protection" toothpaste, which are primarily red with blue and white lettering. A small "TikTok @herballaw" watermark is visible in the bottom right corner.

The man speaks, and corresponding text overlays appear on the screen:
*   "SO I HEARD A LOT OF TALK ABOUT THE POTENTIAL DANGERS OF FLUORIDE." (0:01-0:03)
*   "SO I WENT TO COLGATE'S WEBSITE WHERE THEY HAVE FLUORIDE TOOTHPASTE TO SEE WHAT THEY HAVE TO SAY." (0:04-0:07)
*   "NOW KEEP IN MIND NINE OUT OF TEN DENTISTS RECOMMEND COLGATE TOOTHPASTE" (0:08-0:11)
*   "AND TEN OUT OF TEN DENTISTS ARE OUT OF BUSINESS IF YOUR TEETH ARE HEALTHY." (0:12-0:15)
*   "DON'T THINK ABOUT THAT TOO HARD." (0:15-0:16)

The man explains his research into fluoride dangers, mentioning Colgate's website. He then uses a sarcastic tone to highlight the "nine out of ten dentists" marketing claim, adding, "and ten out of ten dentists are out of business if your teeth are healthy. Don't think about that too hard."

**0:16-0:29:** The background changes to a screenshot of a webpage. The top portion of the screenshot shows a young boy, reflected in a mirror, brushing his teeth in a bathroom. Below this image is a red banner with white text that reads: "Can You Have Too Much Fluoride? Get The Facts". Further down on the webpage, white text against a white background is visible, discussing fluoride. The man remains in the bottom-left corner, reacting to the text.

New text overlays appear, synchronized with his speech:
*   "CAN YOU GET TOO MUCH FLUORIDE? GET THE FACTS FROM COLGATE SELLING FLUORIDE." (0:17-0:20)
*   "IT'S CALLED NATURE'S CAVITY FIGHTER," (0:21-0:22)
*   "AND IT'S ESPECIALLY GOOD FOR DEVELOPING TEETH A.K.A. CHILDREN." (0:23-0:25)
*   "OVERALL THE MINERAL IS SAFE AND EFFECTIVE." (0:26-0:28)
*   "SAFE AND EFFECTIVE. WHERE HAVE I HEARD" (0:28-0:29)

The man sarcastically comments on "getting the facts from Colgate selling fluoride." He highlights phrases from the webpage like "nature's cavity fighter" and "especially good for developing teeth, aka children." He then repeatedly says "safe and effective," trailing off as if trying to recall something.

**0:29-0:46:** The background transitions to a blurred, gray-toned image depicting an outdoor scene where several individuals in full white hazmat suits are working around what appears to be a chemical spill. One person in a blue suit is also visible further back. The man is still in the bottom-left, maintaining his sarcastic tone.

More text overlays appear:
*   "WHERE HAVE I HEARD NOW WHEN THIS VERY SAFE CHEMICAL SPILLS" (0:30-0:32)
*   "THIS IS WHO COMES TO CLEAN IT UP." (0:32-0:34)
*   "THIS IS A SCREENSHOT FROM AN INCIDENT IN OHIO WHERE A GIGANTIC TRUCK OF FLUORIDE SPILLED ALL OVER THE GROUND." (0:35-0:38)
*   A brief, nearly subliminal text overlay "HYDROFLUOROSILICIC ACID ANOTHER NAME FOR FLUORIDE" flashes on the screen at 0:38.
*   "NOW IT DOES CORRODE CONCRETE AND CEMENT AND WILL DEGRADE" (0:39-0:41)
*   "LITERALLY EAT A WHOLE THROUGH A ROAD IF IT'S SPILLED" (0:41-0:44)
*   "BUT IT'S FOR YOUR TEETH. TRUST ME." (0:44-0:46)

The man links the "safe and effective" claim to the hazmat scene, stating that the image is from an incident in Ohio where a truck of fluoride (which he specifies as Hydrofluorosilicic Acid) spilled. He sarcastically points out that this chemical "corrodes concrete and cement" and can "literally eat a hole through a road," concluding with a highly sarcastic "but it's for your teeth, trust me." The video ends as he finishes this statement.

caption.video0:
The video features a man with long brown hair and a beard, showcasing a custom-made prosthetic eye that functions as a flashlight. An upbeat, electronic/techno music track plays throughout the entire video.

0:00-0:02: The video opens with a close-up of the man's face. His left eye is a natural brown, while his right eye, a prosthetic, emits a bright, glowing white light, like a powerful LED. Yellow text appears at the bottom: "I lost my eye to cancer so I made this and turned my head into a flashlight."

0:02-0:05: The man steps back slightly, revealing more of his upper body. Initially, both of his prosthetic eyes (left and right from the viewer's perspective) glow brightly. Then, the light from his left prosthetic eye turns off, leaving only the right one glowing. The text remains on screen.

0:05-0:09: The scene cuts to a dark room. The man's glowing prosthetic eye is now visible, casting a strong beam of light. He turns his head, and the light reflects intensely off a surface, momentarily creating a bright flare in the camera.

0:09-0:12: The man walks around a dark hallway, using the light from his prosthetic eye to illuminate the walls and the doorframe ahead. The beam is focused and bright.

0:12-0:18: The man continues to walk and shine the light, demonstrating its reach and intensity. Yellow text appears: "Many of you have asked how well it functions as a flashlight." The light beam is clearly visible and powerful.

0:18-0:26: The man continues walking through the dark space, the bright beam from his eye illuminating his path and surroundings. New yellow text appears: "Battery life: Roughly 20 hours as a flashlight, no it does not get hot."

0:26-0:36: The man continues to showcase the light, which appears very bright. The screen then splits vertically. On the left, the man's eye is still glowing brightly. On the right, a hand is shown holding a small, metallic, circular object. This object lights up with a green glow from a small LED within it. Yellow text reads: "This is half of my maximum power output. Safety is paramount, I will work my way to full power eventually." The man's eye light briefly flickers on the left side, then dims. The green light on the right also turns off.

0:36-0:45: The split screen disappears, and the scene returns to a full view of the dark room. The light from the man's prosthetic eye is now much dimmer, casting a softer, warm glow. New yellow text appears: "I like to call it my Titanium Skull Lamp. It's perfect for reading in the dark as well!" The man is seen sitting, holding an open book, and the soft light from his eye effectively illuminates the pages for reading. The light briefly brightens intensely before dimming back down for reading.

0:45-0:50: The man continues to read by the soft light of his prosthetic eye. Yellow text appears: "Power source and all hardware is safely contained inside the prosthetic eye." The light then brightens intensely again.

0:50-0:56: The bright light from the prosthetic eye is shown, almost blinding. It then slowly fades to black, signifying the end of the demonstration. Final yellow text appears: "See my page for other types of cyborg eyes I've made for myself 👁️". The video ends on a black screen with the text.

The man is extremely sweaty, with beads of sweat visible glistening on his back and arms, indicating a high level of exertion. He is wearing black shorts. His head is pressed into the towel on the Bosu ball, acting as a pivot point for the exercise. His hands are clasped firmly behind his lower back, showcasing significant core strength and balance as he performs a pike push-up or a similar inverted bodyweight movement.

He repeatedly lowers his upper body forward and then pushes back up, making his chest and shoulders move closer to and further from the Bosu ball, while his head maintains contact with the towel. His body forms an inverted V-shape, with his legs extended straight behind him, providing stability.

In the background, other gym equipment such as a "HOIST" branded machine and what appears to be part of a weight rack are visible. The feet of another person can also be seen briefly in the background.

The audio features a consistent percussive electronic beat throughout the video, typical of gym background music. There are also faint male voices speaking in Spanish intermittently. For example, a voice says "No, no, no" around the 0:01 mark, and further Spanish phrases are heard around 0:02-0:03 and 0:06-0:07, as well as a brief interjection around 0:09. The exercise is performed continuously with these background sounds.

She says, "it's called Methylene Blue," and the text on the screen changes to reflect this. She adds that "it's been blowing up on the internet lately, so let's do a deep dive on it."

From 0:10, she begins to explain the substance. Text appears on screen: "methylene blue is a synthetic compound that functions as both a medication and a dye." She continues to explain that "it's the same stuff used in the lab for steaming cells in microscopy."

At 0:22, the text updates to: "so learning memory and focus." She then adds, "it's also an antioxidant that will protect your cells and your brain."

caption.video0:
The video begins at **00:00** with a view of a highway checkpoint at a state line, showing a truck passing by. On the left, a digital sign displays "CHECKPOINT AHEAD" and "SPEED 55 MPH" in red text. The bottom of the screen has a red news banner that reads "CORONAVIRUS PANDEMIC" and "MANDATORY CHECKPOINT AT STATE LINE ALABAMA-FLORIDA." Upbeat, tense news music plays.

At **00:02**, the camera zooms in on the digital sign, making the text clearer. A narrator states, "It's a travel checkpoint unlike anything most Americans have ever seen."

From **00:04**, an aerial shot reveals a long line of cars and trucks backed up on a multi-lane highway, stretching for miles through a forested area. The narrator comments, "Backing up traffic for miles."

At **00:08**, another aerial view shows the extensive traffic queue on the highway, with a body of water or marshland visible in the distance. The narrator continues, "Yesterday, we saw huge delays there. Some people waiting hours to get to that checkpoint."

At **00:12**, an aerial shot captures the checkpoint itself, with orange traffic cones delineating lanes and several large trucks navigating through. A white utility truck and an overpass are also visible. "firstcoastnews.com" is displayed in the top-left corner.

From **00:17**, the scene switches to a live shot of reporter Kelly Foster, wearing a red top and turquoise necklace, standing in front of a temporary setup with a tent, a white truck, tables, and cones. She holds a microphone and says, "Kim, no state border checkpoints in Alabama, but as you can see, here in Florida, Governor Ron DeSantis issued an executive order." On-screen text includes "LIVE," "KELLY FOSTER @KELLY_WPMI," "CORONAVIRUS PANDEMIC," and "FLORIDA SCREENING STATE VISITORS PENSACOLA."

At **00:22**, Governor Ron DeSantis is shown at a press conference, speaking into microphones. An American flag is to his left, and the Florida state flag to his right. An American Sign Language interpreter, Alagos Clemente, is signing next to him. On-screen text reads "MAR. 17, 2020 10:49 AM ET LIVE," "Press Conference on the Coronavirus - COVID-19 Tallahassee, Florida," and a news ticker "4 UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA STUDENTS TEST POSITIVE FOR COVID-19." DeSantis states, "I am recommending to the Board of Governors, who are going to talk to the university presidents, um about going to remote learning for the rest of the spring semester at all Florida universities."

The scene continues with DeSantis at **00:33**, as he adds, "restaurants, uh, we're going to do similar to what California did. Bars and nightclubs, uh, effective 5:00 p.m. today, um, you know, they're going to be suspended for 30 days. I worked with, um, Miami Beach Mayor and Fort Lauderdale,"

At **00:47**, the screen splits. The left side shows DeSantis continuing his speech with the interpreter. The right side displays an aerial view of a crowded beach with many people. DeSantis concludes, "supported their closures of some of their beaches."

From **00:50**, a female news anchor in a floral dress is shown against a blue background, speaking to the camera. A graphic on the right displays "CORONAVIRUS PANDEMIC" with a stylized virus image. She reports, "More restrictions put in place tonight in Miami Beach. The entertainment district between 5th and 15th streets is getting an 8:00 p.m. curfew."

At **00:57**, Governor DeSantis is sitting at a wooden desk in an office, speaking directly to the camera. An American flag and framed photos are visible. He says, "we're going to suspend for two weeks any vacation, new vacation rentals in the state of Florida." A news ticker is partially visible, showing "THOSE POSITIONS, THEY WILL BE ELIGIBLE FOR CALL-BACK." and "CENTRAL FLORIDA CO".

At **01:04**, a narrator says, "The governor issuing Executive Order 20-87," as DeSantis continues to speak.

From **01:07**, a night shot of a Best Western hotel shows its illuminated exterior and a dark parking lot with orange traffic cones. The narrator finishes, "ordering vacation rental properties to suspend operations." Somber news music plays.

At **01:10**, reporter in a red dress stands on a sidewalk at night, holding a microphone. Headlights of cars are visible in the background. She explains, "I talked with one business owner that said he's tired of hearing about closures. He wants some good news."

At **01:16**, reporter Troy Campbell, wearing a patterned shirt, stands outdoors at night, holding a microphone. The background is dark. On-screen text identifies him and shows "NEW AT 11:00," "NEWS 6," and weather information. A news ticker appears, stating "PEOPLE GATHER AT THE BEACH DESPITE WARNING DAYTONA BEACH SHORES, VOLUSIA COUNTY." Campbell says, "Daytona Beach Shores police say last night their officers went around to all restaurants and bars, handing them a copy of the governor's executive order. They say they're out tonight patrolling to make sure everyone's in compliance."

From **01:27**, the female news anchor from earlier returns, speaking to the camera. A graphic to her right now shows "UNEMPLOYMENT" and "CORONAVIRUS THE FACTS," with a microscope and virus images. She states, "Theme parks, restaurants, retail stores and other businesses are still closed."

The screen goes black at **01:32** with a whooshing sound effect.

Finally, at **01:33**, Governor Ron DeSantis reappears, speaking at a podium against a red background. He smiles and declares, "For those of you who aren't from Florida, welcome to our oasis of freedom." An electronic "blip" sound is heard as the screen goes black again at **01:38**.

At 0:04, the scene shifts slightly. The pharmacist is now looking forward, while another woman with dark hair and glasses (presumably another pharmacist or staff member) looks intently forward as well. Keir Starmer's voiceover states, "We spend quite a lot of time in parliament."

At 0:06, the shot returns to Keir Starmer talking to the pharmacist behind the counter, who nods and says, "Yes." Keir Starmer continues, "Um, and then the rest of the week out and about." A man with a beard and glasses can be seen in the background, holding up his phone as if recording.

At 0:08, Keir Starmer is talking to a different female pharmacist with short, grey curly hair and glasses, wearing a black and white top, standing in an aisle filled with cosmetics. Keir Starmer's voiceover continues, "I like the out and about, talking to people like you."

At 0:18, the shot focuses on two female pharmacists behind a counter in the prescription area. One is putting a small box into a drawer. Sebastian James's voiceover continues, "and it's incredibly easy, you don't have to have an appointment, you just walk in."
