The video is a split-screen news segment from Fox News's "The Ingraham Angle." On the left, Laura Ingraham, a blonde woman in a black sleeveless top, is speaking. Her background shows a blurred cityscape at night. On the right, various video clips are displayed, illustrating her commentary. Below the split-screen, a banner reads "FOX NEWS channel" on the left, "THE INGRAHAM ANGLE" in the center, and a changing headline on the right, along with a smaller "REP BYRON DONALDS MOMENTS AWAY" or "SCHOEN & FLEISCHER MOMENTS AWAY" text.

**00:00-00:08**: Laura Ingraham is speaking. The headline at the bottom reads "DEMS TURN TO POT & ABORTION TO BAIL THEM OUT". The right screen shows a close-up of a hand holding a lit, smoking marijuana joint. Ingraham says, "If you're interested in smoking all that high THC weed that's causing psychosis and schizophrenia, bipolar disorder..." The camera zooms in slightly on the joint.

**00:08-00:17**: Ingraham continues, "...and if you lose sleep at the thought that third-trimester abortions aren't happening..." The B-roll changes to a "FILE" footage of an empty examination room in what appears to be a clinic, featuring an ultrasound machine and an examination table.

**00:17-00:23**: Ingraham states, "Then leftists in Florida have a few ballot initiatives just for you. Amendments three and four are on the ballot November 5th, and um Amendment three legalizes recreational marijuana..." The B-roll switches back to "FILE" footage showing rows of marijuana plants in an indoor grow facility. Then it cuts to gloved hands trimming harvested marijuana plants.

**00:23-00:36**: Ingraham finishes, "...because that's worked out so well in other areas of the country. And Amendment four makes abortion a constitutional right, effectively making it impossible to add any regulations of the procedure at all." The B-roll shows more marijuana plants. Then it transitions to a purple "Vote Vote Vote" sign with an arrow, suggesting a voting booth entrance, followed by a person inserting a ballot into an electronic voting machine.

**00:36-00:53**: The screen transitions to a direct split-screen interview. Laura Ingraham is on the left, and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis is on the right, wearing a blue suit and tie, seated in an office with a US flag and books in the background. The headline changes to "PRO-WEED MEASURE IN FLORIDA BACKED BY HUGE $", with "RON DESANTIS (R) | FLORIDA GOVERNOR" below his image. Ingraham introduces him and asks about Amendment 3, mentioning "big money poured into your state."

**00:53-01:14**: DeSantis responds, "Well, Amendment three is being bankrolled by one big weed company that's traded on the Canadian stock exchange. They have poured over $100 million into passing this Amendment three. They're not doing that because they care about the best interests of Florida, they're doing it because they want to make profits." During this, the B-roll remains on the full screen of DeSantis.

**01:14-01:46**: DeSantis continues, "And people say it's the legalization of recreational weed, that's partially true. They're saying you have a right to possess and smoke it, but only if you buy it from them 'cause they're actually setting up a cartel in the Constitution of Florida. So if you want to grow your own marijuana, that is not in the amendment. They also have nothing in here that would prevent, that would allow any type of limitation on public use. So you look at places like Colorado, people see weed everywhere. Colorado actually has prohibitions against using it in public..." As he speaks, the right-hand screen cycles through various "FILE" clips related to marijuana use: a lit joint, a hand lighting a bong/pipe, someone exhaling smoke, and someone rolling a joint.

**01:46-02:04**: DeSantis finishes, "...Here there is nothing in there. They're saying you have a right to use it with no limitations. So whatever negative has happened in Colorado, I think this particular amendment, the way it's written, I think the problems would be much more significant in Florida as a result of Amendment three." The B-roll continues to show the smoking bong/pipe, then rolling a joint, then exhaling smoke.

**02:04-02:15**: Ingraham says, "Yeah, so everyone has to vote no on that. I've been on this from day one. And the The New York Times, of all places, just published a piece in the last few weeks about the dangers of marijuana use." The right screen shows DeSantis, then switches back to a full screen of DeSantis.

**02:15-02:32**: The B-roll on the right changes to an image of a torn newspaper article. The headline reads "AS AMERICA'S MARIJUANA USE GROWS, SO DO THE HARMS" with a yellow highlight on the first part. The body text states: "The accumulating harm is broader and more severe than previously reported... Again and again, The New York Times found dangerous misconceptions. Many users believe... that people cannot become addicted to cannabis. But millions do. By Megan Twohey, Danielle Ivory and Carson Kessler | Oct 4".

**02:32-02:54**: Ingraham continues, "It went on to talk about those over the age of from 18 to 25. The most damage done to them, governor, uh in terms of mental uh effects of marijuana use because of the high THC levels. I mean, this is just a disaster. It's the last thing our young people need, or frankly, any Americans need uh given the experience in other states." The right screen shows DeSantis. The headline on the bottom changes to "LEGALIZING POT AN 'EXPERIMENT GONE HORRIBLY WRONG'".

**02:54-03:19**: DeSantis responds, "Well, exactly, Laura. I mean, when Colorado did it, it was kind of an issue of first impression. They claimed it would be no black market, big tax revenue, safe and effective, and none of that happened. So now Florida is looking at those results. And it's not just the 18 to 21, uh there's actually a great commercial running now against Amendment 3 with an ER doctor from California. And what she says is she sees very young kids coming in because they package it like candy." During this, the B-roll cycles through more "FILE" clips of marijuana use: a person exhaling smoke, a close-up of a person's arm with a wristband, a hand lighting a joint, another person exhaling smoke, someone handling a pile of what appears to be ground marijuana, and hands rolling a joint.

**03:19-04:05**: DeSantis concludes, "So you think it's sour patch kids, but it's really marijuana. So the experiment has failed. This is not something that's appropriate to put into a constitution. And I think you're right that the no vote on Amendment 3 is what's in the best interest of the state of Florida. We don't want to become California through the back door when you write some some some very uh uh uh, you know, deceptive amendment summary. You don't even put everything that's going to be in it when it's on the ballot, so voters don't get the full picture. And then you sink a hundred million dollars in, and voila, you get to ensconce yourself in the constitution of a state as this big weed cartel. That is bad government. Even if you like marijuana, this is not the appropriate vehicle for it." The right screen shows DeSantis throughout this final statement. The lower right graphic updates to "SEEN AND UNSEEN" with Raymond Arroyo's picture, and then "SCHOEN & FLEISCHER MOMENTS AWAY".

The video captures a segment of a cabinet meeting, featuring President Donald Trump and other officials. The scene is set in a wood-paneled room, with the American flag and what appears to be a presidential seal visible in the background. The Fox News logo and "BREAKING NEWS" chyron are present at the bottom of the screen.

Here's a detailed breakdown:

**0:00-0:12 (Visual: Close-up on Donald Trump)**
* **Visual:** The video opens with a close-up shot of Donald Trump, seated at a long, dark wooden table. He is wearing a dark suit with a white shirt and a bright red tie. His blonde hair is styled in his characteristic way. He listens intently, looking slightly to his left (off-camera). His expression is serious. Behind him, the American flag stands prominently. Other figures are blurred in the background. The on-screen text shows "THE WHITE HOUSE LIVE | 12:41 PM" at the top left, and "FOX NEWS BREAKING NEWS TRUMP TAKES QUESTIONS AT FIRST CABINET MEETING" at the bottom, with a "FOX NEWS ALERT" beneath it.
* **Audio:** A reporter asks, "Mr. President, the measles outbreak in Texas at the moment in which a child is reported to have died. Do you have concerns about that and have you asked Secretary Kennedy to look into that?"

**0:09-0:12 (Visual: Trump responds)**
* **Visual:** Trump turns his head to his right, looking at someone off-camera, his expression still serious.
* **Audio:** Trump says, "Well, why don't we, uh, Bobby, do you want to speak on that?"

**0:12-0:15 (Visual: Wider shot, focus on Kennedy and a woman)**
* **Visual:** The camera cuts to a wider shot, showing several cabinet members seated around the table. In the foreground, a woman with blonde hair in a purple top is visible on the left. Next to her, a man (identified as Robert F. Kennedy Jr. by the chyron) is speaking. He is wearing a blue suit with a light blue patterned tie. Trump is visible further down the table to the right, listening. The chyron changes to "ROBERT F KENNEDY JR | HHS SECRETARY".
* **Audio:** Robert F. Kennedy Jr. begins speaking: "We are following the measles epidemic every day."

**0:15-0:55 (Visual: Wider shot of the cabinet meeting)**
* **Visual:** The shot widens further, showing more of the cabinet members. Robert F. Kennedy Jr. continues to speak, gesturing occasionally with his hands. Trump is visible on the right side of the screen, listening and looking towards Kennedy. The woman in purple is to Kennedy's left. Other cabinet members, including Marco Rubio, are seated along the table. The room has light brown curtains and ornate wall sconces. The American flag and presidential seal are still visible. The chyron at the bottom now reads "NOW: PRESIDENT TRUMP HOLDS FIRST CABINET MEETING" along with "ROBERT F KENNEDY JR | HHS SECRETARY".
* **Audio:** Robert F. Kennedy Jr. continues his explanation: "I think there's 124 people who have contracted measles at this point, mainly in Gaines County, Texas. Um, mainly, we're told, in the Mennonite community. Um, there are two people who have died. Um, but, uh, we're watching it and there, there are about 20 people hospitalized, mainly for quarantine. We're watching it, we're at a post on it yesterday, and we're gonna continue to follow it." He then adds, "Incidentally, there have been four measles outbreaks this year in this country. Last year there were 16. So, it's not unusual. We have measles outbreaks every year."

**0:55-1:03 (Visual: Kennedy speaks, then cut-off)**
* **Visual:** Kennedy is still speaking, making a point with his hand. Trump continues to listen.
* **Audio:** A reporter interjects, "Mr. President?" Kennedy continues, "You sound a little under the weather, sir..." The audio then cuts off abruptly. The video also cuts off.

They never came back."

**0:21 - 0:44:** The video transitions to a split screen. Jordan Peterson, a man with a beard wearing a patterned blazer and a pink shirt with a dark tie, is on the left, sitting in a home-like setting. He listens to Dr. Phil, occasionally nodding and taking notes with a pink pen. Dr. Phil continues to speak from the right side of the screen.
- **Audio:** "And you see what happened. And one of my problems is we do have a generation that is experiencing a mental emotional crisis with the highest levels of anxiety, depression, and loneliness since records have been kept."

**0:44 - 1:23:** The video returns to Dr. Phil speaking on the full screen. He continues to gesture with his pen and hands.
- **Audio:** "And the agencies that keep those records, the CDC and the Department of Education, and others, are the very ones that shut the schools down. They shut the schools down. And as I said early in our conversation, I was fine with that for a couple of weeks, but then it turns into months, and then it turns into a year, and in some cases, it turned into two years with remote learning that they knew did not work, particularly with low socioeconomic and inner city populations who didn't have good WiFi connections, didn't have parents there to help them along the way."

**1:23 - 2:48:** The video switches back to the split screen. Jordan Peterson continues to listen and take notes, maintaining a serious expression. Dr. Phil continues to articulate his points, gesturing with his hands.
- **Audio:** "And when they shut the schools down for that long, they did that knowing these kids were in a mental and emotional crisis. And they knew that those schools were a lifeline to those kids, that they needed that for emotional development. They needed it for educational achievement. They needed it so they had social development, and they shut it down anyway. They also knew that the mandated reporters, the teachers, the counselors, the cafeteria workers, and bus drivers and coaches were at the schools. And those were the ones who had their eyes on these children and could report if they saw signs that the child was being molested or the child was being abused in the home. They shut all of that down. And when they did, those referrals dropped 40% to 50% in some major markets. And these kids were sent home behind closed doors, locked up with the very abusers. It's not that the abuse went down 40 or 50%. It probably went up because of the frustration of being locked up at home behind those doors. And they shut the schools down without any plan for bringing them back. And so, you ask questions about it, and they say, well, we did the best we could with what we knew at the time. No, you did not do the best you could with what you knew at the time. You had information that these children were not as susceptible to this disease as everyone else was."

**2:48 - 3:00:** Dr. Phil is again on the full screen, emphasizing his points with his pen and hands.
- **Audio:** "You knew they were in a mental health crisis. You knew this was their lifeline, and you yanked it out from under them. And you damn well knew what you were doing when you did it."

Oncologist: I've Never Before Seen Cancers Behaving Like This:

Young people, in their 20s, 30s, and 40s, are developing aggressive and rapidly-growing "turbo cancers."

Dr. William Makis, who has diagnosed 20,000 cancer patients in his career, says, "I've never seen anything like this."

The video features President Joe Biden standing at a podium, speaking to an audience. He is wearing a dark blue suit, a white shirt, and a red tie. The podium has the Seal of the President of the United States. On either side of the podium, two American flags are displayed. Behind him, a dark grey banner is visible, adorned with multiple "NABTU" logos and the phrase "Foundations For the Future" repeated several times. In the bottom left corner, the "PBS NEWS HOUR" logo is present.

To the right of the podium, seated in the audience, a man with a beard and wearing a suit with a maroon tie is visible. He is looking towards Biden and frequently reacting to his speech.

**At 0:00**, Joe Biden begins speaking, saying, "By the way..." followed by immediate laughter from the audience.
**From 0:01 to 0:03**, he continues, "...remember when he was trying to deal with Covid..." The man in the audience on the right is seen smiling.
**At 0:03**, Biden delivers a line, "He said, 'Just inject a little bleach in your veins.'" This is met with a wave of laughter and applause from the audience. The man on the right is seen laughing.
**From 0:05 to 0:09**, the laughter and applause continue.
**At 0:09**, Biden adds a punchline: "He missed it, all went to his hair." This triggers a significantly louder and more enthusiastic burst of laughter and applause from the crowd. The man on the right reacts visibly, laughing heartily and starting to clap vigorously.
**Between 0:11 and 0:17**, the audience's laughter and applause peak. Biden smiles broadly, looking around at the cheering crowd. The man on the right continues to clap enthusiastically, with a wide smile on his face.
**At 0:17**, Biden, still smiling, states, "I shouldn't have said that." This remark elicits another wave of laughter and applause. The man on the right is still clapping and smiling.
**From 0:21 to 0:29**, the audience's positive reaction with laughter and applause continues. The man on the right keeps clapping and smiling.
**At 0:29**, Biden reiterates with a chuckle, "Probably shouldn't have said that." More laughter follows.
**At 0:31**, he looks down and then back up, addressing the audience directly: "You guys are a bad influence on me!" This concluding remark is met with further laughter and applause. The man on the right is seen laughing and nodding in agreement.

The video features two young girls inside a car, seemingly during the evening or night, indicated by the car's interior lighting and external darkness. The camera is held by the girl in the foreground, who has long, dark brown hair and is wearing a black top. She's holding a foil balloon shaped like a heart, which is red and silver with pink hearts printed on it.

At the beginning of the video, from 00:00 to 00:01, the girl in the foreground looks at the camera, smiling with an open mouth, and makes a sound like a baby crying (specifically "wah wah"). Simultaneously, the second girl, visible in the background, to the right, with dark curly hair and a white "Hello Kitty" shirt, also opens her mouth wide in a playful scream.

From 00:01 to 00:02, the girl in the foreground closes her mouth, then looks to her right, and closes her eyes, maintaining a smile. The girl in the background continues to open her mouth wide, making a playful screaming sound ("ahhh!").

Between 00:02 and 00:04, the girl in the foreground looks back at the camera, her eyes still closed, and then opens them, looking around. She takes a deep breath, filling the balloon she's holding. The girl in the background closes her mouth and looks at the girl in the foreground.

From 00:04 to 00:07, the girl in the foreground continues to inflate the balloon, her cheeks puffed out. She glances to her right, then back at the camera. The girl in the background smiles, makes a mischievous "hmph" sound, and then places her hand over her mouth as if trying to hold back a laugh.

At 00:07, the girl in the foreground stops inflating the balloon, holding it in front of her. The girl in the background continues to smile mischievously.

From 00:08 to 00:10, the girl in the foreground slowly exhales, letting the air out of her mouth, and her cheeks deflate. She looks down at the balloon, then up at the camera. The girl in the background remains smiling.

At 00:10, the girl in the foreground looks up and to her right, then back to her left. The girl in the background continues to look at her.

From 00:10 to 00:13, the girl in the foreground looks at someone off-camera and smiles, then frowns slightly, her eyes widening in surprise. The girl in the background maintains her smile. An off-camera voice asks "Qué pasó?" (What happened?).

From 00:13 to 00:15, a hand reaches in from the right and gently touches the cheek of the girl in the foreground. She flinches slightly, her expression shifting to surprise. The off-camera voice exclaims "Luanghela, se me pasó! Luanghela!" (Luanghela, I got carried away! Luanghela!). The girl in the background watches intently.

Between 00:15 and 00:18, the girl in the foreground laughs and covers her face with her hand. The hand touching her cheek is still there. The off-camera voice repeats "Luanghela!" and then says "Luanghela!" playfully. The girl in the background laughs and points playfully at the girl in the foreground.

From 00:18 to 00:20, the girl in the foreground lowers her hand from her face, still laughing. The girl in the background laughs and claps her hands together, then places one hand on the girl in the foreground's shoulder.

Between 00:20 and 00:22, the girl in the foreground turns to look at the girl in the background, still with a slight smile. The girl in the background leans forward, looking at the girl in the foreground with a concerned expression.

From 00:22 to 00:24, the girl in the foreground's smile fades, and she looks directly at the girl in the background with a serious expression, as if listening intently. The off-camera voice asks "Qué te pasa?" (What's wrong with you?). The girl in the background's expression shifts from concern to a slight frown.

Between 00:24 and 00:26, the girl in the foreground looks surprised. The off-camera voice repeats "Luanghela, qué te pasa? Qué te pasa de qué?" (Luanghela, what's wrong with you? What do you mean 'what's wrong'?"). The girl in the background looks directly at the camera with a puzzled expression.

From 00:26 to 00:28, the girl in the foreground looks down at the balloon and then back up, still surprised. The off-camera voice states "No te gustan las moradas" (You don't like the purple ones). The girl in the background continues to look at the camera.

Between 00:28 and 00:30, the girl in the foreground looks at the balloon, then back at the girl in the background. The off-camera voice asks "Morada? Si, el chicle es..." (Purple? Yes, the gum is...). The girl in the background's expression is one of slight confusion.

From 00:30 to 00:33, the girl in the foreground frowns slightly, then a hand quickly reaches in and snatches the balloon from her. The girl in the foreground reacts with surprise. The off-camera voice says "Luanghela, qué te pasa? Qué te pasó, Luanghela?" (Luanghela, what's wrong with you? What happened to you, Luanghela?). The girl in the background also looks surprised by the sudden action.

From 00:33 to 00:35, the girl in the foreground looks to her right, her eyes wide, then back toward the camera with a slight frown. The off-camera voice says "No te gustan las moradas!" (You don't like the purple ones!). The girl in the background looks at the camera, then back at the girl in the foreground.

At 00:35, the girl in the foreground shakes her head, then places her hands over her face, running them through her hair. The girl in the background watches, her expression unreadable.

From 00:36 to 00:38, the girl in the foreground lowers her hands from her face, smiling broadly, as if amused by the situation. The off-camera voice says "Deja de hacerte la que te daña" (Stop pretending to be upset). The girl in the background glances at the girl in the foreground and then back to the camera.

- **00:13-00:27**: He then states, "Millions of DNA fragments. And there are people who have religious objections toward those. And those people should be treated with compassion if they come to a hospital and they're sick. We need to know how to treat them."
- **00:27-00:43**: Kennedy explains, "And one of the failures of the CDC in the past is they weren't telling doctors. No, no kid should die of measles in this country. Even in 1963, before the introduction of the vaccine, there were 400 deaths a year and there were up to 2 million measles cases."

The video is divided into three segments, each showing the person eating a different food.

**Segment 1: Eating a flatbread wrap (0:00 - 0:25)**

- **0:15-0:19:** The person continues eating the wrap, taking several bites and chewing.
- **0:19-0:25:** They take the last bite and finish the wrap, showing the small remaining piece.

**Segment 2: Preparing and eating a sandwich (0:25 - 1:20)**
- **0:25-0:26:** The scene changes. The person is now wearing a black t-shirt with white splatters/patterns. They are holding a piece of bread and what looks like a pink slice of meat (possibly bologna or salami). They ask in Turkish, "Abi bitti mi?" (Bro, is it finished?).
- **0:26-0:29:** They continue, "Bitmez." (It won't finish.) They then place small cherry tomatoes onto the bread with their fingers.
- **0:29-0:34:** As they place more tomatoes, they say, "Çeri domatesi de koyacağız. Hayatınızı ve yemeğinizi güzelleştirmek sizin elinizde." (We will also put cherry tomatoes. Making your life and your food beautiful is in your hands.)
- **0:34-0:40:** They carefully arrange more cherry tomatoes on the bread.
- **0:40-0:49:** They continue to add tomatoes. They comment, "Bunu böyle de yiyebilirdik ama...güzelleştireceğiz." (We could have eaten this like this too, but... we will make it beautiful.)
- **0:49-0:55:** The sandwich is fully assembled with multiple slices of pink meat and cherry tomatoes. They hold it up, showcasing it, and say, "Bakın şöyle, güzel bir sandviç yaptık." - **0:55-1:07:** They continue to pack more tomatoes onto the sandwich.
- **1:07-1:12:** They take a very large bite of the thick sandwich. Loud chewing sounds are prominent.
- **1:12-1:20:** They continue chewing and taking more bites, expressing satisfaction with "Mhm."

**Segment 3: Eating noodles (1:20 - 2:09)**

caption.video0:
The video begins with a hand placing a small, rectangular, light gray block onto a dark, textured surface. Text appears on screen, stating, "Imagine this is one injection." The block is labeled "Aluminum" at 0:02.

At 0:03, the hand returns, holding a syringe containing a dark, silvery liquid. Text appears: "Now Imagine this is a second injection." The liquid in the syringe is labeled "Mercury" at 0:06.

The scene transitions at 0:07, and a drill bit descends from above, drilling into the center of the aluminum block. Metallic shavings fly off as the drill creates a hole. Text reads, "Imagine this is An injection Into the human body." The drilling continues until 0:13, when the drill bit retracts, leaving a clean hole in the aluminum.

At 0:14, a hand wearing a blue glove holds the syringe filled with mercury and injects the silvery liquid directly into the hole in the aluminum block. The mercury forms a small, dark bead in the hole. Text appears again: "Now Imagine this is that second injection Into the human body."

From 0:21, the camera focuses on the aluminum block and the injected mercury. A subtle reaction begins, and a white, fibrous, almost fluffy material starts to grow out of the mercury bead and around the hole. The growth quickly expands, forming a dome-like structure as if bubbling up from beneath the surface. Text reads, "Imagine this represents Both injected into the human Body."

The video then shows a time-lapse of this reaction from 0:30 to 0:45. The white, fibrous material grows significantly taller and more complex, resembling a delicate, intricate, almost organic column or stalagmite. It appears to be "eating" the aluminum from the inside out, causing the material to expand and rise. The structure continues to grow, becoming quite substantial and reaching several times the height of the original aluminum block.

At 0:48, new text appears: "Imagine this represents The results of the combination Within the human body." The white, fibrous growth continues to stand tall on the aluminum block.

At 0:56, the scene changes to "After 4 hours." The fibrous growth has somewhat collapsed and spread out across the aluminum block, appearing more brittle and entangled. A gloved hand gently pushes the growth at 0:58, demonstrating its fragility; it flakes and crumbles easily. The underlying aluminum is visibly corroded and pitted where the growth has formed. Text updates to "After just 4 hours" at 1:00.

From 1:03, the gloved hand continues to manipulate the brittle, white growth, peeling it back from the aluminum plate. The plate beneath is visibly damaged and appears much thinner in places, with a rough, eaten-away texture. Text states: "This is what happens When combined."

At 1:07, the text changes to "This is why its spread out Between multiple shots." The hand continues to show the damage to the aluminum.

Finally, at 1:10, the text reads: "This is what happens when you mix mercury with aluminium." The hand tears off a piece of the fibrous growth, further revealing the corroded aluminum. At 1:13, the concluding text appears: "Both extremely toxic when injected, and both used as adjuvants in vaccine." The video ends on this final statement, with the damaged aluminum and the fibrous material remaining on screen.

Throughout the video, a continuous, somber, and somewhat dramatic instrumental track plays, intensifying slightly during the reaction time-lapse and becoming more urgent and percussive towards the end.

tweetText:
🚨🚨🚨 Diagnostic Pathologist @ClareCraigPath presents SHOCKING facts about the #Pfizer #Covid #Vaccine trials for children👇😳 :

“There is an awful lot about this trial that has SHOCKED me, and will probably SHOCK you too!!”

“The trial recruited 4526 children, aged from 6 months to 4 years old - 3000 children did NOT make it to the END of the trial!!😳 That’s a HUGE number!! There should be an answer for that!? Without an answer, this trial should be NULL and VOID!!!” 

“They DEFINED, ‘Severe #COVID’ as a child that had a SLIGHTLY elevated heart rate or a few more BREATHS PER MINUTE!! On that basis, 6 people from the #Vaccine group had ‘severe #Covid’ and 1 child from the placebo did as well - Based on the applied definition, the #Vaccine is causing ‘Severe #Covid’ at a rate of 6:1”🤔

“One #Vaccinated child was hospitalized during the trial with a fever and seizure”

“How did they define ANY #Covid? Well they absolutely TWISTED the data - They #Vaccinate the children and then wait 3 weeks until they give them their second dose and then wait 8 weeks to give them their third dose - The data shows that the #Vaccinated children were getting #COVID at a much higher rate than the placebo group between doses and they still chose to IGNORE those FACTS!!”😠

“#Pfizer IGNORED 97% of the FACTS in the TRIAL!! In the END, they picked 3 children that had #Covid from the #Vaccinated group and 7 children from the placebo group and then said that their #Vaccine was effective”🧐

So let me get this straight :

(1) They defined ‘Severe’ #Covid as: ‘slightly elevated heart rate’ and ‘more breaths per minute’!?😑 is this a joke?

(2) Did they really use skewed data from 10 out of 4526 children to justify this #Covid #Vaccine for them? 

(3) 3000 children did not make it to the end of the trials? Why is this MASSIVE and disturbing finding NOT being taken into consideration when it come to this #Covid #Vaccine approval for our children? WHAT HAPPENED TO THOSE 3000 KIDS!? 

CONCLUSION :

#Pfizer ignored 97% of the data, and relied on 10 children, 3 from the #Vaccinated group and 7 from the #Placebo group for their conclusion = 10/4526 = 0.22% of the data

Can anyone in the world rely on 0.22% of an entire STORY to make a solid and informed decision? Especially when it comes to injecting our BABIES!? The answer is………ABSOLUTELY NOT!!!! It’s insane!!!! ANDDD even then, the data is heavily SKEWED!!

Why doesn’t the media tell anyone about this? Did anyone hear this on the news before? WHY NOT!?

Imagine what they’ve been doing to other ‘safety’ trials for drug approval? I mean, do you see how they conducted this trial!? it’s OUTRAGEOUS!!!

I wonder how anyone in the world could defend this?

Would you still get you child #Vaccinated for #Covid after knowing these facts?

The video features a woman with light brown hair, wearing glasses and a white cardigan over a dark top. She is seated, speaking directly to the camera, which is framed from her chest up. The background behind her is a dark blue wall with a framed picture on the left and a lamp with a textured lampshade on the right. A circular white sign with the word "HART" in black text is visible above her right shoulder.

Below the video, a dark blue bar displays video controls, including a play/pause button, a progress bar, and a volume icon. Further down, there's a profile picture with a yellow circle and a black symbol, alongside the username "govt.exe is corrupt" and the handle "@govt_corrupt", with "1d" indicating the post was made one day ago. A "Following" button and a three-dot menu icon are also present. Underneath this, a text overlay reads: "BREAKING: Pfizer's court released files shows they manipulated their study & excluded 97% of data in children - where over 60% never finished the tria... more".

Here's a detailed timeline of the video's content:

- **00:00-00:02**: The woman says, "I'm Dr. Clare Craig." This text appears briefly as a subtitle.
- **00:02-00:04**: She continues, "I'm a diagnostic pathologist," with the subtitle changing to reflect this.
- **00:04-00:06**: She adds, "and I'm co-chair of the HART Group." The subtitle updates.
- **00:06-00:08**: The woman states, "and I want to take you through the evidence." The subtitle updates.
- **00:08-00:11**: She continues, "that Pfizer just presented to the FDA on the 6 months to 4 year old children." The subtitle updates.
- **00:11-00:14**: She then says, "There's an awful lot about this trial." The subtitle updates.
- **00:14-00:17**: She elaborates, "that has shocked me and I think will shock you too." The subtitle updates.
- **00:17-00:19**: The woman states, "The trial recruited 4526 children." The subtitle updates.
- **00:19-00:24**: She continues, "aged from six months to four years old." The subtitle updates.
- **00:24-00:27**: She then says, "3000 of these children." The subtitle updates.
- **00:27-00:29**: She adds, "did not make it to the end of the trial." The subtitle updates.
- **00:29-00:32**: The woman says, "That is a huge number, two thirds of them." The subtitle updates.
- **00:32-00:34**: She asks, "Why was there this drop off?" The subtitle updates.
- **00:34-00:36**: She states, "That needs to be answered." The subtitle updates.
- **00:36-00:40**: She continues, "and without an answer to that, on that basis alone, this trial should be deemed null and void." The subtitle updates.
- **00:40-00:42**: She asks, "So what did the trial show?" The subtitle updates.
- **00:42-00:45**: She responds, "Well, they defined severe covid." The subtitle updates.
- **00:45-00:51**: She explains, "as children who had a slightly raised heart rate or a few more breaths per minute." The subtitle updates.
- **00:51-00:57**: She states, "There were 6 children aged 2 to 4 who had severe covid in the vaccine group." The subtitle updates.
- **00:57-00:59**: She contrasts this with, "but only one in the placebo group." The subtitle updates.
- **00:59-01:03**: She concludes, "So on that basis, the likelihood that this vaccine." The subtitle updates.
- **01:03-01:08**: She continues, "is actually causing severe covid is higher than the likelihood that it isn't." The subtitle updates.
- **01:08-01:12**: She adds, "There was actually one child who was hospitalised in this trial, they had a fever and a seizure." The subtitle updates.
- **01:12-01:15**: She clarifies, "They had been vaccinated." The subtitle updates.
- **01:15-01:22**: She then transitions, "So now let's turn to what they defined as any covid, and what they did was to utterly twist the data." The subtitle updates.
- **01:22-01:29**: She explains, "They vaccinated the children and they waited three weeks after the first dose before the second dose." The subtitle updates.
- **01:29-01:36**: She continues, "In that three week period, 34 of the vaccinated children got covid and only 13 in the placebo group." The subtitle updates.
- **01:36-01:45**: She elaborates, "which worked out as a 30% increased chance of catching covid in that three week period if you were vaccinated." The subtitle updates.
- **01:45-01:47**: She then says, "So they ignored that data." The subtitle updates.
- **01:47-01:55**: She describes, "and then there was an eight week gap between the second dose and the third dose, where again, children were getting plenty of covid in the vaccine arm. So they ignored that data." The subtitle updates.
- **01:55-02:00**: She continues, "There was then several weeks after the third dose which they also ignored." The subtitle updates.
- **02:00-02:08**: She concludes, "which meant that in the end they had ignored 97% of the covid that occurred during the trial, and they just looked at tiny numbers - so tiny." The subtitle updates.
- **02:08-02:18**: She further explains, "In the end they were comparing 3 children in the vaccine arm who had covid with 7 in the placebo arm and they said that this showed the vaccine was effective." The subtitle updates.

In 22-23 the budget had risen to £181.7bn.    This is an uplift of £24.7bn per year or £475m per week, far above what was promised.    https://www.kingsfund.org.uk/insight-and-analysis/data-and-charts/nhs-budget-nutshell

caption.video0:
This video features a panel discussion on a show called "BBCQT," with a purple and red background featuring vertical light strips.

At the beginning of the video (0:00-0:05), the camera focuses on a man in a black zipped shirt over a white t-shirt. He has short, styled hair and is speaking animatedly, gesturing with his hands. He says, "in fact, but luckily for me, most people know I'm not here for me great political brain."

From 0:05-0:08, the man leans forward slightly, adjusting his microphone, and continues, "But that, that didn't stop Boris Johnson so..."

At 0:08, the camera cuts to a shot of the audience, who begin to laugh and applaud in response to the man's comment. They are a diverse group of people, seated in rows.

At 0:12, the shot changes to a closer view of a different section of the audience, still laughing and applauding.

The camera returns to the man at 0:15. He crosses his hands on the table and speaks more calmly, saying, "I think for me like James, you know, you saying to Wes that Keir Starmer is saying anything to get a vote is a bit rich coming from a Tory."

As he finishes his sentence (0:23), the audience laughs and applauds again. A white text overlay appears at the bottom of the screen, identifying him as "Paddy McGuinness, Comedian and TV Presenter."

At 0:26, the camera zooms out to show the full panel. Paddy McGuinness is on the far right. To his left is a man in a blue suit and tie, and then three women in various outfits. The host, a woman in a green top, is seated in the middle. The background behind the panel is a vibrant mix of purple and red, with glowing vertical lines. Paddy lifts his hand and points his pen while saying, "We're still waiting on the 350 million pounds a week Boris Johnson promised us."

At 0:31, the man in the blue suit interjects, "It's coming, it's here." Paddy looks at him with a surprised expression and responds, "Whoa! It's here?"

The man in the blue suit says, "Yeah, it is." Paddy replies, "The NHS you're saying?" The other man confirms, "The NHS, Paddy. On Fred."

Paddy then asks (0:39), "Well, where is it? Why is the NHS on its knees if it's here? Why are they going on strike, nurses and doctors, if it's here?"

The man in the blue suit responds (0:52), "The NHS, in respect to the areas where we are, are delivering excellent care to local people in the town, Barry, the next town, the next town to you. We're delivering more elective procedures, we've got more doctors and nurses. We're putting record amount of..."

During this exchange, the camera frequently cuts between close-ups of Paddy, the man in the blue suit, and a third male panelist in a gray suit and red tie, who listens intently with a slightly critical expression (1:05 and 1:17). A purple text overlay now appears at the bottom of the screen, stating, "With Labour dropping their flagship election promise, can we believe anything they pledge?"

The host interjects at 1:20, "Well, let's, let's get back to, to, uh, to the question from Kevin, 'Can we believe anything that Labour say or pledge?' That's what Kevin's asking. I mean, what, what do you think about this, this decision today?"

Paddy then begins to speak again (1:30), saying, "I, I see myself as a a floating voter. And I think this day and age, certainly the last five years, politics seems to have been led by personality."

He continues (1:45), "And if you look at Boris Johnson and how he did it to get into power, that's a prime example. And me worry with Keir Starmer, well not a worry as such, is, what he said today, and I don't know the ins and outs of it, but I almost kind of admire him for doing it now rather than after they get in power. Because after having the country decimated for the last 14 years by the Conservatives, I think they're an absolute shoo-in, Labour. I'll be flabbergasted if they don't get in. And I think him saying that for me personally, I think, well, I'd hate for him to say that after he got in power and then you feel you feel even more."

The video ends with Paddy McGuinness still speaking, his face visible in a close-up, and the purple text overlay remaining on screen.
